Linha 9: Audit day: |

Linha 13: Customer contact (e-mail and phone) | 
Linha 14: Supervisor contact (phone), if applicable | 
Linha 15: Client permission for recording | Total       Partially      Not authorized

=== PAGINA 1/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:1 de 40 
 
1. 
OBJECTIVE 
This document sets out the general requirements for companies in category K (GSO 
2055-2) and L1 (SMIIC 02) (personal hygiene products, cosmetics and perfumes) to certify 
Halal with FAMBRAS HALAL.  
 
 
2. 
APPLICATION FIELD 
This document applies to all FAMBRAS HALAL certified companies in category K 
(GSO 2055-2) and L1 (SMIIC 02). 
 
 
3. 
REFERENCE DOCUMENTS 
• 
UAE.S 2055-4:2021. Halal Products - Part 4: Requirements for Cosmetics and 
Personal care; 
• 
GSO 2055-4:2021. Halal Products - Part 4: Requirements for Halal Cosmetics and 
Personal care; 
• 
GSO 1943:2021 - Safety Requirements of Cosmetics and Personal Care Products; 
• 
GSO 1046:2021 - Cosmetic products – perfumery products based on ethanol; 
• 
GSO 1047:2021 - Cosmetic products - perfumery products based on ethanol - 
methods of test 
• 
GSO 2020:2010 - Cosmetics - Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) – Guidelines 
on Good Manufacturing Practices; 
• 
GSO 2528:2016 - Cosmetic products – Technical Regulation of cosmetic and 
personal care products claims; 
• 
GSO 396:2022 - Cosmetic products -Hair shampoo; 
• 
GSO 1217:2022 - Cosmetic products - hair oils; 
• 
GSO 1846:2023 – Cosmetics products: Al Kohl (Athmad); 
• 
KEPKABAN BPJPH - Number 20: 2023 - Decree of the Head of Halal Product 
Assurance Organizing Agency – Indonesia; 
• 
PEPRES BPJPH - Number 6: 2023 – Halal Certification of Drugs, Biological 
Products and Health Equipment – Indonesia; 
• 
KMA BPJPH Number 748: 2021 - Products that must be certified Halal – Indonesia;

•

de benefícios para conservação e uso sustentável da biodiversidade. 
 
 
4. 
GENERAL PROVISIONS 
All products that aim to obtains the Halal certification by FAMBRAS HALAL for 
category K (GSO 2055-2) and L1 (SMIIC 02) shall meet the requirements established in this 
document.  
In this document, the word “shall” indicates a requirement and the word “should”, a 
recommendation.  
 
 
5. DEFINITIONS 
 
5.1. 
Shariah  
The word Shariah (Islamic jurisprudence) refers to the totality of the orders of Allah 
(God) that regulate all aspects of the life of a Muslim (MS 1900:2005), meaning, everything 
that is described in the holy Quran, that aside the God's revelations to Prophet Muhammad 
(SAWS). In addition to the Qur'an, the Sunnah, i.e. all practices and sayings of the Prophet 
Muhammad (SAW) are also part of the Shariah. 
 
5.2. 
 Halal  
The word 'Halal' means lawful, the same as allowed. Halal designated products 
are those which consumption is allowed by God, according to Shariah.  
Halal is then the basis of all that is lawful, in food, in social life, in acts practiced 
(conduct), in justice, in clothes, in finances, etc., it is a system based on the principles 
and values that benefit the humanity.  
The Holy Quran teaches us in 5th Surah, 4th verse: "Lawful for you are all good 
foods".  


=== PAGINA 5/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:5 de 40 
 
 
5.3. 
Halal slaughter (Tazkeya)  
Halal slaughter is an Islamic ritual that should be done quickly, so it does not allow 
the animal suffering, in which the trachea, esophagus and both arteries and jugular vein 
should be cut to hasten the bleeding and the animal´s death.  
The slaughter ritual shall be practiced ethically and with respect for the animals to 
be slaughtered, following the Islamic laws of the Holy Quran. One of them consists of the 
Tasmiyah, which consist in invoking the phrase Bismillah, Allahu Akbar (In the name of 
God, God is the greatest), immediately before the slaughter. Bismillah is one of the ways 
to seek God's help before any action to be performed by a Muslim. There is no text in 
Islamic jurisprudence that confirms exactly the meaning of the Tasmiyah, but Islamic 
scholars claim that it would be a way of thanking God for the food offered by Him, since 
the sacrifice of animals is performed to ensure the sustenance of human beings and not 
for fun or sadism. 
 
5.4. 
Haram  
The word Haram means illicit, impure, illegal. Refers to what is unacceptable, 
detrimental to the health of the human body and its morals, as determined in the Qur'an 
and Hadith (confirmation of the acts, affirmations, opinions and the way of Prophet 
Muhammad lived (Peace and blessings of Allaah be upon him).  
 
5.5. 
Mashbouh  
There is a large number of industrialized products that use additives and 
ingredients that can bring dangers to spiritual and physical health, as they belong to the 
Mashbouh (questionable/ doubtful) category, which are:  
Elements that can be from numerous sources of extraction, such as:  
a) Glycerin which may be of vegetable or animal origin;  
b) L-Cysteine a non-essential amino acid can be obtained from human hair, pork, 
duck feather, oil and from vegetable.  
Processed products of Halal origin, for example:  
a) Clarified fruit juice with pork;  
b) Products such as yogurt, biscuit, cereal bar, that uses gelatin and mono and 
diglycerides; 


=== PAGINA 6/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:6 de 40 
 
c) Flavours with alcohol; 
d) Cheeses, monosodium glutamate (flavor enhancer) and other products 
fermented with enzymes; 
e) Among others.  
 
5.6. 
Makruh  
Inappropriate. Not recommended (encouraged) according to Shariah. 
  
5.7. 
Najasah (Impurities)  
Najasah means everything that is considered impure, such as feces, urine, 
prostatic fluid, a discharge from the urethra, sperm, pus, vomit, and excrement of any 
animal whose meat is not allowed for consumption.  
 
5.8. 
Halal Certificate  
It is a faithful guarantee document issued by a Halal certifying institution, 
recognized by Islamic countries, to attest that the company, process and products, follow 
the legal requirements and criteria determined by Shariah. 
 
5.9. 
Genetically Modified Organisms (GMO) 
Organisms obtained by gene transfer from other living species to a plant, animal or 
micro-organism by genetic engineering technologies, thus altering the natural 
characteristics of that organism. 
  
5.10. Cosmetics and personal care products 
Any substance, natural or synthetic, or mixture intended for external use in the various 
parts of the human body, skin, capillary system, nails, lips, external genital organs, teeth 
and mucous membranes of the oral cavity, with the sole or principal purpose of cleaning, 
perfuming, altering their appearance and / or correcting body odors and / or protecting or 
keeping them in good condition. 
 
5.11. Ingredient 
Ingredient is any substance or preparation of synthetic or natural origin used in the 
composition of a cosmetic product, including coloring agent, botanicals, and fragrances. The 


=== PAGINA 7/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:7 de 40 
 
definition includes the materials used in quantities strictly necessary as solvents or as a 
conductor for perfuming and aromatic compositions, but does not include the subsidiary 
technical materials used in the preparation, that are not present in the final product, as a 
result of a certain chemical process. 
 
5.12. Al Kohl/ Athmad Kohl 
Black substance found in the form of solid rocky nodules or in the form of dust, 
prepared by grinding these nodules, or prepared in the form of pencils to darken the eyes, 
eyelids, eyelashes or eyebrows. 
 
5.13. Contaminants 
Unintended substances, biological and chemical agents, foreign matter, or any other 
substance not intentionally added to the product, that may originate from external sources 
to the product and to the chemical process, due the incorrect substance’s storage, or the 
instability of the primary packaging, for example, and that may compromise product safety 
or its compliance. 
 
5.14. Genetic Modified Organisms (GMOs) 
They are organisms obtained from the transfer of genes from another donor 
organism, using genetic engineering techniques and DNA alteration. GMOs are created with 
the objective of favoring a certain characteristic, such as odor and color, for example. 
 
5.15. Packaging 
All material or container developed to store and wrap the product, in order to protect 
against damage and deterioration, under specific conditions of handling, storage and 
transport. 
 
5.16. Halal Mizen (weight/ measurement) 
Islam cares about the protection of consumer rights by seeking to obtain the right 
measure and/or weight in the product. This way, any practice or process applied 
ineffectively, configures fraudulent act. The holy Quran mentions: "Woe unto fraudsters, 
those who, when one measures them something, require the exact/full measure. But when 
they measure or weigh on others, they defraud them." (Sura 83:1-3).  


=== PAGINA 8/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:8 de 40 
 
With this in mind, when we also talk about commercialization, we should transpose 
the same concept of justice that is so strongly defended in Islam, as it is in the holy Quran: 
"Oh my people, have measure and weight with equity; do not defraud humans in their goods, 
and do not practice debauchery on earth as corruptors." (Sura 11:85). 
 
5.17. Appeal of cosmetics and personal care products 
The appeal of cosmetics and personal care products are information about the nature 
and/or impact and/or effectiveness and/or content mentioned in the product or advertising 
or in any promotional way. 
 
5.18. Preservatives 
Substances added to cosmetics and personal care products to inhibit microorganisms 
growth during their production and storage, and/or to protect the products from 
contamination during their use. 
 
5.19. Identification of the categories GSO 
The certified products are classified according to the categories presented in table 1 
– Gulf standard, according to GSO 2055-2. 
The Gulf countries referenced in this document are those that are part of the GCC 
(GCC Accreditation Center) and follow the standards established by the GSO. They are: 
Saudi Arabia, Oman, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Yemen and the United Arab Emirates.  
FAMBRAS Halal may operate in all categories indicated in the table, however, for the 
Gulf countries the recognition is applicable only for the categories marked in blue. For other 
categories, upon demand and necessity, FAMBRAS HALAL may request recognition.

=== PAGINA 11/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO

OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:11 de 40 
 
 
5.20. Identification of the categories SMIIC 
Certified products are classified according to the categories presented in table 2 - 
SMIIC 02:2019 standard. 
SMIIC stands for Standards and Metrology Institute for Islamic Countries, being an 
intergovernmental organization, that aims to establish common standards to be 
implemented throughout the region of OIC - Organization of the Islamic Cooperation, which 
aims to ensure consumer protection and interoperability of products, and also to strengthen 
the market position of OIC member states in the global market. 
The countries that are part of OIC are: Afghanistan, Albania, Azerbaijan, Bangladesh, 
United Arab Emirates, Brunei, Darussalam, Algeria, Indonesia, Morocco, Palestine, Gabon, 
Iraq, Iran, Cameroon, Qatar, Kyrgyzstan, Kuwait, Libya, Lebanon, Maldives, Malaysia, 
Egypt, Nigeria, Oman, Uzbekistan, Pakistan, Senegal, Somalia, Sudan, Syria, Saudi Arabia, 
Tajikistan, Tunisia, Turkey, Turkmenistan, Uganda, Jordan, Yemen, Bosnia and 
Herzegovina.

=== PAGINA 14/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION

DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:14 de 40 
 
 
6. GENERAL REQUERIMENTS FOR THE MANUFACTURING OF COSMETICS AND 
HALAL PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
 
6.1. 
The manufacturer shall demonstrate compliance with all legal issues applicable to 
the country and region; 
 
6.2. 
Cosmetics and personal care products shall not cause intoxication and shall be 
safe for health when used under normal or reasonably foreseeable conditions of 
use. 
6.2.1. 
Product safety evidence shall be available, e.g. test reports or other relevant 
documents. 
 
6.3. 
Cosmetics and personal care products shall be homogeneous, stable and their 
properties that impact on safety, efficacy and quality shall not change during their 
useful life, when stored and used in accordance with the instructions. 
 
6.4. 
Cosmetics and personal care products and its raw materials, process aids, inputs 
and packaging shall be free of any non-Halal elements, described in table 3, and 
from any impurity (Najasah), according to item 5.7; 
In the table of attachment 1 are listed some Halal critical points that shall be 
considered, for each product type.  
6.4.1. There shall be a supplier approval process for the selection and approval of raw 
materials, inputs, process aids and packaging materials, in order to ensure the 
quality and integrity of the product, as well as its safety for Halal. 
 
6.5. 
Cosmetics and personal care products and their raw materials, processing aids, 
supplies and packaging do not contain animal derivatives that have not been 
slaughtered in Halal (according to Shariah). 
 
6.6. 
Cosmetics and personal care products and its raw materials, inputs, process aids, 
and packaging materials shall be guaranteed against its Halal status with official 
documents that demonstrate its legal origin, except when they are of 100% natural 


=== PAGINA 15/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:15 de 40 
 
 
origin, such as vegetables, ores and others - as evaluation of FAMBRAS HALAL; 
 
6.7. 
Cosmetics and personal care products and its raw materials, inputs, process aids 
and packaging materials shall not contain contaminants, toxic substances and 
dangerous pollutants that can be considered harmful to health.  
6.7.1. 
There shall be evidence proving that the products are stable and safe, for 
example, atoxicity reports, product stability/compatibility reports or other 
relevant documents. 
 
6.8. 
All products certified by FAMBRAS HALAL and its raw materials, inputs, 
processing aids and packaging materials shall meet the physical-chemical and 
microbiological standards established for drugs and medications and shall contain 
only allowed substances and within the maximum limits established by current 
legislation. 
 
6.9. 
All raw materials, inputs, processing aids and packaging materials, used in certified 
products, shall be previously informed and approved by FAMBRAS HALAL.

Table 3. Non-Halal Table of Products 
Species 
Non-Halal 
Animals 
The animals that may not be slaughtered according to Islamic Rules, the slaughtered 
animals of polytheists, irreligious, seculars, atheists, Magis, apostates and other 
disbelievers other than Christians and Jews, dead animals, suffocated animals, fatally 
beaten animals (Mawqouza), falling animals (Motaradiah), butted animals (Nateehah), 
animals eaten by beast of prey and animals on whose slaughtering the name of Allah 
is not mentioned, the animals slaughtered on idols, the animals slaughtered not in 
approach of Allah, or the animals contaminated with prohibited animals of harm, if 
eaten;
Pigs, domestic donkeys, mules, elephants, monkeys, dogs, foxes, cats and the like. 
Predators such as fanged beasts of prey and the like such as lions, cheetahs, bears, 
Birds of prey with sharp claws used to attack and similars, such as eagles, hawks, 
sparrows, pilgrims, parrots and owls. 
Rodents, reptiles, pests and the like such as mice, rats, centipedes, serpents, snakes, 
monitors, lizards, geckos, chameleons, hedgehogs, martins, bats, squirrels, polecats, 
moles and Coleoptera (except for dabb lizards and jerboas) 
Scorpions and all types of insects, worms and animals prohibited to be killed by Rules 
and the like such as ants, bees, woodpeckers and hoopoes, except for locusts and 
unavoidable bee parts falling in honey 
Odious creatures and the like such as shells, larva and other similar animals. 
Animals fed with unclean items, unless they are confined and fed with permissible 
feeding according to Islamic Rules for at least three days. 
Aquatic 
Toxicant or harmful species, unless toxicant or harmful substances are removed 
during preparation. 
Plantation 
Plants, their products and their toxicant, harmful or narcotic derivatives, unless 
toxicant or harmful substances are removed during preparation. 
Beverages 
Intoxicating beverages or those containing alcohols, narcotic, calming, toxicant or 
harmful substances 
GMO 
All elements produced by genetic modification from a prohibited specie or more than 
one species, of them one specie is prohibited (non-halal) 
Blood and Blood 
Derivatives from 
a Human or 
Animal Origin: 
Blood and all its derivatives are prohibited and all body liquids from a human or animal 
origin such as vomiting and urine. 
Food Additives 
All toxicant, harmful or calming additives, products and their products and derivatives 
or derived from non-Halal materials 
Enzymes 
All enzymes derived from non-Halal sources. 
Microorganisms 
All microorganisms such as germs, fungi, toxicant and harmful yeast produced on non-
Halal environments or using non-Halal ingredients 
Source: GSO 2055-2 adapted.  
 
 
 


=== PAGINA 17/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:17 de 40 
 
 
6.10. Ethyl alcohol / Ethanol: 
6.10.1. Cosmetics and personal care products and its raw materials, inputs, process 
aids or any other element that comes into direct contact with the Halal product 
are not / can not contain alcoholic beverages (khamr), or derivatives 
physically separated from the alcoholic beverages. 
6.10.2. Products derived from the alcoholic beverage, such as yeast extract, can be 
used since they undergo a purification process according to Shariah, which 
consists of the complete removal of color, odor and flavor of the alcoholic 
beverage. 
6.10.3. Ethyl alcohol (ethanol) may occur naturally in products due to spontaneous 
fermentation, however, it is necessary that these do not exceed the maximum 
limit of ethanol established by FAMBRAS HALAL, which is 1%. 
6.10.4. In some cases, ethyl alcohol (ethanol) is indispensable in manufacturing, and 
it is used as an auxiliary/process additive. In this case, ethanol is allowed, 
since the source of production is not from alcoholic beverages (eg wine) it 
does not exceed the maximum limit of ethanol established by FAMBRAS 
HALAL, which is 1%. 
 
6.11. Ingredients:  
6.11.1. All water types are allowed for application as ingredients with the exception 
of reused water - from sewage and contaminated water with any non-halal 
element, indicated in table 2.  
6.11.2. All land types are allowed with the exception of those set-aside land from the 
graveyard, animal farms and dump areas. 
6.11.3. All agricultural ingredients from Halal origin, physically or chemically treated, 
are allowed as long as they are not exposed to Najasah at any stage of the 
entire production process. 
6.11.4. Animals and plants of endangered species (meeting the requirements of the 
Convention on International Trade in Species of Flora and Fauna - CITES) 
shall not be used. 
6.11.5. When applicable, the economic exploitation of finished product or 
reproductive material developed from access to genetic heritage or 


=== PAGINA 18/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:18 de 40 
 
 
associated traditional knowledge shall comply with Law nº 13,123 of 2015. 
6.11.6. Genetically modified organisms are permitted when derived from Halal 
sources and are not originated from a human gene, an illicit animal or which 
has not been slaughtered in accordance with Islamic case-law. 
6.11.7. In the presence of microorganisms in the process, the culture medium shall 
be Halal. It is prohibited to use microorganisms, such as bacteria, fungi, and 
yeasts, which are toxic and harmful to human health. 
 
6.12. The name of the final product, the brand, the logo, the sensory characteristics and 
the model of the packaging do not infringe the principles of Shariah, for example 
the brand does not allude to Haram elements, as bacon, beer, or any other that 
might confuse the consumer.  
 
6.13. The Islamic Rules shall be applied in all phases of the chain of products certified 
by FAMBRAS HALAL, including receipt, preparation, packaging, labeling, storage, 
transport, marketing, and distribution; 
6.14. The official authorities may take all necessary procedures to verify the conformity 
of products with the special requirements of Halal products and may take 
appropriate measures in accordance with local legislation. 
 
6.15. All types of cleaning or maintenance products such as detergents, sanitizers, 
greases, and lubricants shall be previously informed and approved by FAMBRAS 
HALAL and shall be free of non-Halal components or materials (prohibited by 
Shariah). 
 
6.16. Cosmetics and personal care products shall be packed in suitable, clean, dry and 
safely closed containers that do not affect or react to the product, protecting it from 
damages and deterioration. 
 
6.17.  The weight of the Halal product declared on the product packaging may not be 
less than its actual weight (see 5.16). 
6.17.1. 
The company shall carry out sample tests to measure the Halal products 


=== PAGINA 19/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:19 de 40 
 
 
weight, comparing the weight described on the package with the actual weight;   
6.17.2. In case of non-conforming products, where the real weight is inferior to the 
weight of the package, there shall be a segregation, where the product will be 
condemned for commercialization as a Halal product. The company shall the 
correction and corrective action in cases of non-conformities;  
6.17.3. There shall be daily records of these conferences. 
 
 
 
7. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR HALAL PRODUCTION AREAS 
 
7.1. 
Cosmetics and personal care products shall be completely separated from non-
Halal products at any and all stages of the manufacturing flow (receipt, preparation, 
packaging, labeling, storage, and transportation) to avoid cross-contamination.  
 
7.2. 
For units that manufacture Halal and non-Halal products: 
7.2.1. 
The equipment shall be sanitized before the start of Halal production, and the 
hygiene procedure is duly validated; 
7.2.2. 
It is recommended that the day of production begins with the manufacture of 
Halal products; 
7.2.3. 
It is necessary to identify the lines that process the Halal, in order to prevent 
cross contamination; 
7.2.4. 
It is not allowed to carry out the sanitization alternately between equipment 
that processes Halal and equipment that processes non-Halal products; 
7.2.5. 
The sampling practices shall ensure that cross-contamination of the Halal 
certified product or its raw materials, inputs and process aids does not occur 
with any non-Halal product. For example, use a sample collection utensil in a 
Halal product that was not properly sanitized, and was also previously used 
to collect some non-Halal product. 
7.2.6. 
Validation tests may be carried out on the first Halal production following non-
Halal production, whether on the product and / or equipment, as determined 
by the certification body; 
7.2.7. 
Halal and non-Halal raw materials shall be segregated and identified; 


=== PAGINA 20/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:20 de 40 
 
 
7.3. 
If the production lines have already been used to make products of pork origin, for 
example, an enzymatic detergent with lipase, before converting it for the 
manufacture of Halal products it is necessary that the same goes through a ritual 
of purification, which consists of subjecting the equipment to the passage of water 
+ cleaning agents such as detergent and sanitizer, at least 7 times, for the 
complete removal of color, taste and aroma from the contaminant  
7.3.1. 
If the certified company's standard procedure does not add up to 7 product 
passes (adding water, cleaning and rinsing), it shall be repeated as many 
times as necessary until totalization, or additional rinsings may be performed 
only with water, so that in open equipment, the uninterrupted flow of water 
occurs for at least 30 seconds, followed by total water evacuation. This 
procedure will be considered as 1 (one) rinse. 
7.3.2. 
The conversion of the lines between Halal production and swine shall not be 
carried out continuously.  
 
7.4. 
There shall be a FAMBRAS HALAL supervisor, accompanying all the 
manufactures of cosmetics and Halal personal care products containing raw 
material of animal origin (from legal animals slaughtered according to Halal), which 
are acquired with an exclusive Halal certificate per batch, such as gelatin, collagen. 
Other types of products may have the same obligation when the company 
manufactures Halal and non-Halal products, and they are identified as high risk for 
Halal, and this need will be defined by FAMBRAS HALAL. 
7.4.1. The supervisor shall be Muslim, mentally healthy, with at least 18 years old, and 
competent to perform his role efficiently, demonstrating knowledge and 
understanding of the Islamic fundamentals and requirements referring to Halal. 
 
7.5. 
All equipment, production lines, tools, utensils and associated materials used for 
the preparation of products certified by FAMBRAS HALAL shall be clean, free of 
contamination with non-Halal materials and free of impurities and shall not be 
manufactured or contain any type of non-Halal material, such as bone, nail or teeth 
(stainless steel is preferred). 
 


=== PAGINA 21/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:21 de 40 
 
 
8. 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR STORAGE AND TRANSPORT OF HALAL 
PRODUCTS 
 
8.1. 
If the company has final products with different Halal status, the Halal products 
shall be properly segregated and identified according to it.  
8.1.1. For powder products stored in flexible packaging (such as flexible plastic 
packaging, paper sachets), there shall have a significant physical segregation 
(average of 50 cm) between products with different status, to ensure no cross 
contamination occur. When stored on shelves, the Halal products can not be 
placed below non-Halal products.  
8.1.2. For solid and liquid products stored in rigid packages (drums, cylinders, cans, 
for example) a physical segregation with a significant distance is not 
mandatory, however Halal and non-Halal products shall not be grounded (even 
when already packed in primary, secondary and palletized packaging). It is 
highly recommended that Halal products when stored on shelves do not stay 
below non-Halal products.  
8.1.3. The Halal certified company shall implement a hazard evaluation and take the 
appropriate procedure to determine what measures shall be taken in case of 
non-Halal packaging breakdown/ leakage in the final product warehouse, with 
the aim of ensuring no cross-contamination with the Halal product.  
8.1.4. About the identification of the products, it can be applied only to Halal products, 
and the seal is visible on the secondary packaging as a sufficient form of 
identification. 
 
8.2. 
Preferably transport all FAMBRAS HALAL products certified from non-Halal 
products. 
8.2.1. For Islamic / Islamic-majority countries, mixed transport of Halal and non-Halal 
products is prohibited. 
8.2.2. In non-Islamic countries, mixed transport of Halal and non-Halal products, may 
be permitted provided that there is no Halal import rule in the country that 
determines otherwise. In these cases, the Halal product shall contain individual 
identification, being sufficient to ensure that there is no exchange between Halal 


=== PAGINA 22/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:22 de 40 
 
 
and non-Halal product.  
 
8.3. 
It is expressly prohibited to carry Halal products and products of pork origin and/ 
or their derivatives. 
 
8.4. 
The means of transport shall be adequate to avoid cross-contamination with non-
Halal products, besides satisfying the conditions of hygiene.  
 
 
9. 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR LABELLING 
 
9.1. 
The labeling of the Halal certified product shall comply with the relevant laws of the 
country of origin and, in the case of exportation, with the country of destination. 
9.2. 
The Halal seal shall be applied according to the instruction: IT 4.1 FAMBRAS 
HALAL SEAL USE INSTRUCTION. 
9.2.1. 
Every media that apply the Halal seal, such as packaging, advertising 
materials, reports and e-mail signatures, shall be previously approved by 
FAMBRAS HALAL. 
Note 1: To obtain this approval, it is mandatory that the client sends an e-mail 
to the auditor responsible for your process, with the materials arts that will 
apply the Halal seal, for evaluation and subsequent authorization of its use.  
9.2.2. 
Packaging and media can not use the FAMBRAS HALAL seal before 
obtaining the certification body approval.   
 
9.3. 
Advertising of the Halal product can not contradict the principles of Shariah, such 
as, showing elements considered indecent.  
 
9.4. 
Advertising materials and packaging of Halal products shall not contain misleading 
information to the consumer, nor Haram elements. 
 
9.5. 
In the case of traded products as kits, for example, shampoo, conditioner and 
capillary mask, it shall be affixed the Halal identification on the kit outer package, it is 


=== PAGINA 23/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:23 de 40 
 
 
necessary that all the products that compose it are Halal.  
 
9.6. 
The following information shall be available on the label (in an indelible, easily 
legible and visible form): 
a. Product name and trademark; 
b. Name and address of the manufacturer or distributor; 
c. Country of origin of the product; 
d. Nominal content, expressed in weight or volume units; 
e. Date of manufacture and validity; 
f. 
Conditions of use, warnings, and precautionary information. When it is not 
possible to include these information on the primary and secondary package, due 
to its size, an accompanying leaflet can be included. There shall be a phrase on 
the primary or secondary package that leads the consumer to see the annexed 
information; 
g. Lot number or code; 
h. Product function. The product function should be clearly described on primary 
and secondary package, unless it can be spontaneously deducted from the 
products’ presentation (shape, size, and volume), its name or trademark, its 
appeals, images, and logo; 
i. 
List of ingredientes. When it is not possible to include the list of ingredients on 
the primary and secondary package, due to its size, an accompanying leaflet can 
be included. There shall be a phrase on the primary or secondary package that 
leads the consumer to see the annexed information; 
9.6.1. 
For Gulf countries (see item 5.17), besides the information disposed on item 
9.5, the following requirements shall apply: 
a. Product’s country origin – if two or more countries are involved in cosmetic 
product production, the country origin identified on label shall be the one where 
the economic operation was carried out. When these countries have the same 
production cost, the country responsible for the last step of production process 
shall be identified on the label as the origin country; 
b. The label may not contain the nominal content in the following cases: products 
with less than 5ml or 5g, single application products, and free samples; 


=== PAGINA 24/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6

c. The expiry date may be expressed in the primary and secondary package in 
two ways: 
I. 
Products with durabibility within 30 months, from the manufacturing 
date, may express the expiry date indicating the month and year; or the 
day, month and year. If necessary, this date shall be accompanied by 
the conditions that shall be met in order to guarantee the product’s 
durability. 
The date shall be preceded by one of the following options: “best used 
before the end of”, “Exp.”, “E”, or by the symbol presented in figure 1. 
 
Figure 1. Symbol that represents the expiry date for products with durability within 30 
months 
Source: GSO 1943 
II. 
Products with durability over 30 months, from the manufacturing date, 
shall express the expiry date after the product opening. The expiry date 
may be expressed in months or years and shall be accompanied by the 
symbol shown in figure 2. 
 
Figure 2. Symbol that represents the durability of the product after opened. 
Source: GSO 1943 
This declaration is not necessary for: products in containers where 
there is no need of physical opening and there is no possibility of 
contact between the product and the external environment (pressurized 
and sealed containers); single application products; products with low 
risk of microbiological contamination (pH≥10.0, pH≤3.5) and products 
that state the expiry date. 
d. Lot number or code shall be on the label of primary and secondary package.

When it is impossible to put it on the primary package, it will be allowed the 
identification only in the secondary package. 
e. All information that shall be on the label, stated in item 9.5, shall be in Arabic 
and/ or English, except for the following information that shall be on both 
languages, either on primary and secondary package: 
I. 
Product’s name; 
II. 
Conditions of use, warnings, and precautionary information; 
III. 
Product’s function. 
 
9.7. 
Cosmetic and personal care products that have specific label requirements are: 
9.7.1. 
Products commercialized as kits: the list of ingredients shall be indicated by 
one of the following options: 
a. 
On the secondary package separately for each product of the kit or combined 
into one list; 
b. 
When it is not possible to include the list of ingredients on the secondary 
package, due to its size, an accompanying leaflet can be included, and there 
shall be a phrase on the secondary package that leads the consumer to see 
the annexed information; 
c. 
In case of a transparent package where the list of ingredients of the individual 
products are clearly visible, a secondary labelling is not necessary. 
9.7.2. 
Products commercialized as ampules and vials: these products should have 
the following instructions, in both Arabic and English languages: 
a. 
For external use only; 
b. 
Avoid contact with eyes; 
c. 
Open with caution. 
9.7.3. 
Perfumery products: its ingredientes shall be referred by the terms “parfum” 
or “aroma”. When it has ingredients listed in Annex 3 of GSO 1943/2021, they 
shall be described along with the terms “parfum” or “aroma”. 
These products shall have the following instructions, in both Arabic and English 
languages: 
a. 
For external use only; 
b. 
Avoid contact with eyes. 


=== PAGINA 26/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:26 de 40 
 
 
9.8. 
The appeals indicated on the labels of cosmetics and personal care products shall 
meet the following criteria: 
9.8.1. 
Legal compliance: Comply with the legislation of the manufacturer country 
and also not infringe the laws of the importing country, including absence of 
conflicts with Islamic habits and values of society; 
9.8.2. 
 Truthfulness: The general presentation and appeal of cosmetics and 
personal care products shall be based on true and relevant information. If the 
product claims it is preservative-free, it really should not have preservatives.   
9.8.3. 
Evidence supporting information: Explicit or implicit appeals shall be 
supported by adequate and verifiable evidence. It is possible to link reference 
and scientific studies as evidence. The level of evidence or reasoning shall 
be consistent with the type of appeal being made, in particular for appeals 
where lack of effectiveness can cause a security problem. (For example, 
products with sunscreen that call for a certain sun protection factor (SPF) 
should have been tested in accordance with relevant international standards.) 
9.8.4. 
Honesty: The appeal regarding the performance of the product should not be 
exaggerated, for example, changing an image from before and after, of the 
use of the product. The appeal shall not assign the product as the only one 
to perform such an action, when there are similar products that have the same 
characteristics. Or when the action of the product is linked to specific 
conditions, such as use in association with other products, this shall be clearly 
indicated. 
9.8.5. 
Justice: Appeals should be objective and should not denigrate competitors 
(such as: Maximum, the best, the strongest), nor defame ingredients that can 
be legally used. 
9.8.6. 
Informed decision making: Appeals should be clear and understandable to 
the average final user. Marketing communications should be clear, accurate, 
relevant and understandable considering the capacity of the target audience. 
 
 
9.9. 
The appeals indicated on the labels of cosmetics and personal care products 
should not make claims that are not comply with the definition of cosmetic product, 
for example appealing medicinal conditions, or significant change in the

FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO

physiological functions of the body or influences on metabolism. 
9.9.1. 
According to RDC nº 250/2018, the labeling of the products sold shall not 
contain indications and therapeutic mentions, denominations and indications 
that lead to error, mistake or confusion regarding their origin, origin, 
composition, purpose or safety. 
9.9.2. 
If the product is exported to the Gulf countries, GSO 2528/2016, it shall be 
followed, so as not to be made appeals indicated in the table of item 5, as 
unacceptable. 
 
 
10. 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR QUALITY AND SAFETY OF HALAL 
PRODUCTS 
 
10.1. The company shall have a Halal Assurance System (HAS), as provided by DT 7.3 
In the table of attachment 1 are listed some Halal critical points that shall be 
considered, for each product type.  
 
10.2. Halal personal hygiene products, cosmetics and perfumes shall have the quality 
characteristics and / or provide the desired results, according to what is stated on 
the label and advertising materials.  
 
10.3. The layout of the facilities shall allow: Product protection; Hygiene and sanitization, 
if necessary, of equipment and maintenance; Minimize the risk of mixing products, 
raw materials and packaging materials; 
 
10.4. The conditions of the installations, equipment, auxiliary areas and quality control 
laboratories shall be in accordance with RDC No. 48, OF 25 OCTOBER 2013, 
which in turn complies with GSO 2020/2010 and ISO 22716:2007. 
 
10.5. The company shall have a Quality Management System, which shall contain: 
a. Organizational structure; 
b. Key responsibilities; 


=== PAGINA 28/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:28 de 40 
 
 
c. Staff management, training and visitor control; 
d. Control of documents and records; 
e. Outsourcing/ subcontracting control; 
f. Change control;  
 
10.6. Hygiene and safety of cosmetics and personal care products are prerequisites for 
the manufacture of halal product. This way, the company shall have a system of 
Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP), which shall contain at least the following 
controls and records: 
a. Cleaning of facilities, equipment, furniture and utensils;  
b. Water control;  
c. Hygiene, health, clothing, conduct and training of employees;  
d. Waste management;  
e. Preventive maintenance and calibration of equipment;  
f. Integrated vector and pest control;  
g. Selection and control of raw materials and packaging materials;  
h. Recall program; 
i. Dealing with deviations, non-conforming products and customer complaints; 
j. Return;  
k. Sampling and Retention Sample; 
l. Self-Inspection / Internal Audit. 
***The compliance of these requirements will be evaluated, before RDC No. 48, 
OF OCTOBER 25, 2013, which in turn is in accordance with GSO 2020/2010 and 
ISO 22716:2007. 
 
10.7. Good Manufacturing Practices shall be applied in all sectors of industry: 
• Storage of raw materials; 
• Storage of final products; 
• Storage of chemicals in general; 
• Production/Elaboration; 
• Process control areas;  
• Auxiliary areas; 


=== PAGINA 29/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:29 de 40 
 
 
• Laboratories, including material sampling, Quality Control and Retention 
samples. 
10.8. The recall program shall include communication with the Halal certification body, 
as provided for in procedure PR 7.1 - Conditions for granting, maintenance, 
extension, reduction, suspension, cancellation, termination and renewal of halal 
certification. 
10.8.1. 
The recall program shall foresee the need to carry it out in the event of 
contamination that undermines the product Halal status.  
 
10.9. The company has a risk analysis with actionss to avoid risks to consumer health, 
such as allergy, including in peculiar segments, such as children. 
 
10.10. Halal cosmetic and personal care products shall be traceable. Traceability 
procedures adopted by units shall be based on approved specifications or any 
appropriate authorized actions, and shall be applied at all stages of the 
manufacturing flow (receipt, preparation, packaging, labeling, storage, and 
transport). 
 
10.11. The methodology for handling deviations, complaints, and non-conformities 
(relating to internal and external audits) shall include the corrective actions taken 
to avoid future occurrences. 
 
10.12. Nonconformities from previous process/ audits shall be resolved, and evidence 
presented whenever requested. 
 
10.13. The pH value of the products shall be in the range (4-8), with the exception of hair 
colorants. 
10.13.1. If the product has pH value outside the mentioned range, supporting 
documents shall be submitted to validate its need to ensure a safe and stable 
product. 
10.13.2. For baby shampoo, the pH shall be between 6-7 at room temperature. 
 


=== PAGINA 30/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:30 de 40 
 
 
10.14.  The products may not present, in their composition, any prohibited element, and 
shall also meet the maximum concentration stipulated, according to lists found in 
RDC 530/2021, RDC 528/2021, RDC 529/2021, RDC 628/2022 and RDC 
752/2022 
10.14.1. For Gulf countries, cosmetics and personal care products shall meet the 
requirements listed in the annexes of GSO 1943/2021. 
 
10.15. The presence of small amounts of prohibited substances, arising from impurities 
of natural or synthetic ingredients, from the production process, storage, packaging 
migration, which is technically unavoidable in Good Manufacturing Practices, is 
allowed provided that it is not harmful to human health, and is within the limits 
established for the presence as an impurity. 
 
10.16.  If dye is used in the manufacturing process, the applications shall be in 
accordance with RDC 628/2022. 
 
10.17. If preservative is used in the production process, the application shall meet RDC 
528/2021. 
 
10.18. If 
the 
products 
contain 
lead 
acetate, 
pyrogalol, 
formaldehyde 
and 
paraformaldehyde, the limits on the final product shall be in accordance with RDC 
645/2022. 
 
10.19. In cosmetic products for sun protection, the concentration of ultraviolet filters shall 
meet RDC 600/2022. 
 
10.20. The limits of allowed actives in cosmetic products to straighten or curl the hair shall 
meet IN 124/2022. 
 
10.21. The cosmetic products and the personal care products destined to children’s 
audience shall meet the established requirements of RDC 639/2022. 
 


=== PAGINA 31/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:31 de 40 
 
 
10.22.  For shampoos the following additional requirements apply: 
• Should be viscous and homogeneous;  
• It should be easy to spread on hair;  
• Anionic cyclic compounds are not allowed;  
• Should be easily removed from hair, rinsing with water;  
• The compound 1,4 – dioxane, when present as an impurity, shall not exceed 
10ppm; 
• The content of active materials should not be less than 8% by mass, including 
shampoo for babies;  
• The number of microorganisms shall not exceed 100 CFU/gram and shall not 
contain any pathogenic microorganisms; 
• The percentage of soluble matter in non-volatile alcohol (inorganic salt) should 
not exceed 7% by mass;  
• Volatile matter at 221°F should not exceed 95% by mass;  
• Should not change its characteristics substantially when stored under normal 
conditions and humidity appropriate to room temperature, with the ability to 
maintain its stability for a period of not less than 18 months and not more than 
5 years; 
• It shall maintain its properties without any change when stored in its original 
airtight package at a temperature of 25ºC, for the validity period indicated on 
label. 
 
10.23.  For hair oils the following additional requirements apply: 
• Should be colourless or coloured; 
• It should have a pleasant scent or smell; 
• Should be free of any sediment and suspended material at 80,6ºF; 
• Should be free of unpleasant rancid odor; 
• Should contain appropriate antioxidants, if necessary; 
• The acid value of hair oils of the plant-based types and based on the mixture of 
vegetable and mineral oils shall not exceed 1.0; 
• The number of microorganisms shall not exceed 100 CFU/gram of oil and shall 
not contain any pathogenic microorganisms; 


=== PAGINA 32/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION 
OF COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:32 de 40 
 
 
• The peroxide value should not exceed 10 milliequivalents / 1000 g of oil in case 
of use of natural oils; 
• For cold-pressed and unrefined natural oils, the maximum value of peroxide shall 
be in accordance with the requirements of RDC 481/2021 and its Normative 
Instruction 87/2021, therefore, it shall not exceed 15 milliequivalents / 1000 g of 
oil; 
• In case of using aloe oil in the product with ratio more than 5%, it shall be 
guaranteed that anthra quinone glucoside substances are not present. 
 
10.24. For Al Kohl the following additional requirements apply: 
• Should be free of galena (lead sulfide); 
• Antimony concentration should not exceed 2 ppm; 
• It should be free of any harmful or irritating substances to the eyes or tissues; 
• Additives, whether oils, liquids, semi-liquid substances or inorganic dyes, should 
be the substances allowed for use in cosmetics; 
• Should be free of organic dyes or pigments; 
• Should be free of lead, sulphur, arsenic, barium, mercury, aluminium, copper and 
its oxides; 
• Impurities in the form of ash should not exceed 0.1%. 
 
10.25. For perfumery products based on ethanol, such as cologne, lotion and perfumes, 
the following additional requirements apply: 
• It shall be clear and free from sediments; 
• It shall not become turbid when temperature reaches 15ºC or 59ºF; 
• Methanol content shall not exceed 5% by volume of ethanol content; 
• To avoid its ingestion, it shall have denaturants agents or any other substance, 
internationally permissible, that make the product bitter; 
• Propellents used in aerosol dispensers shall not cause damage to human health 
and to environment, and shall comply with current regulations; 
• If any allergen is present with concentration more than 0,001%, its presence 
should be mentioned in the list of ingredients, in accordance with the annex of 
GSO 1046/2021.

shampoo, creams, 
makeup 
 Ingredients of animal origin, such as keratin,glycerin, collagen, gelatin, shall be from 
Halal sources of origin, proven with a certificate 
Ethyl alcohol, if used, shall be from sources permitted for Halal certification, and the 
residual in the final product shall not exceed the limits established by the certification 
scheme (DT 7.6) 
Fragrances shall only be composed of ingredients suitable for Halal certification, free 
from non-Halal elements 
Natural cosmetics should not be extracted from endangered species 
Cosmetics shall not be toxic or cause allergies to users, with tests proving their 
safety. 
Permeability test, depending on the product application. 
Cross-contamination in the production process and storage, with non-Halal products 
Physical, chemical and microbiological contamination - controlled or reduced to an 
acceptable level, in accordance with cosmetics safety legislation 
Chemicals and 
cleaning products 
Enzymes and fatty acids shall come from Halal origin, proven with the Halal certificate 
Chemical products shall be free from non-Halal ingredients 
Products from esterification reactions shall control residual ethanol 
Fragrances shall only be composed of ingredients suitable for Halal certification, free 
from non-Halal elements 
Guidelines for use shall be clear to ensure that it does not cause poisoning to users 
Cross-contamination in the production process and storage, with non-Halal products 
Physical, chemical and microbiological contamination - controlled or reduced to an 
acceptable level, in accordance with the safety legislation applicable to the sector

1. 
OBJETIVO 
Este documento estabelece os requisitos gerais para as empresas da categoria K 
(GSO 2055-2) e L1 (SMIIC 02) (produtos de higiene pessoal, cosméticos e perfumes) que 
certificarem-se Halal com a FAMBRAS HALAL.  
 
 
2. 
CAMPO DE APLICAÇÃO 
Este documento é aplicável a todas as empresas certificadas pela FAMBRAS HALAL 
na categoria K (GSO 2055-2) e L1 (SMIIC 02). 
 
 
3.

4. 
DISPOSIÇÕES GERAIS 
Todos os produtos que almejam obter a certificação Halal da FAMBRAS HALAL para 
categoria K (GSO 2055-2) e L1 (SMIIC 02) devem atender aos requisitos estabelecidos 
neste documento.  
Neste documento, a palavra “deve” indica um requisito e a palavra “poderia”, uma 
recomendação. 
 
 
5. DEFINIÇÕES 
 
5.1. 
Shariah  
A palavra Shariah (jurisprudência islâmica) se refere a totalidade das ordens de Allah 
(Deus) que regulam todos os aspectos da vida de um muçulmano (MS 1900:2005), ou seja, 
tudo o que está descrito no alcorão sagrado, que fora as revelações de Deus ao profeta 
Muhammad (SAWS). Além do alcorão, a sunnah, ou seja, todas as práticas e ditos do 
profeta Muhammad (SAW) também fazem parte da shariah. 
 
5.2. 
Halal 
A palavra “Halal” significa lícito, o mesmo que permitido. Produtos designados Halal 
são aqueles os quais o consumo é permitido por Deus, de acordo com a Shariah.  
Halal é então a base de tudo o que é lícito, na alimentação, no social, nos atos 
praticados (conduta), na justiça, na vestimenta, nas finanças, etc., é um sistema baseado 


=== PAGINA 5/42 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE COSMÉTICOS E PRODUTOS PARA CUIDADOS PESSOAIS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:5 de 42 
 
nos princípios e valores que beneficiem a humanidade.  
O Alcorão Sagrado nos ensina na Sura 5a, verso 4: "Foram-vos permitidas todas as 
coisas sadias”.  
 
5.3. 
Abate Halal (Tazkeya) 
O abate Halal é um ritual islâmico de degola que deve ser feito de forma rápida, de 
modo a não permitir o sofrimento do animal, no qual deve ser cortada a traqueia, o esôfago 
e ambas as artérias e a veia jugular para apressar o sangramento e a morte do animal.  
O ritual de abate deve ser praticado de forma ética e com respeito aos animais a 
serem abatidos, seguindo as leis islâmicas do Alcorão Sagrado. Uma delas consiste na 
Tasmiyah, ou seja, invocar a frase Bismillah, Allahu Akbar (Em nome de Deus, Deus é 
maior), imediatamente antes da degola. A Bismillah é uma das formas de buscar a ajuda 
de Deus antes de qualquer ação a ser realizada por um muçulmano. Não há na 
jurisprudência islâmica um texto que confirme exatamente o significado da Tasmiyah, mas 
eruditos islâmicos afirmam que seria uma forma de agradecer a Deus pelo alimento, já que 
o sacrifício dos animais é realizado para garantir o sustento dos seres humanos e não por 
diversão ou sadismo. 
 
5.4. 
Haram  
A palavra Haram significa ilícito, impuro, ilegal. Refere-se ao que é inaceitável, 
prejudicial à saúde do corpo humano e sua moral, conforme determinado no Alcorão e 
Hadith (confirmação dos atos, afirmações, opiniões e modo de vida do profeta Muhammad 
(Que a paz e as bênçãos de Allah estejam sobre ele). 
 
5.5. Mashbouh 
Existem inúmeros produtos industrializados que utilizam aditivos e ingredientes que 
podem trazer perigos a saúde espiritual e material, pois pertencem a categoria Mashbouh 
(questionável/ duvidoso), que são: 
Elementos que podem ser de inúmeras origens de extração, como: 
a) 
Glicerina que pode ser de origem vegetal ou animal; 
b) 
L-Cisteína um aminoácido não essencial pode ser obtido de cabelo humano, 
pelo de porco, pena de pato, petróleo e vegetal. 
Produtos de origem Halal processados, por exemplo:  


=== PAGINA 6/42 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE COSMÉTICOS E PRODUTOS PARA CUIDADOS PESSOAIS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:6 de 42 
 
a) 
Suco de fruta clarificado com gelatina;  
b) 
Produtos como iogurte, biscoito, barra de cereal, que usam gelatina e mono 
e diglicerídeos;  
c) 
Aromas com álcool;  
d) 
Queijos, glutamato monossódico (realçador de sabor) e outros produtos 
fermentados com enzimas; 
e) 
Outros. 
 
5.6. 
Makruh 
Inapropriado. Não recomendado (encorajado) de acordo com a Shariah. 
 
5.7. 
Najasah (Impurezas)  
Najasah significa tudo que é considerado impuro, como fezes, urina, fluido prostático, 
uma descarga da uretra, esperma, pus, vômito, e excrementos de qualquer animal cuja 
carne não é permitida para consumo.  
 
5.8. 
Certificado Halal 
É um documento fiel de garantia emitido por uma instituição certificadora Halal, 
reconhecida por países islâmicos, para atestar que a empresa, processo e produtos, 
seguem os requisitos legais e critérios determinados pela Shariah. 
 
5.9. 
Organismos geneticamente modificados (OGM) 
Organismos obtidos através da transferência de gene de outras espécies vivas para 
uma planta, animal ou microrganismo por tecnologias de engenharia genética, alterando 
assim as características natas daquele organismo. 
  
5.10. Cosméticos e produtos para cuidados pessoais 
Qualquer substância, natural ou sintética, ou mistura destinada a uso externo nas 
diversas partes do corpo humano, pele, sistema capilar, unhas, lábios, órgãos genitais 
externos, dentes e membranas mucosas da cavidade oral, com o objetivo exclusivo ou 
principal de limpá-los, perfumá-los, alterar sua aparência e ou corrigir odores corporais e 
ou protegê-los ou mantê-los em bom estado. 
 


=== PAGINA 7/42 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE COSMÉTICOS E PRODUTOS PARA CUIDADOS PESSOAIS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:7 de 42 
 
5.11. Ingrediente 
Ingrediente é qualquer substância ou preparação de origem sintética ou natural 
usada na composição de um produto cosmético, incluindo agentes corantes, botânicos e 
fragrâncias. A definição inclui os materiais usados em quantidades estritamente 
necessárias como solventes ou como veículos para composições perfumantes e 
aromáticas, mas não inclui os materiais técnicos subsidiários usados na preparação, que 
não estão presentes no produto final em decorrência de um determinado processo químico. 
 
5.12. Al Kohl/ Athmad Kohl 
Substância negra encontrada na forma de nódulos rochosos sólidos ou na forma de 
pó, preparada por trituração desses nódulos, ou preparada na forma de lápis para escurecer 
os olhos, pálpebras, cílios ou sobrancelhas. 
 
5.13. Contaminantes 
Substâncias indesejadas, agentes biológicos, químicos, matérias estranhas, ou 
qualquer outra substância adicionada não intencionalmente ao produto, que podem se 
originar de fontes externas ao produto, e aos processos químicos, devido ao 
armazenamento incorreto de substâncias ou instabilidade da embalagem primária, por 
exemplo, e que possam comprometer a segurança do produto ou sua conformidade. 
 
5.14. Organismos Geneticamente Modificados (OGM) 
São organismos obtidos a partir da transferência de genes de um outro organismo 
doador, a partir de técnicas de engenharia genética e alteração de DNA. Os OGM são 
criados com o objetivo de favorecer determinada característica, como por exemplo, odor e 
coloração. 
 
5.15. Embalagem 
Todo o material ou recipiente desenvolvido para armazenar e envolver o produto, 
com o objetivo de proteger contra danos e deterioração, sob condições específicas de 
manuseio, armazenagem e transporte. 
 
5.16. Halal Mizen (peso/ medida) 
O Islam se preocupa com a proteção dos direitos do consumidor buscando obter a 


=== PAGINA 8/42 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE COSMÉTICOS E PRODUTOS PARA CUIDADOS PESSOAIS 
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:8 de 42 
 
medida e/ou peso correto no produto. Desta forma, qualquer prática ou processo aplicado 
de forma ineficaz, configura ato fraudulento. O Alcorão sagrado menciona: “Ai dos 
fraudadores, aqueles que, quando alguém lhes mede algo, exigem a medida exata/plena. 
Porém, quando eles medem ou pesam para os demais, fraudam-nos.” (Surata 83:1-3).  
Tendo isto em consideração, quando falamos também sobre comercialização, deve-
se transpor o mesmo conceito de justiça que é tão fortemente defendido no Islam, como 
está no sagrado alcorão: “Ó povo meu, disponde da medida e do peso com equidade; não 
defraudeis os humanos em seus bens e não pratiqueis a devassidão na terra, como 
corruptores.” (Surata 11:85). 
 
5.17. Apelo dos cosméticos e produtos para cuidados pessoais 
O apelo dos cosméticos e produtos de cuidados pessoais são as informações sobre 
a natureza e/ ou impacto e/ ou efetividade e/ ou conteúdo mencionados no produto ou na 
publicidade ou de qualquer forma promocional.  
 
5.18. Conservantes 
Substâncias adicionadas aos cosméticos e produtos de cuidados pessoais com o 
objetivo de inibir o crescimento de microrganismos durante sua produção e estocagem, 
e/ou proteger os produtos da contaminação durante o uso. 
 
5.19. Identificação das categorias GSO 
Os produtos certificados são classificados conforme categorias apresentadas na 
tabela 1 – norma Golfo, conforme GSO 2055-2.  
Os países do Golfo, referenciados neste documento são aqueles que fazem parte 
da GAC (GCC Accreditation Center) e seguem os standards estabelecidos pelo GSO, 
sendo estes Arábia Saudita, Omã, Bahrein, Kuwait, Qatar, Iêmen e Emirados Árabes 
Unidos.  
A FAMBRAS Halal poderá atuar em todas as categorias indicadas na tabela, no 
entanto, para os países do Golfo o reconhecimento é aplicável somente para as categorias 
sinalizadas em azul, sendo para as demais categorias, mediante demanda e necessidade, 
a FAMBRAS HALAL poderá pleitear o reconhecimento.

5.20. Identificação das categorias SMIIC 
Os produtos certificados são classificados conforme categorias apresentadas na 
tabela 2 – norma SMIIC 02:2019; 
SMIIC significa Standards and Metrology Institute for Islamic Countries (Instituto de 
Normas e Metrologia para Países Islâmicos), sendo uma organização intergovernamental, 
visa estabelecer padrões comuns a serem implementados em toda a região da OIC - 
Organisation of the Islamic Cooperation (Organização da Cooperação Islâmica), que visa 
garantir a proteção dos consumidores e a interoperabilidade dos produtos, e também para 
fortalecer a posição de mercado dos estados membros da OIC no mercado global. 
Os países que integram a OIC são: Afeganistão, Albânia, Azerbaijão, Bangladesh, 
Emirados Árabes Unidos, Brunei, Darussalam, Argélia, Indonésia, Marrocos, Palestina, 
Gabão, Iraque, Irã, Camarões, Catar, Quirguistão, Kuwait, Líbia, Líbano, Maldivas, Malásia, 
Egito, Nigéria, Omã, Uzbequistão, Paquistão, Senegal, Somália, Sudão, Síria, Arábia 
Saudita, Tadjiquistão, Tunísia, Turquia, Turcomenistão, Uganda, Jordânia, Iêmen, Bósnia 
e Herzegovina.

=== PAGINA 14/42 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE COSMÉTICOS E PRODUTOS PARA CUIDADOS PESSOAIS  
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:14 de 42 
 
 
6. REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA FABRICAÇÃO DE COSMÉTICOS E PRODUTOS PARA 
CUIDADOS PESSOAIS HALAL  
 
6.1. 
O fabricante deve evidenciar conformidade com as questões legais aplicáveis ao 
país e região; 
 
6.2. 
Os cosméticos e produtos para cuidados pessoais não devem causar intoxicação 
e devem ser seguros para a saúde quando usados em condições normais ou 
razoavelmente previsíveis de uso. 
6.2.1. 
Deve existir evidências de segurança dos produtos, por exemplo, relatórios 
de teste ou outros documentos relevantes.  
 
6.3. 
Os cosméticos e produtos para cuidados pessoais devem ser homogêneos, 
estáveis e suas propriedades que impactam na segurança, eficácia e qualidade 
não devem mudar durante sua vida útil, quando armazenadas e usadas de acordo 
com as instruções. 
 
6.4. 
Os cosméticos e produtos para cuidados pessoais e seus ingredientes, auxiliares 
de processo, insumos e embalagens devem estar livres de qualquer elemento 
não-Halal, descritos na tabela 3, e de qualquer impureza (Najasah), conforme item 
5.7. 
Na tabela do anexo 1 estão listados alguns pontos críticos Halal que devem ser 
considerados, para cada tipo de produto. 
6.4.1. Deve haver um processo de homologação dos fornecedores para a seleção e 
aprovação das matérias primas, insumos, auxiliares de processo e materiais de 
embalagem, de forma a garantir a qualidade e integridade do produto, bem como 
sua segurança para o Halal. 
 
6.5. 
Os cosméticos e produtos para cuidados pessoais e seus ingredientes, auxiliares 
de processo, insumos e embalagens não contém derivados animais que não 
foram abatidos de maneira Halal (de acordo com a Shariah). 
  


=== PAGINA 15/42 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE COSMÉTICOS E PRODUTOS PARA CUIDADOS PESSOAIS  
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:15 de 42 
 
 
6.6. 
Os cosméticos e produtos para cuidados pessoais e seus ingredientes, insumos, 
auxiliares de processo, e materiais de embalagem devem ser garantidos frente ao 
seu status Halal com documentos oficiais que demonstrem sua origem lícita, 
exceto quando forem de origem 100% natural, como por exemplo vegetais, 
minérios e outros – conforme avaliação da FAMBRAS HALAL; 
 
6.7. 
Os cosméticos e produtos para cuidados pessoais e seus ingredientes, insumos, 
auxiliares de processo e materiais de embalagem não devem conter 
contaminantes, substâncias tóxicas e poluentes perigosos que possam ser 
considerados prejudiciais para a saúde.  
6.7.1. 
Devem existir evidências que comprovem que os produtos são estáveis e 
seguros, por exemplo, laudos de atoxidade, laudos de estabilidade/ 
compatibilidade do produto ou outros documentos relevantes.  
 
6.8. 
Todos os produtos certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL, e suas matérias-primas, 
insumos, auxiliares de processo e materiais de embalagem devem atender aos 
padrões físico-químicos e microbiológicos estabelecidos para cosméticos e 
produtos para cuidados pessoais, e conter apenas substâncias permitidas e dento 
dos limites máximos estabelecidos pela legislação vigente. 
 
6.9. 
Todas as matérias-primas, insumos, auxiliares de processo e materiais de 
embalagem, utilizados nos produtos certificados, devem ser previamente 
informados e aprovados pela FAMBRAS HALAL. 
 
 
 
 


=== PAGINA 16/42 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE COSMÉTICOS E PRODUTOS PARA CUIDADOS PESSOAIS  
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:16 de 42 
 
 
Tabela 3. Tabela de produtos não Halal 
Origem 
Não-Halal 
Animais 
Animais não abatidos de maneira Halal – de acordo com as regras islâmicas; Animais 
abatidos por politeístas, irreligiosos, seculares, ateus, magias, apostasia e outros 
descrentes exceto por cristãos e judeus; Animais mortos naturalmente; Animais 
sufocados; Animais fatalmente espancados (Mawqouza); Animais mortos em 
decorrência de queda (Motaradiah); Animais mortos por cabeçada (Nateehah); 
Animais comidos por animais de rapina; Animais em cujo abate o nome de Deus 
(Allah) não é mencionado; Animais abatidos por idolatria; 
Porco; Burros domésticos; Mulas; Elefantes; Macacos; Cachorros; Raposas, gatos e 
similares. 
Predadores como animais de presas e similares, como leões, chitas, ursos. 
Aves de rapina com garras afiadas utilizadas para atacar e semelhantes, como 
águias, falcões, pardais, peregrinos, papagaios e corujas. 
Roedores, répteis, pragas e similares, tais como ratos, centopéias, serpentes, cobras, 
varanus, lagartos, geckos, camaleões, ouriços, marten, morcegos, esquilos, tourão, 
toupeira e Coleópteros (exceto dabb lagartos e jerboas). 
Os escorpiões, todos os tipos de insetos, vermes e animais que são proibidos por lei 
de serem mortos, como formigas, abelhas, pica-pau e poupa (ou boubela/ hoopoes), 
com exceção de gafanhotos (a cochonilha devido a sua similaridade com o gafanhoto) 
e e partes inevitáveis de abelhas como o mel. 
Criaturas repulsivas e similares, tais como animais de conchas, larvas e outros 
animais semelhantes. 
Animais alimentados com itens impuros, a menos que estejam confinados e 
alimentados nos últimos três dias com alimentação permitida pelas Regras Islâmicas. 
Aquáticos  
Animais tóxicos ou espécies prejudiciais, a menos que substâncias tóxicas ou 
substâncias nocivas sejam removidas durante a preparação. 
Plantas 
Plantas e seus produtos/ partes toxicas, derivados nocivos, venenosos ou narcóticos, 
exceto se a substancia nociva ou venenosa seja removida durante a preparação. 
Bebidas 
Bebidas alcoólicas (contém etanol), bebidas intoxicantes ou aquelas que contêm 
álcoois, substâncias narcóticas, venenosas, tóxicas ou prejudiciais. 
OGM 
Todos os elementos produzidos por modificação genética a partir de uma espécie ou 
mais espécies proibidas (não halal). 
Sangue e 
derivados de 
sangue de 
animais ou 
humanos 
O sangue e todos os seus derivados são proibidos, assim como todos os fluidos 
corporais de origem humana ou animal, como vômitos e urina. 
Aditivos 
alimentares 
Todos os aditivos tóxicos, prejudiciais e seus derivados. 
Produtos e derivados de materiais não Halal. 
Enzimas 
Todas as enzimas derivadas de fontes não Halal. 
Microrganismos 
Microrganismos como germes, fungos, toxinas e fermento nocivo produzidos em 
ambientes não Halal ou usando ingredientes não Halal. 
Fonte: GSO 2055-2 adaptado.  
 
 


=== PAGINA 17/42 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE COSMÉTICOS E PRODUTOS PARA CUIDADOS PESSOAIS  
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:17 de 42 
 
 
6.10. Álcool etílico/ Etanol: 
6.10.1. Os cosméticos e produtos para cuidados pessoais e seus ingredientes, 
insumos, auxiliares de processo ou qualquer outro elemento que entre em 
contato diretamente com o produto Halal não são/ não podem conter bebidas 
alcoólicas (khamr), ou derivados separados fisicamente das bebidas 
alcoólicas. 
6.10.2. Produtos derivados de indústria de bebida alcoólica, como extrato de 
levedura, podem ser usados desde que submetidos a um processo de 
purificação de acordo com a Shariah, que consiste na remoção completa de 
cor, odor e sabor da bebida alcoólica. 
6.10.3. Álcool etílico (etanol) pode ocorrer naturalmente nos produtos devido a 
fermentação espontânea, no entanto, é necessário que estes não excedam 
o limite máximo de etanol estabelecido pela FAMBRAS HALAL, que é de 1%. 
6.10.4. Em alguns casos, o álcool etílico (etanol) é indispensável na fabricação, 
sendo este usado como auxiliar/ aditivo de processo. Neste caso, o etanol é 
permitido, desde que a fonte de obtenção não seja de bebidas alcoólicas (ex. 
vinho) e que estes não excedam o limite máximo de etanol estabelecido pela 
FAMBRAS HALAL, que é de 1%.  
 
6.11. Ingredientes:  
6.11.1. Todos os tipos de águas são permitidos para aplicação como ingredientes 
com exceção de água reutilizada – proveniente de esgoto e água 
contaminada com qualquer elemento não Halal, indicado na tabela 1.  
6.11.2. Todos os tipos de terra são permitidos com exceção daquelas terras 
retiradas do cemitério, fazendas de animais e áreas de despejo. 
6.11.3. Todos os ingredientes agrícolas de origem Halal, tratados física ou 
quimicamente, são permitidos desde que não sejam expostos a Najasah em 
nenhuma etapa de todo o processo de produção. 
6.11.4. Animais e plantas de espécies ameaçadas (que atendam aos requisitos da 
Convenção sobre o Comércio Internacional das Espécies da Flora e Fauna 
- CITES) não devem ser utilizados. 
6.11.5. Quando aplicável, a exploração econômica de produto acabado ou de 


=== PAGINA 18/42 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE COSMÉTICOS E PRODUTOS PARA CUIDADOS PESSOAIS  
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:18 de 42 
 
 
material reprodutivo desenvolvido a partir de acesso ao patrimônio genético 
ou ao conhecimento tradicional associado deve estar em conformidade com 
a Lei nº 13.123, de 2015. 
6.11.6. Organismos 
geneticamente 
modificados 
são 
permitidos 
quando 
provenientes de fonte Halal, não sendo originados de gene humano, animal 
ilícito ou que que não tenha sido abatido conforme a jurisprudência islâmica. 
6.11.7. Na presença de microrganismos no processo, o meio de cultura deve ser 
Halal. É proibido usar microrganismos, como bactérias, fungos, e leveduras, 
que sejam tóxicos e prejudiciais à saúde humana. 
 
6.12. O nome do produto final, a marca, o logo, as características sensoriais e o modelo 
da embalagem não infringem os princípios da Shariah, por exemplo a marca não 
faz alusão a elementos Haram, como bacon, cerveja, ou qualquer outro que possa 
confundir o consumidor.  
 
6.13. As Regras Islâmicas devem ser aplicadas em todas as fases da cadeia de 
produtos certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL, incluindo recebimento, preparação, 
embalagem, 
rotulagem, 
armazenamento, 
transporte, 
comercialização 
e 
distribuição; 
 
6.14. As autoridades oficiais podem adotar todos os procedimentos necessários para 
verificar a conformidade dos produtos com os requisitos especiais dos produtos 
Halal e podem tomar as medidas adequadas de acordo com a legislação local. 
 
6.15. Todos os tipos de produtos de limpeza, ou manutenção, tais como detergentes, 
sanitizantes, graxas e lubrificantes, devem ser previamente informados e 
aprovados pela FAMBRAS HALAL, e devem ser livres de componentes ou 
materiais não-halal (proibidos para a Shariah). 
 
6.16. Os cosméticos e produtos para cuidados pessoais devem ser embalados em 
recipientes adequados, limpos, secos e fechados com segurança de forma que 
não afetem ou reajam com o produto, protegendo-o contra danos e deterioração. 


=== PAGINA 19/42 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE COSMÉTICOS E PRODUTOS PARA CUIDADOS PESSOAIS  
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:19 de 42 
 
 
6.17. O peso do produto Halal declarado na embalagem do produto não pode ser inferior 
ao seu peso real (vide 5.16). 
6.17.1. A empresa deve realizar testes amostrais para aferição dos pesos dos produtos 
Halal, comparando o peso descrito na embalagem, com o peso real; 
6.17.2. No caso de produtos não conformes, onde o peso real é menor que o peso da 
embalagem, deverá haver uma segregação, onde o produto será condenado 
para comercialização como produto Halal. A empresa deve registrar a correção 
e a ação corretiva em casos de não conformidades;  
6.17.3. Deve existir registros diários destas conferências. 
 
 
7. REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA AS ÁREAS DE PRODUÇÃO HALAL 
 
7.1. 
Os cosméticos e produtos para cuidados pessoais Halal devem estar 
completamente separados dos produtos não Halal em toda e qualquer etapa do 
fluxo de fabricação 
(recebimento, 
preparação, 
embalagem, 
rotulagem, 
armazenamento e transporte), para evitar a contaminação cruzada.  
 
7.2. 
Para unidades que fabricam produtos Halal e não Halal: 
7.2.1. 
Os equipamentos devem obrigatoriamente ser higienizados antes do início 
da produção Halal, sendo o procedimento de higienização devidamente 
validado; 
7.2.2. 
É recomendado que o dia de produção inicie com a fabricação dos produtos 
Halal; 
7.2.3. 
É necessário identificar as linhas que processam o Halal, com o intuito de 
prevenir contaminação cruzada; 
7.2.4. 
Não é autorizado realizar a higienização de forma alternada entre 
equipamentos que processam Halal e equipamentos que processam 
produtos não Halal; 
7.2.5. 
As práticas aplicadas na amostragem devem garantir que não ocorre 
contaminação cruzada do produto certificado Halal, ou seus ingredientes, 
insumos e auxiliares de processo, com qualquer produto não Halal. Por 


=== PAGINA 20/42 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE COSMÉTICOS E PRODUTOS PARA CUIDADOS PESSOAIS  
DT 7.6 
Rev.: 09 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:20 de 42 
 
 
exemplo, usar um utensílio para coletar a amostra em um produto não Halal 
e Halal, sem a devida higienização.  
7.2.6. 
Testes de validação poderão ser realizados na primeira produção Halal 
seguinte a produção não Halal, sejam eles no produto e/ou equipamentos, 
conforme determinado pelo organismo de certificação; 
7.2.7. 
As matérias-primas/ ingredientes Halal e não Halal, devem ser segregadas 
e identificadas; 
 
7.3. 
Se as linhas de produção já tiverem sido utilizadas para fabricar produtos com 
ingredientes de origem suína, por exemplo shampoo com colágeno ou queratina 
suína, antes de convertê-la para fabricação de produtos Halal é necessário que a 
mesma passe por um ritual de purificação, o qual consiste em submeter os 
equipamentos a passagem de água + agentes de limpeza, como detergente e 
sanitizante, no mínimo 7 vezes, para que ocorra a remoção completa de cor, sabor 
e aroma do contaminante.  
7.3.1. 
Caso o procedimento padrão da empresa certificada não totalize 7 
passagens de produto (somando a passagem de água, produtos de limpeza 
e enxague), o mesmo deverá ser repetido quantas vezes necessárias até 
essa totalização, ou poderão ser realizados enxagues adicionais apenas com 
água, de forma que, em equipamentos abertos, ocorra o fluxo ininterrupto de 
água por no mínimo 30 segundos, seguido de total evacuação da água. Este 
procedimento será considerado como 1 (um) enxague. 
7.3.2. 
A conversão das linhas entre produção Halal e não Halal, com ingredientes 
de origem suína, não deve ser realizada de forma contínua.  
 
7.4. 
Deverá haver um supervisor da FAMBRAS HALAL, acompanhando todas as 
fabricações dos cosméticos e produtos para cuidados pessoais Halal que contém 
ingredientes de origem animal (proveniente de animais lícitos abatidos conforme 
o Halal), os quais são adquiridos com um certificado Halal exclusivo por lote, como 
gelatina, colágeno. Outros tipos de produtos podem ter a mesma obrigação 
quando a empresa fabrica produtos Halal e não Halal, e são identificados como 
alto risco para o Halal, sendo que está necessidade será definida pela FAMBRAS

FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE COSMÉTICOS E PRODUTOS PARA CUIDADOS PESSOAIS

HALAL. 
7.4.1. 
O supervisor deve ser muçulmano, mentalmente sadio, com idade mínima 
de 18 anos e competente para realizar com eficiência sua função, 
demonstrando conhecimento e compreensão dos fundamentos islâmicos e 
dos requerimentos referentes ao Halal. 
 
7.5. 
Todos os equipamentos, linhas de produção, ferramentas, utensílios e materiais 
associados utilizados para a preparação de produtos certificados pela FAMBRAS 
HALAL, devem ser limpos, livres de contaminação com materiais não-Halal e de 
impurezas, e não devem ser fabricados ou conter qualquer tipo de material não 
Halal, como osso, unha ou dentes (é preferível o aço inoxidável). 
 
 
8. 
REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA ARMAZENAGEM E TRANSPORTE DE PRODUTOS 
HALAL 
 
8.1. 
Caso a empresa tenha produtos finais com diferentes status Halal, os produtos 
Halal devem estar segregados e devidamente identificados como tal.  
8.1.1. Para produtos em pó armazenados em embalagens flexíveis (embalagens 
plásticas flexíveis, saches de papel, por exemplo), é obrigatório que exista uma 
segregação física significativa (média de 50 cm) entre os produtos com diferentes 
status, para garantir que não ocorra contaminação cruzada. Quando 
armazenados em estantes os produtos Halal não devem estar abaixo de produtos 
não Halal.  
8.1.2. Para produtos sólidos, pastosos e líquidos armazenados em embalagens 
rígidas (tambores, vidros e latas, por exemplo) não é obrigatória uma 
segregação física, com distancia significativa, no entanto os produtos Halal e 
não Halal não devem ficar encostados (mesmo quando já acondicionados em 
embalagem primária, secundária e paletizados). É altamente recomendado 
que os produtos Halal quando armazenados em estantes não fiquem abaixo 
de produtos não Halal.  
8.1.3. A empresa certificada deverá implantar uma avaliação do perigo e um

FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO
