=== SHEET: Informe DETALLADO (dimensoes: A2:I66) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS CERTIFICACIÓN HALAL 
 
INFORME DE AUDITORIA DE MATERIALES DE EMPAQUE Y ENVOLTORIOS (CATEGORIA I) – ETAPA 1 (DT 7.10) |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.15
Rev.: 06
Fecha: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAYOR | NC - MENOR | OBS | NA | Evidencias | 
 | 1.    REQUISITOS GENERALES DE LA EMPRESA FABRICANTE Y DE LOS MATERIALES DE EMPAQUE Y ENVASE |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1.    La empresa tiene sus productos cumpliendo con los requisitos Halal y cuenta con una licencia de operación sanitaria de acuerdo con las cuestiones legales del país/región. (DT 7.10 - Item 6.1 e 6.13). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2. Al evaluar el diagrama de flujo y las fichas técnicas, es posible confirmar que todos los materiales de empaque y envoltorios y sus componentes, incluidos los recubrimientos y lubricantes, están libres de elementos Haram, Najash (impuros), derivados de animales no procedentes de sacrificio Halal y sustancias tóxicas que. son perjudiciales para la salud. (DT 7.10 - Itens 6.2, 6.3 e 6.5) |  |  |  |  |  | Mencionar las fichas técnicas de los productos y el certificado de migración si aplica | 
 | 1.3. Todas las materias primas, coadyuvantes de proceso, insumos y materiales de empaque fueron previamente informados y aprobados por FAMBRAS HALAL, y cuentan con documentación que demuestra su origen legal bajo Halal, garantizando su Estatus Halal. (DT 7.10 - Item 6.4 e 6.9). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. Los materiales de empaque utilizados en los productos con certificación Halal cuentan con documentación que acredita su no toxicidad. (DT 7.10 - Item 6.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5. Existe un procedimiento de aprobación y selección de proveedores de materias primas, insumos, coadyuvantes de proceso y materiales de embalaje, con el fin de garantizar la calidad e integridad de los alimentos, así como su seguridad para Halal.(DT 7.10 – Item 6.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6.Los materiales de empaque y envoltorios, y sus componentes, especialmente pigmentos y colorantes, no tienen tendencia a migrar, lo que puede provocar que los componentes migrados reaccionen o se mezclen con el producto alimenticio envasado. Además, no provocan la degradación de las propiedades sensoriales de los productos envasados. (DT 7.10 - Itens 6.6, 6.7 e 10.2). |  |  |  |  |  | Citar los informes de migración evaluados | 
 | 1.7. Las envoltorios naturales provienen de animales con permitidos Halal que han sido sacrificados de manera Halal y se puede realizar su trazabilidad.(DT 7.10 - Item 6.8). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8. El alcohol etílico, cuando es necesario, se utiliza únicamente como coadyuvante/aditivo de proceso, y existe documentación que demuestra que no proviene de industrias de bebidas alcohólicas.(DT 7.10 - Item 6.10.1 e 6.10.3). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. Si la empresa utiliza productos derivados de industrias de bebidas alcohólicas en forma sólida, como extracto de levadura de cerveza, se cuenta con registros del procedimiento de purificación realizado para eliminar por completo el color, olor y sabor de la bebida alcohólica. (DT 7.10 - Item 6.10.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.10. El nombre del producto final, marca, logotipo, imagen, composición y diseño del empaque no violan los principios de la Shariah, por ejemplo la marca no alude a elementos Haram, como tocino, jamón, cerveza o cualquier otra cosa que pueda confundir al consumidor. consumidor. (DT 7.10 - Item 6.11). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.11. A partir de una evaluación documental general del proceso, por parte del auditor religioso, es posible verificar el cumplimiento de las normas islámicas en toda la fase de la cadena productiva (recepción, preparación, embalaje, etiquetado, almacenamiento y transporte) de los artículos certificados por FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.10 – Item 6.12) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.12.Todo tipo de productos de limpieza o mantenimiento, como detergentes, desinfectantes, grasas y lubricantes, han sido previamente informados y aprobados por FAMBRAS HALAL, y cuentan con documentación que demuestra que están libres de componentes o materiales no halal (prohibidos por la Shariah). (DT 7.10 – Item 6.14) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.13. Los componentes de los materiales de empaque y envoltorios están incluidos en las listas positivas de Componentes para Materiales, Envases en Contacto con Alimentos, y si existen restricciones de uso o criterios de pureza, se respetan. Además, sólo contienen sustancias permitidas, dentro de los límites máximos establecidos por la legislación vigente y cumplen con los límites de migración (DT 7.10 – Item 6.15) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.14. El empaque es resistente a impactos y en el caso de embalajes multicapa existen controles de residuos de disolventes. (DT 7.10 - Item 6.16). |  |  |  |  |  | Mencionar el informe de resistencia evaluado, y en el caso de envases multicapa, el informe de controles residuales. | 
 | 1.15. Las características de permeabilidad del empaque son adecuadas para los materiales envasados. (DT 7.10 - Item 6.17). |  |  |  |  |  | Mencionar el informe de permeabilidad. | 
 | 1.16. Los empaque de materiales de empaque y envoltorios, como paletas, películas y contenedores, están fabricados con materiales adecuados y limpios y no contaminan los envases de alimentos fabricados. En los casos en los que existe un riesgo potencial de contaminación de los alimentos debido a la migración, se realiza un control para la prevención. (DT 7.10 - Item 6.18) |  |  |  |  |  | Mencionar las fichas técnicas del producto y el informe de migración si aplica. | 
 | 2. REQUISITOS GENERALES PARA LA FABRICACIÓN DE MATERIALES DE EMPAQUE HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1. La empresa proporciona en sus documentos, como el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/HAS), el historial de cualificación y formación de los empleados (DT 7.10 - Item 7.2.1 e item 11). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2.Evaluación solo para unidades que fabrican productos Halal y no Halal (DT 7.10 - Item 7.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2.1.La empresa prevé en sus documentos, como el procedimiento de higiene o el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/HAS), la obligación de limpiar los equipos antes del inicio de la producción Halal, así como la validación de este procedimiento. (DT 7.10 - Item 7.2.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2.2. La empresa establece en sus documentos, como el procedimiento de higiene o el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/HAS), que la higiene de los equipos no debe realizarse de forma alternada entre equipos que procesen productos Halal y equipos que procesen productos no Halal (Ej. Realizar un CIP alternado entre equipos que procesen Halal y no Halal). (DT 7.10 - Item 7.2.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.3. La empresa prevé en sus documentos, como el procedimiento de higiene o el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/HAS), que si las líneas de producción ya se han utilizado para fabricar productos de origen porcino, antes de reconvertirlas a la fabricación de productos Halal es necesario que haya pasado por un ritual de purificación, el cual consiste en someter el equipo a agua + agentes de limpieza, como detergente y sanitizante, al menos 7 veces, para que se elimine por completo el color, sabor y aroma contaminante.  (DT 7.10 - Item 7.3). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.4.La empresa prevé en sus documentos, como el procedimiento de higiene o el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/HAS), que la conversión de líneas entre producción Halal y producción porcina no debe realizarse de forma continua. (DT 7.10 - Item 7.3.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.5.    Si la empresa fabrica productos Halal que contienen carne, gelatina u otros elementos de origen animal (procedentes de animales sacrificados), o identificados como de alto riesgo para Halal, hay un supervisor musulmán competente de FAMBRAS HALAL trabajando en el sitio durante la producción Halal (DT 7.10 – Item 7.4 e 7.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.6.    La empresa establece en sus documentos, como el procedimiento de higiene o el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/HAS), que todos los equipos, líneas de producción, herramientas, utensilios y materiales asociados utilizados en la fabricación de materiales de embalaje y envoltorios deben estar limpios y limpios, y no debe estar fabricado ni contener ningún tipo de material que no sea Halal (DT 7.10 – Item 7.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3. REQUISITOS GENERALES DE ALMACENAMIENTO Y TRANSPORTE |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1.    La empresa es consciente y documenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) que todos los empaques y envoltorios certificados por FAMBRAS HALAL deben estar identificados (basta con tener el sello visible en el embalaje secundario del producto Halal), y, según aplicabilidad , separados de otros productos no Halal durante el almacenamiento y transporte, para evitar que se mezclen con productos no Halal. (DT 7.10 – Item 8.1 y 8.2 y DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2. La empresa documenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) la evaluación de los peligros y el procedimiento adecuado, con las medidas que se deben tomar, por ejemplo en caso de rotura/fuga de empaque de productos no Halal en el almacén del producto final, para garantizar la no contaminación cruzada del producto Halal. (DT 7.10 – Item 8.1.2 y DT 7.3 – Item 11.1 y 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3.    La empresa tiene conocimiento y documenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) que está prohibido transportar juntos productos Halal y productos de origen porcino y/o sus derivados, para evitar la contaminación cruzada con productos Haram. (DT 7.10 – Item 8.3 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.4. El procedimiento de transporte de la empresa prevé controles adecuados para la seguridad Halal, evitando la contaminación cruzada con productos no Halal, y el cumplimiento de las condiciones de higiene e integridad de los materiales.(DT 7.10 – Item 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.  REQUISITOS GENERALES DE CALIDAD Y SEGURIDAD |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.   A sistemática de Boas Práticas de Fabricação contém no mínimo (DT 7.10 – Item 10.5): |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.1.   Manual de Boas Práticas de Fabricação e/ ou Manual da Qualidade |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | a) Higienização das instalações, equipamentos, móveis e utensílios; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | b) Controle de água; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | c) Higiene e saúde dos manipuladores; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | d) Manejo dos resíduos; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | e) Manutenção preventiva e calibração de equipamentos; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | f) Controle integrado de vetores e pragas; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | g) Seleção das matérias-primas (componentes) e atividades subcontratadas; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | h) Controle de acesso; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | i) Qualidade do ar e ventilação, se aplicável; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | j) Reprocesso: Controle e rastreabilidade |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2. A empresa possui um programa de rastreabilidade do produto Halal. (DT 7.10 – Item 10.5 e DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.7 e 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. O programa de recolhimento da empresa, contempla a comunicação com a certificadora Halal, e preve a necessidade de realizá-lo mediante contaminação que desabone o status Halal do produto. (DT 7.9 – Item 10.5 e 10.8) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.    A empresa possui um programa de Análise de Perigos e Pontos de Controle Críticos (APPCC), contemplando controle de quebra de vidro, ceramica, plástico ou qualquer elemento pontiagudo, que pode ser proveniente do processo. (DT 7.10 – Item 10.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. A empresa possui uma metodologia de tratamento de desvios, reclamações e não-conformidades (referentes a auditorias internas e externas), incluindo ações corretivas eficazes, adotadas para evitar ocorrências futuras. (DT 7.10 – Item 10.7 e 10.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.    SISTEMA DE GARANTIA HALAL/ HALAL ASSURANCE SYSTEM (HAS) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1.  A empresa possui um Sistema de Garantia Halal documentado de acordo com a condição interna da empresa, sendo este desenvolvido em um formato compatível com o sistema de gestão da qualidade, e de forma a garantir o status Halal dos produtos certificados (DT 7.10 - Item 10.1 e DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 e 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2.  A empresa estabelece uma política Halal (seja ela interligada ou não com outra política da empresa) que indique o seu compromisso com a produção de produtos Halal, estando esta devidamente assinada pela alta direção da empresa. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.1, 7.1 e 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3.  Existe uma equipe responsável pela Gestão Halal, sendo esta apresentada no Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ – HAS), onde estão descritas as devidas responsabilidades e autonomias. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.2, 8.1 e 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4.  A equipe é composta pelas autoridades necessárias (incluindo o supervisor Halal, se aplicável), que estão diretamente envolvidas no processo de produção Halal, e possui autonomia para assegurar a condição Halal dos produtos e responder pelos apontamentos encontrados durante as auditorias (DT 7.3 – Item 8.2 e 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5. A empresa possui um planejamento para realização de treinamentos Halal programados para ocorrer no mínimo uma vez ao ano, e os mesmos são obrigatorios para todas as pessoas envolvidas no processo Halal, incluindo novos funcionários. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 e 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. O conteúdo dos treinamentos Halal contemplam o conceito Halal, Haram e Mashbooh, mercado que a empresa pretende atingir com a certificação, e controles internos estabelecidos para garantir a certificação. Ainda, para os  funcionários envolvidos no monitoramento dos PCCH, os treinamentoss incluem a importância da sua atividade diante da segurança do Halal. (DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 e 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. O documento de registro do Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) descreve a composição e o fluxograma de cada um dos produtos certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.4, 10.1 e 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8. Os Perigos e Pontos Críticos de Controle Halal são identificados, validados e registrados no HAS da empresa, através de uma matriz de risco que proporciona a análise da probabilidade de ocorrência do perigo versus a severidade da ocorrência do perigo, levando em consideração a contaminação cruzada com produtos Haram em cada etapa do fluxograma. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 e 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. O Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) elaborado pela empresa prevê a necessidade de separação completa entre as matérias primas e produtos Halal e não Halal, bem como sua identificação, em toda e qualquer etapa do fluxo de fabricação (recebimento, preparação, embalagem, rotulagem, armazenamento e transporte), a fim de evitar qualquer contaminação cruzada (DT 7.10 - Item 7.1, 7.2.4 e 7.2.9 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10. As medidas preventivas para a ocorrência do risco e as medidas corretivas em caso de desvios estão claramente definidas no HAS da empresa. Bem como as tratativas a serem adotadas para gestão dos Pontos Críticos de Controle Halal estão bem documentadas, incluindo os registros dos monitoramentos, os responsáveis e as ações no caso de desvios.  (DT 7.3 – Item 11.4 e 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. A empresa descreve no documento de registro do Sistema de Garantia Halal uma metodologia para auditoria interna HAS. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.6 e 12.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12. A auditoria interna do HAS está prevista (para o primeiro ano de certificação) ou foi realizada, pelo menos uma vez ao ano, ou mais vezes, se necessário. (DT 7.3 – Item 12.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
=== SHEET: ESCOPO AUDITADO (dimensoes: A1:E142) ===

 | 3.     Principais objetivos da auditoria: |  |  |  |  |  |  |

=== SHEET: Relatório DETALHADO (dimensoes: A2:I66) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO
 
RELATÓRIO DE AUDITORIA DE MATERIAIS DE EMBALAGEM E INVÓLUCROS (CATEGORIA I) – ESTÁGIO 1 (DT 7.10) |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.15
Rev.: 06
Data: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAIOR | NC - MENOR | OBS | NA | Evidências | 
 | 1.    REQUISITOS GERAIS DA EMPRESA FABRICANTE E DOS MATERIAIS DE EMBALAGEM E INVÓLUCROS |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1.    A empresa  possui seus produtos em conformidade com os requisitos Halal e possui um alvará de funcionamento sanitário em conformidade com as questões legais do país/ região. (DT 7.10 - Item 6.1 e 6.13). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2.  Através da avaliação do fluxograma e fichas técnicas é possível confirmar que todos os materiais de embalagem e invólucros e seus componentes, incluindo revestimentos e lubrificantes, estão isentos de elementos Haram, Najash (Impuros), derivados animais não provenientes do abate Halal e de substâncias tóxicas que são prejudiciais à saúde.  (DT 7.10 - Itens 6.2, 6.3 e 6.5) |  |  |  |  |  | Mencionar as fichas técnicas dos produtos e laudo de migração se aplicável | 
 | 1.3. Todas as matérias primas, auxiliares de processo, insumos e materiais de embalagem, foram previamente informados e aprovados pela FAMBRAS HALAL, e possuem documentação que demonstrem sua origem lícita diante do Halal, garantindo seu Status Halal (DT 7.10 - Item 6.4 e 6.9). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. Os materiais de embalagem usados nos produtos certificados Halal possuem documentação que comprovem sua atoxidade. (DT 7.10 - Item 6.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5. Existe um procedimento para homologação e seleção de fornecedores de matérias primas, insumos, auxiliares de processo e materiais de embalagem, de forma a garantir a qualidade e integridade do alimento, bem como sua segurança para o Halal. (DT 7.10 – Item 6.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6. Os materiais de embalagem e invólucros, e seus componentes, especialmente pigmentos e corantes, não possuem tendência para migração, o que pode fazer com que os componentes migrados reajam ou se misturem com o produto alimentício acondicionado. Além disso, não conduzem à degradação das propriedades sensoriais dos produtos acondicionados (DT 7.10 - Itens 6.6, 6.7 e 10.2). |  |  |  |  |  | Citar os laudos de migração avaliados | 
 | 1.7. Os invólucros naturais provém de animais permitidos para o Halal, que foram abatidos de maneira Halal e são rastreáveis (DT 7.10 - Item 6.8). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8. Álcool etílico, quando necessário, é utilizado somente como auxiliar/ aditivo de processo, e  existe documentação que comprova que este não é proveniente de indústrias de bebidas alcoólicas. (DT 7.10 - Item 6.10.1 e 6.10.3). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. Caso a empresa utilize produtos derivados de indústrias de bebidas alcoólicas em forma sólida, como extrato de levedura de cerveja, há registros do procedimento de purificação realizado para eliminação completa da cor, odor e sabor da bebida alcoólica. (DT 7.10 - Item 6.10.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.10. O nome do produto final, marca,o logotipo, imagem, composição da embalagem e o design  da embalagem não infringem os princípios da Shariah, por exemplo a marca não faz alusão a elementos Haram, como bacon, presunto, cerveja, ou qualquer outro que possa confundir o consumidor. (DT 7.10 - Item 6.11). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.11. A partir de uma avaliação geral documental do processo, pelo auditor religioso, é possível verificar o respeito as regras islâmicas em toda a fase da cadeia de produção (recebimento, preparação, embalagem, rotulagem, armazenamento e transporte) dos itens certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.10 – Item 6.12) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.12. Todos os tipos de produtos de limpeza, ou manutenção, tais como detergentes, sanitizantes, graxas e lubrificantes, foram previamente informados e aprovados pela FAMBRAS HALAL, e possuem documentações que demonstram que estes são livres de componentes ou materiais não-halal (proibidos para a Shariah). (DT 7.10 – Item 6.14) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.13. Os componentes dos materiais de embalagens e invólucros estão incluídos na listas positivas de Componentes para Materiais, Embalagens em Contato com Alimentos, e caso exista restrição para uso ou critério de pureza, os mesmos são respeitados. Além disto, possuem apenas substâncias permitidas, dentro dos  limites máximos estabelecidos pela legislação vigente e cumprem com os limites de migração (DT 7.10 – Item 6.15) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.14. As embalagens são resistentes a impactos e no caso de embalagens multicamadas existem controles de residuais de solventes (DT 7.10 - Item 6.16). |  |  |  |  |  | Mencionar o laudo de resistencia avaliado, e no caso de embalagem multicamada o laudo de controles residuais. | 
 | 1.15. As características de permeabilidade das embalagens são adequadas aos materiais embalados (DT 7.10 - Item 6.17). |  |  |  |  |  | Mencionar o laudo de permeabildiade | 
 | 1.16. As embalagens dos materiais de embalagem e invólucros, como paletes, filmes e recipientes, são feitos de materiais adequados, limpos e não contaminam a embalagem dos alimentos produzidas. Em casos em que  exista um potencial risco para a contaminação dos alimentos devido a migração, é feito controle para prevenção (DT 7.10 - Item 6.18) |  |  |  |  |  | Mencionar as fichas técnicas dos produtos e laudo de migração se aplicável | 
 | 2. REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA A FABRICAÇÃO DOS MATERIAIS DE EMBALAGEM HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), o registro de qualificação e treinamento dos colaboradores (DT 7.10 - Item 7.2.1 e item 11). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2. Avaliação apenas para unidades que fabricam produtos Halal e não Halal (DT 7.10 - Item 7.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2.1. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), a obrigatoriedade de limpeza dos equipamentos antes do início da produção Halal, bem como a validação deste procedimento (DT 7.10 - Item 7.2.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2.2. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que a higienização dos equipamentos não deve ser realizada alternadamente entre equipamentos que processam Halal e equipamentos que processam produtos não Halal (Ex. Realizar um CIP alternado entre equipamentos que processam Halal e não Halal). (DT 7.10 - Item 7.2.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.3. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que se as linhas de produção já tiverem sido utilizadas para fabricar produtos de origem suína, antes de converte-la para fabricação de produtos Halal é necessário que a mesma tenha passado por um ritual de purificação, o qual consiste em submeter os equipamentos a passagem de água + agentes de limpeza, como detergente e sanitizante, no mínimo 7 vezes, para que ocorra a remoção completa de cor, sabor e aroma do contaminante,  (DT 7.10 - Item 7.3). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.4. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que a conversão das linhas entre produção Halal e suína não deve ser realizada de forma contínua. (DT 7.10 - Item 7.3.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.5.    Caso a empresa fabrique produtos Halal que contém carne, gelatina ou outros elementos de origem animal (proveniente de animais abatidos), ou identificados como alto risco para o Halal, existe um supervisor,muçulmano e competente, da FAMBRAS HALAL trabalhando no local durante a produção Halal (DT 7.10 – Item 7.4 e 7.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.6.    A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que todos os equipamentos, linhas de produção, ferramentas, utensílios e materiais associados utilizados na fabricação dos materiais de embalagem e invólucros  devem ser limpos e não devem ser fabricados ou conter qualquer tipo de material não Halal (DT 7.10 – Item 7.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3. REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA ARMAZENAGEM E TRANSPORTE |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1.    A empresa está ciente e documenta no Sistema de garantia Halal (HAS) que todos as embalagens e invólucros certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL devem estar identificados (sendo suficiente ter o selo visível na embalagem secundária do produto Halal), e conforme aplicabilidade, separados de outros produtos não Halal durante o armazenamento e transporte, para prevenir que estes sejam misturados com produtos não Halal. (DT 7.10 – Item 8.1 e 8.2 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2. A empresa documenta no Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) a avaliação dos perigos e o procedimento adequado, com as medidas que devem ser praticadas, por exemplo em caso de rompimento/ vazamento de embalagens de produtos não Halal no armazém de produto final, para assegurar a não contaminação cruzada do produto Halal. (DT 7.10 – Item 8.1.2 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.1 e 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3.    A empresa está ciente e documenta no Sistema de garantia Halal (HAS) que é proibido transportar junto produtos Halal e produtos de origem suína e/ ou seus derivados, para evitar contaminação cruzada com produtos Haram (DT 7.10 – Item 8.3 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.4. O procedimento de transporte da empresa prevê os controles adequados para segurança Halal, evitando a contaminação cruzada com produtos não Halal, e o atendimento das condições de higiene e integridade do material. (DT 7.10 – Item 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.   REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA QUALIDADE E SEGURANÇA |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.   A sistemática de Boas Práticas de Fabricação contém no mínimo (DT 7.10 – Item 10.5): |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.1.   Manual de Boas Práticas de Fabricação e/ ou Manual da Qualidade |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | a) Higienização das instalações, equipamentos, móveis e utensílios; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | b) Controle de água; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | c) Higiene e saúde dos manipuladores; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | d) Manejo dos resíduos; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | e) Manutenção preventiva e calibração de equipamentos; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | f) Controle integrado de vetores e pragas; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | g) Seleção das matérias-primas (componentes) e atividades subcontratadas; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | h) Controle de acesso; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | i) Qualidade do ar e ventilação, se aplicável; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | j) Reprocesso: Controle e rastreabilidade |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2. A empresa possui um programa de rastreabilidade do produto Halal. (DT 7.10 – Item 10.5 e DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.7 e 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. O programa de recolhimento da empresa, contempla a comunicação com a certificadora Halal, e preve a necessidade de realizá-lo mediante contaminação que desabone o status Halal do produto. (DT 7.9 – Item 10.5 e 10.8) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.    A empresa possui um programa de Análise de Perigos e Pontos de Controle Críticos (APPCC), contemplando controle de quebra de vidro, ceramica, plástico ou qualquer elemento pontiagudo, que pode ser proveniente do processo. (DT 7.10 – Item 10.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. A empresa possui uma metodologia de tratamento de desvios, reclamações e não-conformidades (referentes a auditorias internas e externas), incluindo ações corretivas eficazes, adotadas para evitar ocorrências futuras. (DT 7.10 – Item 10.7 e 10.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.    SISTEMA DE GARANTIA HALAL/ HALAL ASSURANCE SYSTEM (HAS) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1.  A empresa possui um Sistema de Garantia Halal documentado de acordo com a condição interna da empresa, sendo este desenvolvido em um formato compatível com o sistema de gestão da qualidade, e de forma a garantir o status Halal dos produtos certificados (DT 7.10 - Item 10.1 e DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 e 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2.  A empresa estabelece uma política Halal (seja ela interligada ou não com outra política da empresa) que indique o seu compromisso com a produção de produtos Halal, estando esta devidamente assinada pela alta direção da empresa. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.1, 7.1 e 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3.  Existe uma equipe responsável pela Gestão Halal, sendo esta apresentada no Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ – HAS), onde estão descritas as devidas responsabilidades e autonomias. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.2, 8.1 e 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4.  A equipe é composta pelas autoridades necessárias (incluindo o supervisor Halal, se aplicável), que estão diretamente envolvidas no processo de produção Halal, e possui autonomia para assegurar a condição Halal dos produtos e responder pelos apontamentos encontrados durante as auditorias (DT 7.3 – Item 8.2 e 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5. A empresa possui um planejamento para realização de treinamentos Halal programados para ocorrer no mínimo uma vez ao ano, e os mesmos são obrigatorios para todas as pessoas envolvidas no processo Halal, incluindo novos funcionários. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 e 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. O conteúdo dos treinamentos Halal contemplam o conceito Halal, Haram e Mashbooh, mercado que a empresa pretende atingir com a certificação, e controles internos estabelecidos para garantir a certificação. Ainda, para os  funcionários envolvidos no monitoramento dos PCCH, os treinamentoss incluem a importância da sua atividade diante da segurança do Halal. (DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 e 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. O documento de registro do Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) descreve a composição e o fluxograma de cada um dos produtos certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.4, 10.1 e 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8. Os Perigos e Pontos Críticos de Controle Halal são identificados, validados e registrados no HAS da empresa, através de uma matriz de risco que proporciona a análise da probabilidade de ocorrência do perigo versus a severidade da ocorrência do perigo, levando em consideração a contaminação cruzada com produtos Haram em cada etapa do fluxograma. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 e 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. O Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) elaborado pela empresa prevê a necessidade de separação completa entre as matérias primas e produtos Halal e não Halal, bem como sua identificação, em toda e qualquer etapa do fluxo de fabricação (recebimento, preparação, embalagem, rotulagem, armazenamento e transporte), a fim de evitar qualquer contaminação cruzada (DT 7.10 - Item 7.1, 7.2.4 e 7.2.9 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10. As medidas preventivas para a ocorrência do risco e as medidas corretivas em caso de desvios estão claramente definidas no HAS da empresa. Bem como as tratativas a serem adotadas para gestão dos Pontos Críticos de Controle Halal estão bem documentadas, incluindo os registros dos monitoramentos, os responsáveis e as ações no caso de desvios.  (DT 7.3 – Item 11.4 e 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. A empresa descreve no documento de registro do Sistema de Garantia Halal uma metodologia para auditoria interna HAS. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.6 e 12.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12. A auditoria interna do HAS está prevista (para o primeiro ano de certificação) ou foi realizada, pelo menos uma vez ao ano, ou mais vezes, se necessário. (DT 7.3 – Item 12.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
=== SHEET: ESCOPO AUDITADO (dimensoes: A1:E142) ===

=== PAGINA 1/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:1 de 37 
 
1. OBJECTIVE 
This document establishes the general requirements for Halal certification, carried 
out by FAMBRAS HALAL, for category F (GSO 2055-2 and SMIIC 02) referring to 
distribution and G (GSO 2055-2 and SMIIC 02) referring to warehouses and transportation. 
 
 
2. APPLICATION FIELD  
This document is applicable to all distribution companies, for category F (GSO 2055-
2 and SMIIC 02) and to all warehouses and transportation companies, for category G (GSO 
2055-2 and SMIIC 02), enabled by FAMBRAS HALAL. 
 
 
3. REFERENCE DOCUMENTS 
• GSO 2055-1:2015 – HALAL products - Part one General Requirements for HALAL 
Food. Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf; 
• GSO 323:1994 - General requirements for transportation and storage of chilled and 
frozen foods. Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf; 
• GSO 05/FDS 986:2014 - Frozen Chickens. Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) 
– Gulf; 
• GSO 2469/2021 - Halal foods - Management system requirements for warehousing 
and related activities. Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf; 
• GSO 2468/2021 - Halal foods - Management system requirements for transportation 
of goods and/or cargo chain services. Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – 
Gulf; 
• GSO 2470/2021 - Halal foods - Management system requirements for retailing. Gulf 
Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf; 
• GSO 996:2021 - Livestock Meat Chilled and Frozen 
• MS 1500:2019 - HALAL Food – JAKIM Malaysia; 
• MS 1500:2009 HALAL food - production, preparation, handling and storage - general 
guidelines. JAKIM Maylasian Standard – Malasya;

FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO

• KEPKABAN BPJPH - Number 20: 2023 - Decree of the Head of Halal Product 
Assurance Organizing Agency – Indonesia.  
• KMA BPJPH Number 748: 2021 - Products that must be certified Halal – Indonesia; 
• MUIS-HC-S001:2005 - General Guidelines for the Handling and Processing of Halal 
Food - Majlis Ugama Islam Singapura (Islamic Religious Council of Singapore); 
• MS 2400-1:2018 - Halal supply chain management system - Part 1 - Transportation; 
• MS 2400-2:2018 - Halal supply chain management system - Part 2 -Warehousing; 
• MS 2400-3:2018 - Halal supply chain management system - Part 3 – Retailing; 
• MS 2565:2014 - Halal Packaging - General Guidelines. 
• OIC/ SMIIC 1:2019 General Requirements for Halal Food; 
• IT 4.1 Instrução para o Uso do Selo de Garantia Halal FAMBRAS; 
• ABNT ISO/TS 22002-1:2012 - Programa de pré-requisitos na segurança de 
alimentos. Parte 1: Processamento industrial de alimentos; 
• Brasil - DECRETO Nº 9.013, DE 29 DE MARÇO DE 2017; 
• Brasil - DECRETO-LEI Nº 5.452, DE 1º DE MAIO DE 1943; 
• Brasil - INSTRUÇÃO NORMATIVA Nº 61, DE 24 DE DEZEMBRO DE 2018; 
 
 
4. GENERAL PROVISIONS 
All warehouses, establishments of transportation and distribution approved by 
FAMBRAS HALAL, that aim to obtains the Halal certification by FAMBRAS HALAL for 
categories F and G (GSO 2055-2 and SMIIC 02), shall comply with the requirements 
established in this document for storage and other involved operations. 
In this document, the word “shall” indicates a requirement and the word “should”, a 
recommendation.  
For the purposes of this certification scheme, the word company refers to the 
warehouse, or distribution center, or transport company that is/aims for Halal certification. 
 
 
 
 


=== PAGINA 3/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:3 de 37 
 
5. DEFINITIONS 
 
 
Shariah  
The word Shariah (Islamic jurisprudence) refers to the totality of the orders of Allah 
(God) that regulate all aspects of the life of a Muslim (MS 1900:2005), meaning, everything 
that is described in the holy Quran, that aside the God's revelations to Prophet Muhammad 
(SAWS). In addition to the Qur'an, the Sunnah, i.e. all practices and sayings of the Prophet 
Muhammad (SAW) are also part of the Shariah. 
 
 
Halal 
The word "Halal" means lawful, the same as allowed. Designated "Halal" products 
are those whose consumption is permitted by God.  
Halal is then the basis of all that is lawful, in feed, in the social, in the acts practiced 
(conduct), in justice, in the clothes, in the finances, etc., is a system based on principles and 
values that benefit humanity. 
The Holy Quran teaches us in Sura 5a, verse 4: "All the Healthful Things have been 
allowed to you.” 
 
 
Halal Slaughter (Tazkeya) 
Halal slaughter is an Islamic ritual that should be done quickly, so it does not allow 
the animal suffering, in which the trachea, esophagus and both arteries and jugular vein 
should be cut to hasten the bleeding and the animal’s death.  
The slaughter ritual shall be practiced ethically and with respect for the animals to be 
slaughtered, following the Islamic laws of the Holy Quran. One of them consists of the 
Tasmiyah, which consist in invoking the phrase Bismillah, Allahu Akbar (In the name of God, 
God is the greatest), immediately before the slaughter. Bismillah is one of the ways to seek 
God's help before any action to be performed by a Muslim. There is no text in Islamic 
jurisprudence that confirms exactly the meaning of the Tasmiyah, but Islamic scholars claim 
that it would be a way of thanking God for the food offered by Him, since the sacrifice of 
animals is performed to ensure the sustenance of human beings and not for fun or sadism. 
 
 


=== PAGINA 4/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:4 de 37 
 
 
Haram  
The word Haram means illicit, forbidden, impure, illegal. Refers to anything and action 
that is unacceptable, harmful to the health of the body and moral, as determined in the Holy 
Quran and Hadith (confirmation of the acts, affirmations, opinions and ways of life of the 
Prophet Muhammad (may Allaah's blessing be upon he). 
 
 Mashbouh 
There is a large number of industrialized products that use additives and ingredients 
that can bring dangers to spiritual and physical health, as they belong to the Mashbouh 
(questionable/ doubtful) category, which are: 
Elements that can be from numerous sources of extraction, such as: 
a) Glycerin which may be of vegetable or animal origin; 
b) L-Cysteine a non-essential amino acid can be obtained from human hair, pork, 
duck feather, oil and from vegetable. 
Processed products of Halal origin, for example:  
a) Clarified fruit juice with pork;  
b) Products such as yogurt, biscuit, cereal bar, that uses gelatin and mono and 
diglycerides; 
c) Flavours with alcohol; 
d) Cheeses, monosodium glutamate (flavor enhancer) and other products 
fermented with enzymes; 
e) Among others.  
  
 
Makruh 
Inappropriate. Not recommended (encouraged) according to Shariah. 
 
 
Najasah (Impurities)  
Najasah means everything that is considered impure, such as feces, urine, prostatic 
fluid, a discharge from the urethra, sperm, pus, vomit, and excrement of any animal whose 
meat is not allowed for consumption.  
 
 


=== PAGINA 5/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:5 de 37 
 
 
Warehouse  
An animal products warehouse is an establishment intended for the reception, 
storage and dispatch of products of animal origin, edible or non-edible, which need or do 
not need to be preserved by the use of industrial cold, with specific facilities for carrying out 
reinspection. 
 
 
 
Standard Operating Hygiene Procedure – SOHP  
These procedures are described, developed, implemented, monitored and verified 
by the establishment, in order to establish the routine way in which the establishment 
avoids direct or cross contamination of the product and preserves its quality and 
integrity, through hygiene, before, during and after operations. 
 
 FIFO/ FEFO 
FIFO means “First in First Out”. 
FEFO, on the other hand, means "First-Expire, First-Out". 
Both are techniques for managing the arrangement and shipping of goods from a 
stock, taking into account their entry/ validity period. 
 
 Threat 
Susceptibility or exposure to a food defense act (such as sabotage, malicious 
tampering, disgruntled employee, terrorist act, etc.) which is regarded as a gap or 
deficiency that could impact consumer health if not addressed. 
 
 Halal Mizen (weight / measure) 
Islam is concerned with protecting consumer rights by seeking to obtain the correct 
product measure and / or weight. Thus, any practice or process applied ineffectively, 
constitutes a fraudulent act. The holy Quran mentions: “Woe to those who give less [than 
due], Who, when they take a measure from people, take in full. But if they give by measure 
or by weight to them, they cause loss.” (Surat 83: 1-3). 
Considering this, when also talk about commercialization, should transpose the same 
concept of justice that is so strongly defended in Islam, as it is in the holy Quran: “O my 


=== PAGINA 6/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:6 de 37 
 
people, give full measure and weight in justice and do not deprive the people of their due 
and do not commit abuse on the earth, spreading corruption.” (Surat 11:85). 
 
 Halal Certificate 
It is a faithful guarantee document issued by a Halal certifying institution, recognized 
by Islamic countries, to attest that the company, process and products, follow the legal 
requirements and criteria determined by Shariah. 
 
 Identification of the categories GSO 
The certified products are classified according to the categories presented in table 1 
– Gulf standard, according to GSO 2055-2. 
The Gulf countries referenced in this document are those that are part of the GCC 
(GCC Accreditation Center) and follow the standards established by the GSO. They are: 
Saudi Arabia, Oman, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Yemen and the United Arab Emirates.  
FAMBRAS Halal may operate in all categories indicated in the table, however, for the 
Gulf countries the recognition is applicable only for the categories marked in blue. For other 
categories, upon demand and necessity, FAMBRAS HALAL may request recognition.

b "Farm packaging" means packaging without product or its processing modification. 
c "Secondary packaging" means packaging without product or its processing modification and without primary packaging modification.

=== PAGINA 9/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:9 de 37 
 
 
 Identification of the categories SMIIC 
Certified products are classified according to the categories presented in table 2 - 
SMIIC 02:2019 standard. 
SMIIC stands for Standards and Metrology Institute for Islamic Countries, being an 
intergovernmental organization, that aims to establish common standards to be 
implemented throughout the region of OIC - Organization of the Islamic Cooperation, which 
aims to ensure consumer protection and interoperability of products, and also to strengthen 
the market position of OIC member states in the global market. 
The countries that are part of OIC are: Afghanistan, Albania, Azerbaijan, Bangladesh, 
United Arab Emirates, Brunei, Darussalam, Algeria, Indonesia, Morocco, Palestine, Gabon, 
Iraq, Iran, Cameroon, Qatar, Kyrgyzstan, Kuwait, Libya, Lebanon, Maldives, Malaysia, 
Egypt, Nigeria, Oman, Uzbekistan, Pakistan, Senegal, Somalia, Sudan, Syria, Saudi Arabia, 
Tajikistan, Tunisia, Turkey, Turkmenistan, Uganda, Jordan, Yemen, Bosnia and 
Herzegovina.

=== PAGINA 12/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:12 de 37 
 
 
6. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR WAREHOUSING, TRANSPORTATION AND 
DISTRIBUTION OF HALAL PRODUCTS, GOODS AND CARGO   
 
 
Warehouses, transportation and distribution companies shall demonstrate 
compliance with legal issues applicable to the country and region; 
 
 
No manipulation, fractionation or repackaging work will be allowed, in accordance 
with DECREE No. 9.013/ 2017; 
 
 
All non-Halal products shall be completely separated from Halal products within 
the warehouse, in the means of transport and distribution operation. Non-Halal 
elements are shown in Table 3; 
In the table of attachment 1 are listed some Halal critical points that shall be 
considered, for each product type.  
 
 
The company shall visibly identify Halal products, in order to guarantee their 
differentiation from non-Halal products. 
 
 
The company shall incorporate safety measures, during receipt, cargo formation 
and dispatch, to ensure that the Halal product does not mix with non-Halal 
products, in order to avoid cross contamination, or to avoid that the products may 
be mistaken. 
 
 
Respect for Islamic rules shall be present in all stages of the warehousing 
operation flow, transportation and distribution. 
 
 
The official authorities may take all necessary procedures to verify the conformity 
of products with the special requirements of Halal products and may take 
appropriate measures in accordance with local legislation. 
 
 
Warehouses/ distribution center shall not exceed the maximum legally authorized 
storage capacity;

FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE,

Table 3. Prohibited Origins for Halal 
Origin 
Non-Halal 
Animals: 
Animals not slaughtered in Halal way - according to Islamic rules;  
Animals slaughtered by polytheists, irreligious, secular, atheists, spells, apostasy 
and other unbelievers except by Christians and Jews  
Dead animals naturally; Stifled animals; Fatally beaten animals (Mawqouza); 
Animals killed due to fall (Motaradiah); Animals killed by butthead (Nateehah);  
Animals eaten by raptor animals; Animals in whose slaughter the name of God 
(Allah) is not mentioned; Animals slaughtered by idolatry; 
Pig; Domestic donkeys; Mules; Elephants; Monkeys; Puppies; Foxes, cats and 
similars. 
Predators such as prey animals and similars, such as lions, cheetahs, bears, 
except hyenas. 
Birds of prey with sharp claws used to attack and similars, such as eagles, 
hawks, sparrows, peregrines, parrots and owls. 
Rodents, reptiles, pests and similars, such as rats, centipedes, serpents, snakes, 
varanus, lizards, geckos, chameleons, hedgehogs, martens, bats, squirrels, 
bulls, mole and beetles (except dabb lizards and jerboas). 
Scorpions, all kinds of insects, worms and animals that are forbidden by law to 
be killed, such as ants, bees, woodpeckers and sparrows (or boubela / hoopoes), 
except for locusts and inevitable parts of bees like honey. 
Repulsive creatures and similars, such as shells, larvae and other similar 
animals. 
Animals fed with unclean items unless they are confined and fed in the last three 
days with food allowed by Islamic Rules.  
Aquatic 
Toxic animals or harmful species unless toxic substances or harmful substances 
are removed during preparation. 
Plants 
Plants and their products / toxic parts, harmful derivatives or narcotics, unless 
the new substance is removed during preparation.  
Beverages 
Intoxicating beverages or those containing alcohols, narcotic, soothing, toxic or 
harmful substances. 
GMO 
All elements produced by genetic modification from forbidden species. 
Blood and blood 
products from 
animals or humans  
Blood and all its derivatives are prohibited, as are all body fluids of human or 
animal origin, such as vomiting and urine. 
Food additives 
All toxic, harmful or calming additives and their derivatives. Products and their 
derivatives of non-halal materials. 
Enzymes 
All enzymes derived from non-Halal sources. 
Microorganisms 
Microorganisms such as germs, fungi, toxins and harmful yeast produced in non-
Halal environments or using non-Halal ingredients. 
Source: Translation of GSO 2055-1. 
 
 
 


=== PAGINA 14/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:14 de 37 
 
 
 
Warehouses/ distribution center shall notify FAMBRAS HALAL of any significant 
change in their facilities, such as expansion and increase in maximum storage 
capacity; 
 
 When mounting loads, it is not allowed to mix Halal loads with non-Halal loads, 
and due care shall be taken to ensure that no cross contamination occurs; 
 
 All surfaces that come into contact with Halal products, including tables, tools and 
utensils used to collect samples and quality/ safety tests shall not present a risk of 
cross contamination with non-Halal products.  
6.11.1. All surfaces, tools, and utensils, including thermometers, shall not be made 
of toxic, harmful to health, or non-halal materials, such as bone, horns, 
nails and teeth. 
6.11.2. All surfaces that come into contact with Halal products, tools and utensils 
used to collect samples even to check the internal temperature of Halal 
products shall be clean and sanitized in the contact moment with of Halal 
products;  
6.11.3. All performed sanitizations, related to Halal, shall be verified, monitored 
and validated; 
6.11.4. The tools and utensils for sampling and for verification of the internal 
temperature of Halal products shall not be shared with the sampling of 
swine products; 
6.11.5. Detergents, sanitizers, and water used to sanitize all surfaces that come 
into contact with Halal products, including tables, tools and utensils used 
to collect samples and check temperature, shall be free of non-Halal 
components or materials (prohibited by Shariah). 
6.11.6. All types of cleaning products shall be previously informed and approved 
by FAMBRAS HALAL. 
 
 Storage locations and transport compartments shall be clean, dry, well ventilated, 
protected from dust, condensation, smoke, odors or other sources of 
contamination. 


=== PAGINA 15/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:15 de 37 
 
 
 Warehouses and means of transport shall guarantee the integrity of the products, 
so that the hygienic-sanitary and quality conditions are not compromised, that is, 
that the products do not deteriorate or suffer losses or damages during the periods 
of storage or in transit in containers. 
 
 It shall be ensured that adequate and safe facilities are provided, when 
appropriate, to the nature of the products, goods and/ or cargo to be stored in 
accordance with the requirements of the sender and/ or the recipient. 
 
 Warehouses shall provide adequate means to handle the entry and exit of 
products, goods and/ or cargo to maintain the integrity of Halal principles, for 
example, considering risks of cross contamination in the antechambers. 
 
 The Halal product weight indicated on the product package cannot be less than 
the its real weight. 
6.16.1. The company shall carry out sample tests to measure the Halal products 
weights, comparing the weight described on the package, with the real 
weight;  
6.16.2. In the case of non-conformity products, where the actual weight is less than 
the weight indicated on the package, there shall be a segregation, where 
the product will be condemned for commercialization as a Halal product and 
the manufacturer will be notified; 
6.16.3. Daily records of these conferences shall exist. 
 
NOTE: Although the product already comes from the manufacturer properly weighed, 
with this information on the label, this second weight conference in the warehouse is 
important to meet the Halal Mizen (check 5.12) concept. 
 
 Distribution companies/ warehouse/ transportation shall ensure that the product 
reaches to the final correct destination, in a timely manner, in the proper quantity, 
unscathed physically and considering food and Halal safety. 
 
 


=== PAGINA 16/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:16 de 37 
 
 
7. RECEIPT AND SHIPMENT OF HALAL PRODUCTS, GOODS AND CARGO 
 
 
Halal loads, when received, shall be identified to allow adequate signalling and 
segregation of non-Halal products;  
 
 
The company shall ensure that adequate insurance has been taken out, according 
to the nature of the goods transported, meeting the requirements of the sender and 
of the receiver. 
 
 
Loading and unloading operations shall be carried out as soon as possible, to 
ensure that the product's temperature is not affected. 
 
 
8. TRANSPORTATION OF HALAL PRODUCTS, GOODS AND CARGO 
 
 
Adequate means of transport shall be used to avoid mixing Halal with non-Halal 
products, in addition to meeting hygiene conditions. 
 
 
There shall be no mixing of different animal species during transport; 
8.2.1. It is expressly forbidden to transport Halal and swine products and/ or their 
derivatives together. 
 
 
In the case of transporting refrigerated and frozen products, the means of transport 
and storage of cargo shall be designed for this type of operation, maintaining the 
appropriate temperature, including a device that allows the temperature to be read. 
 
 
All surfaces of the vehicle that may be in contact with Halal product packaging shall 
be sanitized, free from rust and cracks. 
 
 
In case of loss or damage to the goods during transportation, the company shall 
document the occurrence and ensure that the owner of the goods is informed. 
 


=== PAGINA 17/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:17 de 37 
 
 
9. 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR QUALITY AND SAFETY OF HALAL 
PRODUCTS, GOODS AND CARGO 
 
 
The company shall have an efficient system for traceability of packaged Halal 
products, that is able to distinguish the goods that enter the manufacturing stages 
from the suppliers to the distribution routes and their point of arrival. 
9.1.1. The company shall define a retention period for traceability records, which shall 
comply 
with 
statutory 
and 
regulatory 
requirements, 
and 
customer 
requirements. 
 
 
The layout of the facilities shall allow: Protection of the product against cross 
contamination, between and during operations; Sanitation and maintenance; 
minimize the risk of mixing products, raw materials, and packaging materials. 
 
 
The layout of the facilities shall include the following items: identification of places 
with potential risk of Halal products contamination; process and employees flow; 
identification of receiving, storing and dispatching locations; identification of places 
used by employees; and identification of quarantine areas, pest control and waste 
disposal stations. 
 
 
There shall be documentation with all relevant information of the storage, transport, 
and distribution system, including loading information, loading sequence, 
scheduled delivery times, follow-up documentation, product identification, 
identification of those responsible for each step of the process, tracking of the 
product and shipping details. 
9.4.1. The comany shall ensure that all details of the agreements entered into 
concerning the processing, loading, storage of goods, insurance and shipment 
of goods are available to the person responsible for carrying out this service. 
 
 
The application of specific control systems for stock rotation, such as FIFO / FEFO, 
in storage shall be observed. 
 


=== PAGINA 18/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:18 de 37 
 
 
 
The company shall provide effective control of temperature, humidity, pressure, 
and other environmental conditions, as required by the product or storage 
specifications. 
 
 
Aspects of the Facilities:  
9.7.1. The establishment should not be located in areas close to industrial activities 
that present a serious threat; areas subject to flooding; pest infestation areas; 
and areas where waste, solid or liquid, cannot be removed effectively. 
9.7.2. Buildings shall be designed, built, and maintained in a manner appropriate to 
the type of processing operations to be performed, the food safety hazards 
associated with these operations and the potential sources of contamination in 
the various building environments. Buildings shall be durable so it does not 
represent a danger to the stored products and shall allow proper maintenance 
and cleaning. 
9.7.3. The establishment shall be located in a limited area, sufficient for circulation 
and flow of transport vehicles, in addition to paved patios and circulation paths, 
in good condition and clean; 
9.7.4. The area destined for loading the container shall have an external cover that 
prevents the entry of rainwater and the entry of animals; 
9.7.5. The walls of the cold storage chambers shall be clear, intact and allow 
adequate hygiene; 
9.7.6. The floor shall be constructed with impermeable, smooth, non-slip, resistant 
material, which allows an adequate cleaning, and there are also drains that are 
easy to clean and siphoned; 
9.7.7. Access to warehouse facilities is via a sanitary barrier; 
9.7.8. Windows, doors and other openings shall be constructed and protected in order 
to prevent the entry of vectors and pests to prevent the accumulation of dirt; 
9.7.9. The company used for operations of agricultural interest that are chilled or 
frozen shall have air-conditioned facilities: 
 With dimensions compatible with cargo handling;  
 With temperature and humidity compatible with technical, hygienic 
and sanitary conservation conditions; 


=== PAGINA 19/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:19 de 37 
 
 
 There shall be an adequate air flow; 
 The refrigerated and cold chambers shall be equipped with 
thermometers and hygrometers whose display is placed outside the 
room, so that temperature and humidity readings can be taken without 
the need to open the door; 
 There shall be records of temperature and humidity control devices, 
which shall be kept as a reference for a sufficient period; 
9.7.10. The floors and drainage system shall be properly designed to allow adequate 
drainage of water and waste, in order to avoid the risk of product 
contamination.  
9.7.11. There shall be an area segregated from the food warehouse, from that 
destined for the storage of waste materials and chemicals (cleaning products, 
lubricants, and pesticides). 
9.7.12. The piping and connections shall be designed in such a way that condensation 
or leakage that may contaminate the goods or products does not occur. 
9.7.13. There shall be an adequate supply of potable water with adequate facilities for 
its storage, distribution, and temperature control. The non-potable water 
system shall be separated, identified, and shall not connect or allow backflow 
with the potable water system. 
9.7.14. The warehouse ventilation system shall be designed so that the air flow does 
not go from the contaminated areas to the clean areas. 
9.7.15. The lighting of the premises shall be adequate to allow that the operations are 
carried out in accordance with the requirements for Halal. If the lighting 
equipment has a breakage risk, it shall have a protection system to ensure that 
products, goods, or cargo are not contaminated. 
 
 
The equipment shall be durable, mobile and capable of being disassembled to 
allow inspection, maintenance, cleaning and disinfection, avoiding contamination 
of goods and/or cargo. 
 
 
Warehouses shall have: 
9.9.1. Standard Procedures for Operational Hygiene (SPOH) and monitoring. 


=== PAGINA 20/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:20 de 37 
 
 
9.9.2. Procedure for the health, hygiene, clothing and conduct of employees. 
9.9.3. Pest and vector control. 
9.9.4. People access control. 
9.9.5. Handling deviations, complaints, and non-conformities methodologies. 
 The handling methodology, which shall be registered, shall include the 
analysis of deviations, complaints and non-conformities, and the 
determination of their cause; the corrective action taken and the result 
of that action. 
 Nonconformities from previous process/ audits shall be resolved, and 
evidence presented whenever requested. 
9.9.6. Recall procedure. 
 The recall procedure shall predict the notification to interested parties 
(statutory and regulatory authorities, customers and/or consumers); 
handling of removed goods and/or loads, as well as those that are still 
in stock; and the sequence of actions to be taken; 
9.9.7. Maintenance and calibration procedure. 
9.9.8. Procedures for waste disposal. 
 A waste collection system outside of the organization's premises 
should be established to prevent the accumulation of waste. 
 Liquid waste shall be treated so as not to pose a threat to the 
environment. 
9.9.9. Procedure for managing purchased materials, including cleaning products. 
9.9.10. Procedure for emergencies that may result in contamination. 
 
 All employees who are subjected to work in an unhealthy environment, due to 
facilities that generate cold or heat, shall wear appropriate clothing to work in such 
conditions. 
 
 The company shall have programs to prevent, control and detect cross-
contamination. Measures to prevent physical, microbiological or allergenic 
contamination shall be included. 
 


=== PAGINA 21/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:21 de 37 
 
 
 Cold rooms, antechambers, freezing tunnels and cooling and freezing equipment 
shall be kept in adequate hygienic conditions 
 
 The mechanisms used for gauging weight, temperature, humidity and air flow are 
properly gauged and calibrated; by a company with adequate competence for this, 
duly proven through the INMETRO recognition certificate; 
 
 There shall be bands painted on the floor, indicating the position of the pallets, 
packages and necessary passages,  
 
 Corridors shall not be used as food storage areas; 
 
 The packaging of Halal products shall be checked upon receipt for consistency 
and temperature of the product and measured to ensure that they comply with the 
standard storage temperature; 
 
 The packages shall be stacked in regular rows on shelves or pallets and in 
quantities that do not exceed the capacity of the warehouse; 
 
 When products are stacked, it is recommended that attention is given to the 
necessary measures to protect the lower layers; 
 
 Products shall not be stored in direct contact with the floor or walls; 
 
 Food shall not be stored with other non-food products; 
 
 Products that emit strong specific odors shall not be stored in the same place with 
other products; 
 
 Cold rooms and refrigerators normally used to store frozen food shall not be used 
to freeze food. In this case, the freezing process shall be carried out in special 
freezers designed mainly for this purpose; 


=== PAGINA 22/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:22 de 37 
 
 
 Frozen foods that have been defrosted shall never be refrozen and shall not be 
kept in refrigerated conditions; 
 
 A separate area or other means shall be provided to segregate materials identified 
as non-compliant; 
 
 Gasoline or diesel-powered stackers cannot be used in areas where ingredients 
or food products are stored. 
 
 Vehicles, conveyor belts and containers: 
9.26.1. Vehicles, conveyor belts and storage containers shall be kept in an 
appropriate state of conservation and cleanliness, and conditions consistent 
with the requirements established in the relevant specifications. 
9.26.2. Vehicles, conveyor belts and storage containers shall provide protection 
against damage or contamination of the product. Temperature and humidity 
control shall be applied and documented wherever required by the 
organization. 
9.26.3. When the same vehicles, conveyor belts and containers are used for food 
and non-food products, or for Halal and non-Halal products, cleaning between 
loads shall be carried out. 
9.26.4. Bulk shipping containers shall only be used with food. When required by the 
organization, bulk shipping containers shall be dedicated to specified 
material. 
 
 Each establishment may assess the hazard of threat to products posed by 
potential acts of sabotage, fraud, vandalism or terrorism and shall take adequate 
and proportional protective measures protective measures. 
9.27.1. Potentially sensitive areas within the establishment may be identified, mapped, 
and subjected to access control. When doable, access should be physically 
restricted by use of locks, electronic card key or alternative systems. 
 
 If the company outsources any process that interferes with Halal requirements, the 


=== PAGINA 23/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO

Pág.:23 de 37 
 
 
organization shall ensure control of this process in accordance with the Halal 
Management System. 
9.28.1. The evaluation and selection of third party service providers/service 
providers/subcontractors shall be based on their ability to meet Halal 
requirements, and records of this evaluation and selection shall be 
maintained. 
 
 
10. 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR HALAL SECURITY - HALAL GUARANTEE 
SYSTEM 
 
 The company shall develop a program, or procedure, or code for the security of 
the Halal product. The Halal Assurance System documentation can be 
incorporated into the existing Management System documentation as long as it is 
clearly described and complies with the requirements for Halal. 
In the table of attachment 1 are listed some Halal critical points that shall be 
considered, for each product type.  
 
 Halal management Committee 
10.2.1. Top management shall appoint a Halal Committee and the description of 
this team shall be documented in the unit's Halal procedure, as well as the 
definition of their tasks and responsibilities, to ensure the effective operation 
of the Halal risk management, and the integrity of Halal within the 
warehouse. 
10.2.2. Halal Committee members shall have adequate knowledge and training 
regarding the Halal and non-Halal processes, products and services 
aspects, and risk management techniques.  
10.2.3. The company's top management shall provide all necessary resources 
identified by the Halal management team to ensure the Halal status of 
certified products. 
10.2.4. The Halal committee shall develop, document, maintain and review and 
communicate Halal risks in the process, and shall have knowledge and 
experience regarding to the organization's processes, and to the Halal 


=== PAGINA 24/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:24 de 37 
 
 
principles and their application. 
10.2.5. Halal committee shall control, identify, record, and communicate any issues 
related to operations that impact the Halal Management System. 
 The Halal committee shall carry out a continuous and effective review 
of the Halal Management System, keeping it updated in accordance 
with the actual changes in the organization's operations, human 
resources, Halal system and any internal and/or external changes that 
affect Halal operations. The management review, containing the 
appropriate improvements, shall be recorded, maintained, and sent to 
the Halal Committee. 
10.2.6. The company's top management shall ensure that the Halal Management 
team leader is a qualified person with knowledge and experience in Halal 
and non-Halal operations, products and/or services aspects, and risk 
management techniques. The Halal Committee leader shall have Islamic 
knowledge relevant to the work experience. 
 The Halal Committee leader's responsibilities include: 
a. Ensure that the Halal Management System is established, 
implemented, and maintained in accordance with the Halal 
requirements; 
b. Organize and coordinate the Committee's activities; 
c. Initiate corrective actions and process controls, including recall 
procedures until the deviation or unsatisfactory condition related 
to Halal requirements has been corrected; 
d. Start preventive actions to avoid the recurrence that will affect the 
Halal status; 
e. Develop the report on the adequacy and effectiveness of the Halal 
Management System for management review, including the 
results of the recall procedure. 
 In case of Halal Committee leader absence, the organization shall 
immediately appoint another person to ensure that the Halal 
Management System is maintained.

FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8

Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:25 de 37 
 
 
 Halal Policy 
10.3.1. The company shall define and document a policy that defines its 
commitment to Halal's integrity; 
10.3.2. It shall be ensured that the policy related to the Halal objective is understood, 
implemented and maintained at all levels of the organization; 
10.3.3. The Halal policy shall be signed by senior management, who shall have a 
clear knowledge of the Halal Management System scope, making sure that 
the Halal policy adopted is in accordance with Halal requirements.  
Note. There is no need to develop an exclusive Halal policy, so the Halal items 
defined by the top management can be registered in another interlinked policy, 
for example, quality policy. 
 
 Halal Scope 
10.4.1. The company shall clearly define what the Halal scope is, that is, what are 
the Halal products that the company stores. This scope shall be described 
in the Halal procedure; 
 The company should file a copy of the data sheet of the Halal products 
stored/transported 
10.4.2. The company shall point out a process flow chart for the Halal scope, 
including the sequency and interaction of all operation stages, exit and entry 
of goods, and Halal control points. The flowchart shall be verified by the 
Halal Committee. 
 
 Halal risk analysis 
10.5.1. The company shall establish documented procedures and a Halal risk 
management plan to ensure consistency and compliance with Halal 
requirements, the organization's Halal policy and the objective of the Halal 
principles. 
10.5.2. The company shall develop a risk analysis for the integrity of Halal products 
during the flow of storage and transportation, and if necessary, it shall 
change existing processes that negatively affect the safety of Halal products 
commercialized. When developing the risk analysis, the following aspects of 


=== PAGINA 26/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:26 de 37 
 
 
the products that will be stored and transported shall be considered: 
 Biological, chemical, and physical characteristics; 
 List of ingredients, including additives and processing aids, 
considering their origin; 
 Processing, packaging, and labeling method; 
 Storage and handling conditions, and shelf life;  
 Appropriate specifications for storage services.  
10.5.3. For each process step, the company shall identify the existing hazards for 
Halal, and determine the risk from a matrix of probability x severity. For all 
risks, control measures shall be established. The matrix model for risk 
analysis is set out in Annex 1; 
10.5.4. The risk analysis shall describe all the measures incorporated to guarantee 
this security, for example:  
• Halal products are stored in cold rooms different from those used to 
store non-Halal products; 
• All Halal product pallets are labelled with such information; 
• insertion of codes in the traceability system that are used to identify Halal 
products; 
• Halal pickings (order picking and preparation) are identified; 
• Risk of sabotage and/or fraud of the Halal product, by introducing a Haram 
element in the Halal final products, or even replacing the Halal product by 
a Haram product. 
10.5.5. For critical Halal control points, defined based on risk, it is necessary to 
establish criteria, monitoring, corrective actions, and verifications.  
10.5.6. Preventive measures, when the risk occurs, and corrective measures in 
case of deviations shall be clearly defined.  
10.5.7. The procedures to be adopted for the management of Halal Critical Control 
Points shall be clearly defined and well documented, including records 
involving monitoring, those responsible and actions in the case of 
deviations. 
10.5.8. The company shall adopt a Halal risk analysis validation program to ensure 
that the definition of Halal critical points is complete and correct, and that the 


=== PAGINA 27/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:27 de 37 
 
 
control measures and corrective actions taken are effective. 
10.5.9. The embedded control measures shall be monitored, in order to maintain 
the integrity of the Halal products and treat a possible contamination in a 
timely manner to prevent it from reaching the end user. 
10.5.10. The monitoring of control measures aims to confirm that they are being 
correctly applied and shall contain what is being monitored and its location, 
the method and frequency of monitoring, the person responsible and the 
monitoring result, which shall be recorded. When monitoring results indicate 
the ineffectiveness of control measures, appropriate adjustments shall be 
done before a deviation occurs, and these shall be recorded.  
10.5.11. Halal risk analysis verifications, to confirm that the results of the control 
measures are satisfactory, shall be recorded and maintained, and shall be 
carried out by qualified personnel. Verification records shall contain what is 
being verified, the method and frequency of verification, the person 
responsible and a periodic audit. Among the items to be checked, the 
following are applied: 
 Evaluation and review of the Halal risk management plan in 
accordance with the applicable legislation; 
 Evaluation of control measures; 
 Analysis of devolutions received; 
 Processes and layout compliance with what is documented; 
 Analysis of complaints received, mainly regarding to the Halal 
products safety.  
10.5.12. The risk analysis shall be updated at specific intervals to ensure its continuity 
relevance and effectiveness in the Halal risk management process. 
 
 Internal audit 
10.6.1. The company shall establish and maintain documented procedures to 
conduct audits that ensure the integrity of Halal in the warehouse / 
warehouse, and monitor the Halal security program 
10.6.2. The internal audit shall be carried out by a competent and independent 
internal auditor. 


=== PAGINA 28/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:28 de 37 
 
 
10.6.3. The internal audit shall be carried out at least once a year, or more times, if 
necessary. 
10.6.4. The internal audit report shall be delivered to all sectors involved. 
10.6.5. In case of findings, corrective actions shall be applied within a certain limit 
of time, in order to avoid repeated occurrences in the future. 
 
 Training 
10.7.1. Training on the Halal Assurance System (HAS) shall be mandatory for those 
involved in the Halal process. 
10.7.2. The company shall incorporate, in the Halal Assurance System (HAS), the 
training of all people involved in the critical activities of the Halal process, 
including new employees. 
10.7.3. Trainings can be internal or external and must be scheduled and take place 
at least once a year, or more often if necessary. 
Note: Training can be carried out by a competent employee who is in direct 
contact with the certifier, and understands the certification requirements 
from reading and understanding the certification scheme, for example. 
In case the training is carried out by an external consultant, this in turn must 
demonstrate proven competence in understanding the Halal certification 
requirements, for example, having a certificate of participation in Halal 
training. 
10.7.4. Training should promote competence in employees. 
10.7.5. The training shall cover the Halal, Haram and Mashbooh concept, the 
market that the company intends to reach with certification, and internal 
controls established to guarantee certification. 
10.7.6. The training of employees involved in monitoring PCCHs (if any) shall 
consider the importance of their activity in relation to Halal safety. 
10.7.7. The training record shall be kept, as well as evaluating its effectiveness. 
 
 Traceability 
10.8.1. The company shall have a procedure to ensure traceability of the Halal 
process/service. This procedure can be dedicated to Halal, mentioned in the 


=== PAGINA 29/37 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR WAREHOUSE, 
TRANSPORTATION AND DISTRIBUTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.8 
Rev.: 09 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:29 de 37 
 
 
Halal guarantee system or, if the company already has a procedure, just 
reference it. 
10.8.2. The traceability process shall allow the identification of the entire chain, 
including the manufacturer of the Halal product, and shipping details, as well 
as the respective quantities. 
10.8.3. Traceability shall include records related to Halal. 
 
 If the company is requested about a Halal product recall, FAMBRAS Halal shall be 
notified by email qualidade@fambrashalal.com.br. 
10.9.1. 
Depending on the recall cause, it may be necessary to define corrective 
measures to contain possible cross-contamination. 
 
 
11. DISCLOSURE OF HALAL CERTIFICATION 
 
 The certified company may use the FAMBRAS Halal seal in its materials, to 
publicize the Halal certification, as long as it complies with the instructions: IT 4.1 
FAMBRAS HALAL SEAL USE INSTRUCTION. 
11.1.1. 
Every media that apply the Halal seal, advertising materials, reports and 
email signatures, shall be previously approved by FAMBRAS HALAL. 
Note 1: To obtain this approval, it is mandatory that the client sends an e-mail 
to the auditor responsible for his/her process, with the materials arts that will 
apply the Halal seal, for evaluation and subsequent authorization of its use. 
 
 The company cannot apply the FAMBRAS Halal seal to stored products, as the 
responsibility for the label, is entirely with the manufacturer. 
 
 The warehouse's advertising materials shall not contain misleading information to 
the consumer, nor Haram elements, nor contradict the principles of Shariah.

Critical point for Halal 
Milk and dairy 
products, such as

others 
Origin of yeasts, enzymes and coagulants

Origin of milk - cannot come from non-halal animals 
Use of gelatin - it cannot be from pork, and if it is bovine it shall guarantee its Halal 
origin 
Other ingredients and process aids - shall be of permissible origin for Halal, for 
example calcium chloride and lactic acid. 
Cleaning products, especially enzymatic detergent  
 Packaging materials shall be suitable for Halal
