=== SHEET: Report - DETAILED (dimensoes: A2:I52) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO
  
AUDIT REPORT - WAREHOUSE, TRANSPORT AND DISTRIBUTION - (DT 7.8)
STAGE 1 - CATEGORIES F and G |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.11
Rev.: 06
Data: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAJOR | NC - MINOR | NOTE | NA | Evidence | 
 | 1.    GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR STORAGE, TRANSPORT AND DISTRIBUTION OF HALAL PRODUCTS, GOODS AND CARGO |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1. The company has its products in accordance with Halal requirements, and has a License of Functioning/Operation in accordance with the legal issues of the country / region. (DT 7.8 - Item 6.1 and 6.7). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2. From a general documental evaluation of the process, by the religious auditor, it is possible to verify the compliance with Islamic rules in all stages of the warehouse, transport and distribution operation flow (DT 7.8 – Item 6.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.3. The Halal procedure provides notification of FAMBRAS HALAL before of any significant changes of the facilities, such as expansions and changes in storage capacity (DT 7.8 - Item 6.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. There is a procedure for sample collection and verification of the internal temperature of products,  which provides measures so that there is no risk of cross contamination between Halal and non-Halal products, and that the sharing of utensils with pork products does not occur. (DT 7.8 - Item 6.11 and 6.11.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5. There are records that show the verification, monitoring and validation of the sanitation procedure adopted for utensils, instruments and surfaces that come into contact with the Halal product (DT 7.8 - Item 6.11.2 and 6.11.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6. All types of cleaning products, such as detergents and sanitizers, have been previously informed and approved by FAMBRAS HALAL, and have documentation that demonstrates that they are free of non-halal components or materials (prohibited by Shariah) (DT 7.8 – Item 6.11.5 and 6.11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.7. There is an adequate procedure for receiving, storing, dispatching and transporting Halal products, which ensures their integrity, and guarantees the hygienic-sanitary conditions and quality of the goods, so that there is no deterioration, cross contamination, loss or damage (DT 7.8 - Itens 6.13 and 6.15) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8. There are records that show the conformity of the actual weight of the product with the weight described on the package, in accordance with Halal Mizen. In cases of non-compliance (actual weight less than the declared weight), there is a procedure that provides the treatment to be adopted, including the segregation and condemnation of this product for Halal, and notification of the manufacturing company (DT 7.8 – Item 6.16) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. There are documentations that prove that distribution companies deliver the product to its correct final destination, in a timely manner, in the proper quantity, physically intact and that considers the safety of food and Halal (DT 7.8 – Item 6.17) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2. RECEIPT AND SHIPMENT OF HALAL PRODUCTS, GOODS AND CARGO |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1. There is some procedure/ guidance that determines the identification of Halal cargo during receipt. (DT 7.8 - Item 7.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2. There is documentation that proves that the warehouse has taken out the appropriate insurance, according to the nature of the transported goods, meeting the requirements of the sender and receiver (DT 7.8 - Item 7.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3. TRANSPORT OF HALAL PRODUCTS, GOODS AND CARGO |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1. There is an adequate procedure that foresees that the means of transport used avoid mixtures with non-Halal products and with different animal species, prohibit the transport of Halal products with products of pork origin and/or their derivatives, and satisfy hygiene conditions. (DT 7.8 - Items 8.1 and 8.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2. There is an adequate procedure that provides that the means of transport for refrigerated and frozen products are designed for this type of operation, with a device that allows the temperature reading, keeping it adequate at all times (DT 7.8 - Item 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3. The warehouse documents the loss or damage to the goods that occurred during transport, and there is a record of notification to the owner of the goods(DT 7.8 - Item 8.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR QUALITY AND SAFETY OF HALAL PRODUCTS, GOODS AND CARGO |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1. The company has an effective traceability procedure for Halal product control. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.1, 9.1.1 and 10.8 and DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.7 and 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2. The company has a procedure for controlling stock rotation. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. The company has a procedure that provides the control of temperature, humidity and pressure of the storage chambers, as well as the retention of these records. (DT 7.8 - Item  9.6 e 9.7.9.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4. From mandatory documents (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.1. Standard Operating Hygiene Procedure (SOHP). (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.2. Employees' health, hygiene, clothing and conduct procedure (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.3. Pest and vector control. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.4. People access control. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.5. The company has a methodology for handling deviations, complaints and non-conformities (referring to internal and external audits), including effective corrective actions adopted to prevent future occurrences. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.6. The company has a recall procedure, which includes communication with the Halal certifier, and foresees the need to carry it out in the event of contamination that undermines the Halal status of the product (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.6 and 10.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.7. Maintenance and calibration procedure. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.7) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.8. Waste disposal procedure (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.8) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.9. Procedure for managing purchased materials, including cleaning products (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.10. Procedure for emergencies that may result in contamination (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. There is a procedure to prevent, control and detect cross-contamination - physical, microbiological or allergenic. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.11) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.6. There are measures to ensure that the products, at the receiving stage, are in compliance with the standard storage temperature (DT 7.8 - Item 9.16) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.7. There is documentation of the evaluation and selection of outsourced service providers/service providers/subcontractors, who meet Halal requirements. When any process that interferes with Halal requirements is outsourced, the warehouse guarantees the control of this process in accordance with the Halal Management System (DT 7.8 - Item 9.28 e 9.28.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR HALAL SECURITY - HALAL ASSURANCE SYSTEM |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1. The company has a Halal Assurance System documented in accordance with the company's internal condition, which is developed in a format compatible with the quality management system, and in order to guarantee the Halal status of certified products (DT 7.8 - Item 10.1 and DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 and 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2. The company establishes a Halal policy (whether or not it is linked to another company policy) that indicates its commitment to the production of Halal products, which is duly signed by the company's top management. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.3 and DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.1, 7.1 and 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3. There is a team responsible for Halal Management, which is presented in the Halal Assurance System (SGQ – HAS), where the appropriate responsibilities and autonomy are described. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.2 and DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.2, 8.1 and 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4. The team is made up of the necessary authorities (including the Halal supervisor, if applicable), who are directly involved in the Halal production process, and has the autonomy to ensure the Halal condition of the products and respond to the notes found during the audits (DT 7.3 – Item 8.2 and 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5. The company has a plan to carry out Halal training scheduled to take place at least once a year, and they are mandatory for all people involved in the Halal process, including new employees. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.7.1, 10.7.2 and 10.7.3 and DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 and 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. The content of Halal training covers the Halal, Haram and Mashbooh concept, the market that the company intends to reach out with the certification, and internal controls established to guarantee certification. Furthermore, for employees involved in monitoring PCCHs, training includes the importance of their activity regarding the Halal safety. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.7.5 and 10.7.6 and DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 and 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. The Halal Assurance System (HAS) registration document describes the process flowchart for the Halal scope, including the sequence and interaction of all operation steps, output and input of goods, and Halal control points. The flowchart shall be verified by the Halal Committee. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.4 and DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.4, 10.1 and 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8. Halal Hazards and Critical Control Points are identified, validated and recorded in the company's HAS, through a risk matrix that provides analysis of the probability of the hazard occurring versus the severity of the hazard occurring, taking into account cross-contamination with Haram products at each stage of the flowchart. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.5 and DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 and 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. The Halal Assurance System (HAS) developed by the company provides the need for complete separation between raw materials and Halal and non-Halal products, as well as their identification, at each and every stage of the manufacturing flow (receiving, preparation, packaging, labeling , storage and transport), in order to avoid any cross-contamination (DT 7.8 - Item 6.3 and DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10. Preventative measures for the occurrence of risk and corrective measures in case of deviations are clearly defined in the company's HAS. As well as the procedures to be adopted for the management of Halal Critical Control Points are well documented, including monitoring records, those responsible and actions in the case of deviations. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.5.6 and 10.5.7 and DT 7.3 – Item 11.4 and 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. The company describes in the Halal Assurance System registration document a methodology for HAS internal audit. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.6.1 and DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.6 and 12.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12. The HAS internal audit is planned (for the first year of certification) or has been carried out, at least once a year, or more times, if necessary. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.6.3 and DT 7.3 – Item 12.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  |

=== SHEET: Relatório DETALHADO (dimensoes: A2:I52) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO
  
RELATÓRIO DE AUDITORIA – ARMAZÉM, TRANSPORTE E DISTRIBUIÇÃO_(DT 7.8)
ESTÁGIO 1 - CATEGORIAS F e G |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.11
Rev.: 06
Data: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAIOR | NC - MENOR | OBS | NA | Evidências | 
 | 1.    REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA ARMAZENAMENTO, TRANSPORTE E DISTRIBUIÇÃO DE PRODUTOS, MERCADORIAS E CARGAS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1. A empresa possui seus produtos em conformidade com os requisitos Halal e um alvará de funcionamento sanitário em conformidade com as questões legais do país/ região. (DT 7.8 - Item 6.1 e 6.7). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2. A partir de uma avaliação geral documental do processo, pelo auditor religioso, é possível verificar o respeito às regras islâmicas em todas as etapas do fluxo de funcionamento do armazém/ entreposto, transporte e distribuição (DT 7.8 – Item 6.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.3. O procedimento Halal prevê a notificação da FAMBRAS HALAL diante de quaisquer alterações significativas das instalações, como ampliações e mudanças na capacidade de armazenamento (DT 7.8 - Item 6.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. Existe um procedimento de coleta de amostras e verificação de temperatura interna dos produtos, que prevê medidas para que não ocorra contaminação cruzada entre os produtos Halal e não Halal, e para que não ocorra o compartilhamento de utensílios com produtos suínos (DT 7.8 - Item 6.11 e 6.11.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5. Existem registros que mostrem a verificação, monitoramento e validação do procedimento de higienização adotado para os utensílios, instrumentos e superfícies que entram em contato com o produto Halal (DT 7.8 - Item 6.11.2 e 6.11.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6. Todos os tipos de produtos de limpeza, tais como detergentes e sanitizantes, foram previamente informados e aprovados pela FAMBRAS HALAL, e possuem documentações que demonstram que estes são livres de componentes ou materiais não-halal (proibidos para a Shariah). (DT 7.8 – Item 6.11.5 e 6.11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.7. Existe um procedimento adequado para o recebimento, armazenamento, expedição e transporte dos produtos Halal, que zela pela sua integridade, e garante as condições higiênico-sanitárias e qualidade da mercadoria, de modo que não haja deterioração, contaminação cruzada, perda ou dano (DT 7.8 - Itens 6.13 e 6.15) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8. Existem registros que mostrem a conformidade do peso real do produto com o peso descrito na embalagem, atendendo ao Halal Mizen. Em casos de não conformidade (peso real menor que o peso declarado), existe um procedimento que prevê as tratavivas a serem adotadas, incluindo a segregação e condenação deste produto para o Halal,e a notificação da empresa fabricante (DT 7.8 – Item 6.16) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. Existem documentações que comprovem que as empresas de distribuição entregam o produto ao seu destino final correto, em tempo hábil, na quantidade adequada, íntegro fisicamente e que considera a segurança dos alimentos e do Halal.  (DT 7.8 – Item 6.17) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2. RECEBIMENTO E EXPEDIÇÃO DE PRODUTOS, MERCADORIAS E CARGAS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1. Existe algum procedimento/ orientação que determina a identificação das cargas Halal durante o recebimento. (DT 7.8 - Item 7.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2. Existem documentações que comprovem que o armazém/ entreposto contratou os seguros adequados, de acordo com a natureza das mercadorias transportadas, atendendo aos requisitos do remetente e do destinatário. (DT 7.8 - Item 7.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3. TRANSPORTE DE PRODUTOS, MERCADORIAS E CARGAS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1. Existe um procedimento adequado que prevê que os meios de transporte utilizados evitam misturas com produtos não Halal e com diferentes espécies animais, proibam o transporte de produtos Halal com produtos de origem suína e/ou seus derivados, e satisfaçam as condições de higiene. (DT 7.8 - Itens 8.1 e 8.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2. Existe um procedimento adequado que prevê que os meios de transporte destinados a produtos refrigerados e congelados são projetados para este tipo de operação, contando com um dispositivo que permita a leitura da temperatura, mantendo-a sempre adequada (DT 7.8 - Item 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3. O entreposto documenta as perdas ou danos às mercadorias ocorridas durante o transporte, e existe o registro da notificação ao proprietário da mercadoria. (DT 7.8 - Item 8.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4. REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA QUALIDADE E SEGURANÇA DE PRODUTOS, MERCADORIAS E CARGAS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1. A empresa possui um procedimento de rastreabilidade eficaz para controle dos produtos Halal (DT 7.8 - Item 9.1, 9.1.1 e 10.8 e DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.7 e 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2. A empresa possui um procedimento para controle de rotação de estoque. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. A empresa possui um procedimento que prevê o controle de temperatura, umidade e pressão das câmaras de armazenamento, bem como a retenção destes registros. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.6 e 9.7.9.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4. Dos documentos obrigatórios existentes (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.1. Procedimento Padrão de Higiene Operacional (PPHO). (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.2. Procedimento para a saúde, higiene, vestuário e conduta dos funcionários. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.3. Controle de pragas e vetores. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.4. Controle de acesso de pessoas. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.5. A empresa possui uma metodologia de tratamento de desvios, reclamações e não-conformidades (referentes a auditorias internas e externas), incluindo ações corretivas eficazes, adotadas para evitar ocorrências futuras. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.6. A empresa possui um programa de recall/ recolhimento, o qual contempla a comunicação com a certificadora Halal, e preve a necessidade de realizá-lo mediante contaminação que desabone o status Halal do produto (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.6 e 10.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.7. Procedimento de manutenção e calibração. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.7) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.8. Procedimento para descarte de resíduos (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.8) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.9. Procedimento para gestão de materiais adquiridos, incluindo produtos de limpeza (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.10. Procedimento para emergências que podem resultar em contaminação. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. Existe procedimento para prevenir, controlar e detectar contaminação cruzada - física, microbiológica ou por alergênicos.  (DT 7.8 - Item 9.11) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.6. Existem medidas para garantir que os produtos, na etapa de recebimento, estão em conformidade com a temperatura padrão de armazenamento (DT 7.8 - Item 9.16) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.7. Existe documentação da avaliação e seleção dos prestadores de serviço terceirizados/ prestadores de serviços/ subcontratados, os quais atendem aos requisitos Halal. Quando houver terceirização de algum processo que interfira nos requisitos do Halal, o armazém/ entreposto garante o controle deste processo de acordo com o Sistema de Gestão Halal. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.28 e 9.28.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5. REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA SEGURANÇA HALAL - SISTEMA DE GARANTIA HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1.  A empresa possui um Sistema de Garantia Halal documentado de acordo com a condição interna da empresa, sendo este desenvolvido em um formato compatível com o sistema de gestão da qualidade, e de forma a garantir o status Halal dos produtos certificados (DT 7.8 - Item 10.1 e DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 e 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2.  A empresa estabelece uma política Halal (seja ela interligada ou não com outra política da empresa) que indique o seu compromisso com a produção de produtos Halal, estando esta devidamente assinada pela alta direção da empresa. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.3 e DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.1, 7.1 e 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3.  Existe uma equipe responsável pela Gestão Halal, sendo esta apresentada no Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ – HAS), onde estão descritas as devidas responsabilidades e autonomias. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.2 e DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.2, 8.1 e 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4.  A equipe é composta pelas autoridades necessárias (incluindo o supervisor Halal, se aplicável), que estão diretamente envolvidas no processo de produção Halal, e possui autonomia para assegurar a condição Halal dos produtos e responder pelos apontamentos encontrados durante as auditorias (DT 7.3 – Item 8.2 e 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5. A empresa possui um planejamento para realização de treinamentos Halal programados para ocorrer no mínimo uma vez ao ano, e os mesmos são obrigatorios para todas as pessoas envolvidas no processo Halal, incluindo novos funcionários. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.7.1, 10.7.2 e 10.7.3 e DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 e 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. O conteúdo dos treinamentos Halal contemplam o conceito Halal, Haram e Mashbooh, mercado que a empresa pretende atingir com a certificação, e controles internos estabelecidos para garantir a certificação. Ainda, para os  funcionários envolvidos no monitoramento dos PCCH, os treinamentos incluem a importância da sua atividade diante da segurança do Halal. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.7.5 e 10.7.6 e DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 e 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. O documento de registro do Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) descreve o fluxograma de processo para o escopo Halal, incluindo a sequência e interação de todas as etapas da operação, saída e entrada dos bens, e pontos de controle Halal. O fluxograma deve ser verificado pelo Comitê Halal. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.4 e DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.4, 10.1 e 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8. Os Perigos e Pontos Críticos de Controle Halal são identificados, validados e registrados no HAS da empresa, através de uma matriz de risco que proporciona a análise da probabilidade de ocorrência do perigo versus a severidade da ocorrência do perigo, levando em consideração a contaminação cruzada com produtos Haram em cada etapa do fluxograma. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.5 e DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 e 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. O Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) elaborado pela empresa prevê a necessidade de separação completa entre as matérias primas e produtos Halal e não Halal, bem como sua identificação, em toda e qualquer etapa do fluxo de fabricação (recebimento, preparação, embalagem, rotulagem, armazenamento e transporte), a fim de evitar qualquer contaminação cruzada (DT 7.8 - Item 6.3 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10. As medidas preventivas para a ocorrência do risco e as medidas corretivas em caso de desvios estão claramente definidas no HAS da empresa. Bem como as tratativas a serem adotadas para gestão dos Pontos Críticos de Controle Halal estão bem documentadas, incluindo os registros dos monitoramentos, os responsáveis e as ações no caso de desvios.  (DT 7.8 - Item 10.5.6 e 10.5.7 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.4 e 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. A empresa descreve no documento de registro do Sistema de Garantia Halal uma metodologia para auditoria interna HAS. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.6.1 e DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.6 e 12.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12. A auditoria interna do HAS está prevista (para o primeiro ano de certificação) ou foi realizada, pelo menos uma vez ao ano, ou mais vezes, se necessário. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.6.3 e DT 7.3 – Item 12.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
=== SHEET: ESCOPO AUDITADO (dimensoes: A1:E142) ===

=== SHEET: Informe DETALLADO (dimensoes: A2:I52) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS CERTIFICACIÓN HALAL 
  
INFORME DE AUDITORIA – ALMACENAMIENTO, TRANSPORTE Y DISTRIBUCIÓN_(DT 7.8)
ETAPA 1 - CATEGORIAS F y G |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.11
Rev.: 06
Fecha: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAYOR | NC - MENOR | OBS | NA | Evidencias | 
 | 1.    REQUISITOS GENERALES PARA EL ALMACENAMIENTO, TRANSPORTE Y DISTRIBUCIÓN DE PRODUCTOS, MERCANCÍAS Y CARGAS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1. La empresa tiene sus productos conformes con los requisitos Halal y una licencia de funcionamiento sanitaria que se ajusta a las cuestiones legales del país/región. (DT 7.8 - Item 6.1 y 6.7). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2. A partir de una evaluación documental general del proceso por parte del auditor religioso, es posible verificar el cumplimiento de las normas islámicas en todas las etapas del flujo de trabajo del almacén/depósito, el transporte y la distribución (DT 7.8 – Item 6.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.3. El procedimiento Halal prevé que se notifique a FAMBRAS HALAL cualquier cambio significativo en las instalaciones, como ampliaciones y cambios en la capacidad de almacenamiento (DT 7.8 - Item 6.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. Existe un procedimiento para toma de muestras y comprobación de la temperatura interna de los productos, que incluye medidas para evitar la contaminación cruzada entre productos Halal y no Halal, y para garantizar que no se comparten utensilios con productos porcinos. (DT 7.8 - Item 6.11 y 6.11.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5. Existen registros que muestran la verificación, el seguimiento y la validación del procedimiento de higienización adoptado para los utensilios, instrumentos y superficies que entran en contacto con el producto Halal. (DT 7.8 - Item 6.11.2 y 6.11.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6. Todos los tipos de productos de limpieza, como detergentes y desinfectantes, han sido previamente informados y aprobados por FAMBRAS HALAL, y disponen de documentación que demuestra que están libres de componentes o materiales no halal (prohibidos por la ley islámica). (DT 7.8 – Item 6.11.5 y 6.11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.7. Existe un procedimiento adecuado de recepción, almacenamiento, expedición y transporte de los productos Halal, que asegura su integridad y garantiza las condiciones higiénico-sanitarias y la calidad de la mercancía, de forma que no se produzcan deterioros, contaminaciones cruzadas, pérdidas o daños. (DT 7.8 - Item 6.13 y 6.15) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8. Existen registros que demuestran que el peso real del producto se ajusta al peso descrito en el envase, de conformidad con el Halal Mizen. En caso de incumplimiento (peso real inferior al declarado), existe un procedimiento que prevé las medidas que deben adoptarse, incluida la segregación y el rechazo de este producto por Halal, y la notificación de la empresa fabricante. (DT 7.8 – Item 6.16) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. Existe documentación que demuestra que las empresas de distribución entregan el producto en su destino final correcto, a tiempo, en la cantidad adecuada, físicamente intacto y teniendo en cuenta la inocuidad alimentaria y Halal.  (DT 7.8 – Item 6.17) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2. RECEPCIÓN Y EXPEDICIÓN DE PRODUCTOS, MERCANCÍAS Y CARGAS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1. Existe algún procedimiento/lineamiento que determine la identificación de las cargas Halal durante la recepción. (DT 7.8 - Item 7.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2. Existe documentación que demuestra que el almacén/bodega ha contratado el seguro adecuado, según la naturaleza de las mercancías transportadas, cumpliendo los requisitos del remitente y del destinatario. (DT 7.8 - Item 7.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3. TRANSPORTE DE PRODUCTOS, MERCANCÍAS Y CARGAS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1. Existe un procedimiento adecuado para garantizar que los medios de transporte utilizados evitan la mezcla con productos no Halal y especies animales diferentes, prohíben el transporte de productos Halal con productos de origen porcino y/o sus derivados, y cumplen las condiciones de higiene. (DT 7.8 - Itens 8.1 y 8.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2. Existe un procedimiento adecuado que establece que los medios de transporte destinados a productos refrigerados y congelados están diseñados para este tipo de operaciones, con un dispositivo que permite la lectura de la temperatura, manteniéndola adecuada en todo momento (DT 7.8 - Item 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3. El almacén documenta cualquier pérdida o daño de la mercancía durante el transporte, y queda constancia de la notificación al propietario de la mercancía. (DT 7.8 - Item 8.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4. REQUISITOS GENERALES DE CALIDAD E INOCUIDAD DE LOS PRODUCTOS, MERCANCÍAS Y CARGAS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1. La empresa dispone de un procedimiento eficaz de trazabilidad para controlar los productos Halal (DT 7.8 - Item 9.1, 9.1.1 y 10.8 e DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.7 y 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2.La empresa dispone de un procedimiento de control de rotación de inventario. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. La empresa dispone de un procedimiento para controlar la temperatura, la humedad y la presión de las cámaras de almacenamiento, así como para conservar estos registros. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.6 y 9.7.9.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4. Documentos obligatorios existentes (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.1. Procedimiento estándar de higiene operativa. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.2. Procedimientos relativos a la salud, la higiene, la vestimenta y el comportamiento de los empleados. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.3. Control de plagas y vectores. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.4. Control de acceso de personas. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.5. La empresa dispone de una metodología para tratar las desviaciones, reclamaciones y no conformidades (relativas a auditorías internas y externas), incluidas las medidas correctivas eficaces adoptadas para evitar que se produzcan en el futuro. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.6. La empresa dispone de un programa de retirada, que incluye la comunicación con el certificador Halal, y prevé la necesidad de llevarla a cabo en caso de contaminación que desacredite el estatus Halal del producto. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.6 y 10.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.7. Procedimiento de mantenimiento y calibración. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.7) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.8. Procedimiento de eliminación de residuos (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.8) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.9. Procedimiento de gestión de los materiales adquiridos, incluidos los productos de limpieza (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4.10. Procedimiento para emergencias que puedan provocar contaminación. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.9.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. Existe un procedimiento para prevenir, controlar y detectar la contaminación cruzada, ya sea física, microbiológica o por alérgenos.  (DT 7.8 - Item 9.11) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.6. Existen medidas para garantizar que los productos, en la fase de recepción, se ajustan a la temperatura de almacenamiento estándar. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.16) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.7. Existe documentación sobre la evaluación y selección de proveedores de servicios subcontratados/tercerizados que cumplen los requisitos Halal. Cuando se externaliza cualquier proceso que interfiera con los requisitos Halal, el almacén se asegura de que este proceso se controla de acuerdo con el Sistema de Gestión Halal.. (DT 7.8 - Item 9.28 y 9.28.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5. REQUISITOS GENERALES DE SEGURIDAD HALAL - SISTEMA DE GARANTÍA HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1.  La empresa dispone de un Sistema de Garantía Halal documentado de acuerdo con las condiciones internas de la empresa, que se ha desarrollado en un formato compatible con el sistema de gestión de la calidad y de forma que garantice el estatus Halal de los productos certificados. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.1 y DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 y 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2.  La empresa establece una política Halal (vinculada o no a otra política de la empresa) que indica su compromiso con la fabricación de productos Halal, debidamente firmada por la alta dirección de la empresa (DT 7.8 - Item 10.3 y DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.1, 7.1 y 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3.   Existe un equipo responsable de la Gestión Halal, que se presenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS SGC), donde se describen las responsabilidades y autonomías correspondientes. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.2 y DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.2, 8.1 y 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4.  El equipo está formado por las autoridades necesarias (incluido el supervisor Halal, si aplica), que participan directamente en el proceso de producción Halal, y tiene autonomía para garantizar el estatus Halal de los productos y responder a los hallazgos encontradas durante las auditorías. (DT 7.3 – Item 8.2 y 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5. La empresa ha previsto un cronograma de capacitación Halal al menos una vez al año, obligatorios para todas las personas implicadas en el proceso Halal, incluidos los nuevos empleados.. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.7.1, 10.7.2 y 10.7.3 y DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 y 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. El contenido de los programas de formación Halal abarca el concepto de Halal, Haram y Mashbooh, el mercado al que la empresa pretende dirigirse con la certificación y los controles internos establecidos para garantizar la certificación. Además, para los empleados que participan en el control del PCCH, la formación incluye la importancia de su actividad en términos de seguridad Halal. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.7.5 y 10.7.6 y DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 y 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. El documento de registro del Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) describe el diagrama de flujo del proceso para el alcance Halal, incluida la secuencia e interacción de todas las fases de la operación, la salida y entrada de mercancías y los puntos de control Halal. El diagrama de flujo debe ser verificado por el Comité Halal. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.4 y DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.4, 10.1 y 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8. Los Peligros Halal y los Puntos Críticos de Control se identifican, validan y registran en el HAS de la empresa utilizando una matriz de riesgos que analiza la probabilidad de que se produzca el peligro frente a la gravedad de que se produzca, teniendo en cuenta la contaminación cruzada con productos Haram en cada etapa del diagrama de flujo. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.5 y DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 y 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. El Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) elaborado por la empresa estipula la necesidad de una separación completa entre materias primas y productos halal y no halal, así como su identificación, en todas y cada una de las fases del flujo de fabricación (recepción, preparación, envasado, etiquetado, almacenamiento y transporte), con el fin de evitar cualquier contaminación cruzada. (DT 7.8 - Item 6.3 y DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10. Las medidas preventivas para la ocurrencia de riesgos y las medidas correctivas en caso de desviaciones están claramente definidas en la HAS de la empresa. Los procedimientos que deben adoptarse para gestionar los puntos críticos de control Halal están bien documentados, incluidos los registros de seguimiento, los responsables y las acciones en caso de desviaciones.  (DT 7.8 - Item 10.5.6 y 10.5.7 y DT 7.3 – Item 11.4 y 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. La empresa describe en el documento de registro del Sistema de Garantía Halal una metodología para la auditoría interna de HAS. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.6.1 y DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.6 y 12.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12. La auditoría interna de HAS está prevista (para el primer año de certificación) o se ha realizado al menos una vez al año, o con mayor frecuencia si es necesario. (DT 7.8 - Item 10.6.3 y DT 7.3 – Item 12.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  |

=== PAGINA 1/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:1 de 40 
 
1. 
OBJECTIVE 
This document establishes the general requirements for farms of agricultural and 
animals’ activities, classified in category A (GSO 2055-2:2021 e SMIIC 02:2019 – animal 
farms) that certify Halal with FAMBRAS HALAL.  
 
 
2. 
APPLICATION FIELD 
This document is applicable to all farms of animal activities certified by FAMBRAS 
HALAL in category A (GSO 2055-2:2021 and SMIIC 02:2019 – animal farms). 
 
 
3. 
REFERENCE DOCUMENTS 
• 
GSO 2055-1:2015 – HALAL products - Part one General Requirements for 
HALAL Food. Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf; 
• 
GSO 2215:2012 - Good Agricultural Practices – Part 2:Farm. Gulf 
Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf; 
• 
KEPKABAN BPJPH - Number 20: 2023 - Decree of the Head of Halal Product 
Assurance Organizing Agency – Indonesia.  
• 
KEPKABAN BPJPH - Number 78: 2023 Decree on Guidelines for Halal Food and 
Beverage Certification with Management – Indonesia;  
• 
KMA BPJPH Number 748: 2021 - Products that must be certified Halal – 
Indonesia; 
• 
MS 1500:2019 HALAL food - production, preparation, handling and storage - 
general guidelines. JAKIM Malaysian Standard; 
• 
MS 1500:2009 HALAL food - production, preparation, handling and storage - 
general guidelines. JAKIM Maylasian Standard; 
• 
MUIS-HC-S001:2005 General Guidelines for the Handling and Processing of 
Halal Food. MUIS – Singapore  
• 
ISO/TS 22002-3:2011 - Prerequisite programs on food safety — Part 3: Farming; 
• 
OIC/ SMIIC 1:2019 General Requirements for Halal Food; 
 
 


=== PAGINA 2/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:2 de 40 
 
4. 
GENERAL PROVISIONS 
All farms of animal activities, category A (GSO 2055-2:2021 and SMIIC 02:2019 – 
animal farms) that aim to obtain the Halal certification of FAMBRAS HALAL must meet the 
requirements set forth in this document.  
The certification of the farm is linked to the products manufactured by it.  
The word shall in this document, indicates a requirement and the word “may or 
should” indicates a recommendation. 
 
 
5. 
DEFINITIONS 
 
5.1. 
Shariah  
The word Shariah (Islamic jurisprudence) refers to the totality of the orders of Allah 
(God) that regulate all aspects of the life of a Muslim (MS 1900:2005), meaning, everything 
that is described in the holy Quran, that aside the God's revelations to Prophet Muhammad 
(SAWS). In addition to the Qur'an, the Sunnah, i.e. all practices and sayings of the Prophet 
Muhammad (SAW) are also part of the Shariah. 
 
5.2. 
Halal 
The word "Halal" means lawful, the same as allowed. Halal products are those which 
consumption is allowed by God, according to Shariah.  
Halal is then the basis of all that is lawful, in food, in the social, in the acts practiced 
(conduct), in justice, in clothing, in finances, etc., it is a system based on the principles and 
values that benefit mankind.  
The Holy Quran teaches us in 5th Surah, 4th verse 4: "Lawful for you are all good 
foods.”.  
 
5.3. 
Halal Slaughter (Tazkeya) 
The Halal slaughter is an Islamic ritual of sticking that must be done quickly, so as 
not to allow the suffering of the animal, in which the trachea, the esophagus and both arteries 
and the jugular vein should be cut to speed the bleeding and the death of the animal.  
The slaughter ritual must be practiced ethically and with respect for the animals to be 


=== PAGINA 3/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:3 de 40 
 
slaughtered, following the Islamic laws of the Holy Quran. One of them consists of the 
Tasmiyah, which consist in invoking the phrase Bismillah, Allahu Akbar (In the name of God, 
God is the greatest), immediately before the slaughter. Bismillah is one of the ways to seek 
God's help before any action to be performed by a Muslim. There is no text in Islamic 
jurisprudence that confirms exactly the meaning of the Tasmiyah, but Islamic scholars claim 
that it would be a way of thanking God for the food offered by Him, since the sacrifice of 
animals is performed to ensure the sustenance of human beings and not for fun or sadism. 
 
5.4. 
Haram  
The word Haram means illicit, impure, illegal. It refers to what is unacceptable, 
harmful to the health of the human body and its morals, as determined in the Qur’an and 
Hadith (confirmation of the acts, affirmations, opinions, and way of life of Prophet 
Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him). 
 
5.5. Mashbouh 
There are a large number of industrialized products that use additives and ingredients 
that can bring dangers to spiritual and material health because they belong to the category 
Mashbouh (questionable/doubtful), which are: 
Elements that can be from numerous extraction sources, such as: 
a) Glycerin which may be of plant or animal origin; 
b) L-cysteine a non-essential amino acid can be obtained from human hair, pork, 
duck feather, oil and vegetable. 
Processed products of Halal origin, for example:  
a) Clarified fruit juice with pork;  
b) Products such as yogurt, biscuit, cereal bar, that uses gelatin and mono and 
diglycerides; 
c) Flavours with alcohol; 
d) Cheeses, monosodium glutamate (flavor enhancer) and other products 
fermented with enzymes; 
e) Among others.  
 
 


=== PAGINA 4/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:4 de 40 
 
5.6. 
Makruh 
Inappropriate. Not recommended (encouraged) according to Shariah. 
 
 
5.7. 
Najasah (Impurities)  
Najasah means everything that is considered impure, such as feces, urine, prostatic 
fluid, a discharge of the urethra, sperm, pus, vomiting, and excrement of any animal whose 
meat is not allowed for consumption.  
 
5.8. 
Halal Mizen (weight/ measurement) 
Islam cares about the protection of consumer rights by seeking to obtain the right 
measure and/or weight in the product. This way, any practice or process applied 
ineffectively, configures fraudulent act. The holy Quran mentions: "Woe unto fraudsters, 
those who, when one measures them something, require the exact/full measure. But when 
they measure or weigh on others, they defraud them." (Sura 83:1-3).  
With this in mind, when we also talk about commercialization, we should transpose 
the same concept of justice that is so strongly defended in Islam, as it is in the holy Quran: 
"Oh my people, have measure and weight with equity; do not defraud humans in their goods, 
and do not practice debauchery on earth as corruptors." (Sura 11:85). 
 
5.9. 
Halal Certificate 
It is a faithful guarantee document issued by a Halal certifying institution, recognized 
by Islamic countries, to attest that the company, process and products, follow the legal 
requirements and criteria determined by Shariah. 
 
5.10. Genetically modified organisms (GMO) 
Organisms obtained by transferring the gene from other living species to a plant, 
animal or microorganism by genetic engineering technologies, thus altering the natural 
characteristics of that organism. 
 
5.11. Identification of the categories GSO 
The certified products are classified according to the categories presented in table 1 
– Gulf standard, according to GSO 2055-2.

The Gulf countries referenced in this document are those that are part of the GCC 
(GCC Accreditation Center) and follow the standards established by the GSO. They are: 
Saudi Arabia, Oman, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Yemen and the United Arab Emirates.  
FAMBRAS Halal may operate in all categories indicated in the table, however, for the 
Gulf countries the recognition is applicable only for the categories marked in blue. For other 
categories, upon demand and necessity, FAMBRAS HALAL may request recognition.

5.12. Identification of the categories SMIIC 
Certified products are classified according to the categories presented in table 2 - 
SMIIC 02:2019 standard. 
SMIIC stands for Standards and Metrology Institute for Islamic Countries, being an 
intergovernmental organization, that aims to establish common standards to be 
implemented throughout the region of OIC - Organization of the Islamic Cooperation, which 
aims to ensure consumer protection and interoperability of products, and also to strengthen 
the market position of OIC member states in the global market. 
The countries that are part of OIC are: Afghanistan, Albania, Azerbaijan, Bangladesh, 
United Arab Emirates, Brunei, Darussalam, Algeria, Indonesia, Morocco, Palestine, Gabon, 
Iraq, Iran, Cameroon, Qatar, Kyrgyzstan, Kuwait, Libya, Lebanon, Maldives, Malaysia, 
Egypt, Nigeria, Oman, Uzbekistan, Pakistan, Senegal, Somalia, Sudan, Syria, Saudi Arabia, 
Tajikistan, Tunisia, Turkey, Turkmenistan, Uganda, Jordan, Yemen, Bosnia and 
Herzegovina.

=== PAGINA 11/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:11 de 40 
 
 
5.13. Clean Water 
Water that does not compromise the food safety in the circumstances of its use. 
Note: The term refers to natural water or purified that’s not contain microorganisms, 
harmful substances in quantities capable of directly or indirectly food safety. 
 
5.14.  Competent person 
Person qualified by knowledge and practical experience, with the skill and capacity 
necessary to execute a task assigned to them. 
Note: Education, training or experience is how a person achieves competence. 
 
5.15. Contamination  
Introduction or occurrence of a contaminant in food, feed or food and feeds 
environment. 
 
5.16. Contaminants 
Any biological or chemicals agent, foreign matter or another substance not 
intentionally added to food or feed, that come compromise the food safety. 
Note 1: In this context, the term “foreign matter’ ,refers to physical contaminants. 
Note 2: This definition it’s similar to definition of “danger to the safety foods”, identified 
during the dangers analyses, after the establishment of prerequisites program. 
 
5.17. Food  
Any single or multiple material, be processed, semi-processed or raw, intended 
directly for food-producing animals. 
 
5.18. Feed additive 
Any added ingredient intentionally, not normally consumed as food, having or not 
nutritional value, that affects the characteristics of food or products of animal origin. 
 
5.19. Feed ingredients 
Component ,part or constituent of any combination or mixes that makes up a food, 
having or not nutritional value in animal diet, including food additives. 
Note: The ingredients can be vegetable or animal origin, can be terrestrial or aquatic, 


=== PAGINA 12/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:12 de 40 
 
 
or even, other organics or inorganic substances. 
 
5.20. Batch 
Set of unities of a product that was produced, processed and packaged of similar 
circumstances. 
Note 1: The batch is determined to parameters previously established by the 
organization. 
 
5.21. Drug ration 
Any food that contains veterinary medications. 
 
5.22. Organization  
Group of people and facilities with an arrangement of responsibilities, authorities 
and relationships. For example, a company, corporation, firm, institution,charity, sole 
trader, association or parties or combination of some of them. The term refers to the 
farmer, a group of farmers, agricultural company or an association, an authority or a 
processing company that establishes PRPs for farmers. An organization can be public or 
private. 
 
5.23. Packaging 
Any product to be used for containment, protection, handling, delivery, storage, 
transport and presentation of agricultural or food products, such as example, wraps and 
containers. The term packaging refers to the action of placing a agricultural product or food 
in one or more packaging items. 
 
5.24. Pests 
Unwanted species of plants or animals that may have a detrimental effect on The 
plants and animals be come  
 
5.25. Veterinary drug 
Any substance applied or administered to any food-producing animal, such as meat 
or milk-producing animals, poultry, fish or bees, whether used for therapeutic, prophylactic 


=== PAGINA 13/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:13 de 40 
 
 
or diagnostic purposes or for modification of physiological functions or behaviour. 
 
5.26. Withholding period 
Time during which a crop, an animal or its products cannot be used for human 
consumption following the last application of a plant protection product to the crop (including 
pastures), or the last application or administration of a veterinary drug to the animal, that 
ensures that the foodstuff does not contain any residues in quantities in excess of 
established maximum residue limits. 
 
6. 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR THE MANUFACTURE OF HALAL PRODUCTS 
 
6.1. 
The farm shall demonstrate compliance with the legal issues applicable to the 
country and region. 
 
6.2. 
The farm shall define which activities are practiced and have a system have a 
system for establishing and maintaining appropriate documentation and records 
for each production unit or each site. 
 
6.3. 
There shall be a map/ layout/plant of the farm, which identifies the areas destined 
for the management of healthy, sick and dead animals, and when applicable, the 
milking and egg collection areas, which guides the correct paths to be followed. 
 
6.4. 
The farm shall ensure that food products are not subject to contamination, whether 
by waste, people, water or equipment that may constitute sources of 
contamination. The farm shall ensure that control measures are in place to reduce 
the likelihood of contamination. 
Note 1. To guarantee this requirement, the farm can rely on a Good Agricultural 
Practices program/manual. 
 
6.5. 
The farm shall not produce any non-Halal item, as described in table 3. 
In the table of attachment 1 are listed some Halal critical points that shall be 
considered, for each product type.

Table 3. Table of non-Halal products 
Origin 
Non-Halal 
Animals 
 The animals that may not be slaughtered according to Islamic Rules, the slaughtered 
animals of polytheists, irreligious, seculars, atheists, Magis, apostates and other 
disbelievers other than Christians and Jews, dead animals, suffocated animals, 
fatally beaten animals (Mawqouza), falling animals (Motaradiah), butted animals 
(Nateehah), animals eaten by beast of prey and animals on whose slaughtering the 
name of Allah is not mentioned, the animals slaughtered on idols, the animals 
slaughtered not in approach of Allah, or the animals contaminated with prohibited 
animals of harm, if eaten. 
 Pigs, domestic donkeys, mules, elephants, monkeys, dogs, foxes, cats and the like. 
 Predators such as fanged beasts of prey and the like such as lions, cheetahs, bears, 
except for hyenas. 
 Birds of prey with sharp claws used for attacking and the like such as eagles, falcons, 
hawks, sparrows, peregrines, kites and owls. 
  Rodents, reptiles, pests and the like such as mice, rats, centipedes, serpents, 
snakes, monitors, lizards, geckos, chameleons, hedgehogs, martins, bats, squirrels, 
polecats, moles and Coleoptera (except for dabb lizards and jerboas). 
  Scorpions and all types of insects, worms and animals prohibited to be killed by 
Rules and the like such as ants, bees, woodpeckers and hoopoes, except for locusts 
and unavoidable bee parts falling in honey. 
 Odious creatures and the like such as shells, larva and other similar animals. 
 Animals fed with unclean items, unless they are confined and fed with permissible 
feeding according to Islamic Rules for at least three days. 
Aquatic 
 Toxicant or harmful species, unless toxicant or harmful substances are removed 
during preparation. 
Plantation 
 Plants, their products and their toxicant, harmful or narcotic derivatives, unless 
toxicant or harmful substances are removed during preparation. 
Beverages 
 Intoxicating beverages or those containing alcohols, narcotic, calming, toxicant or 
harmful substances. 
GMO 
 All elements produced by genetic modification from a prohibited specie or more than 
one species, of them one specie is prohibited. 
Blood and Blood 
Derivatives from a 
Human or Animal 
Origin: 
  
 Blood and all its derivatives are prohibited and all body liquids from a human or 
animal origin such as vomiting and urine. 
Food Additives 
 All toxicant, harmful or calming additives, products and their products and derivatives 
or derived from non-Halal materials. 
Enzymes 
 All enzymes derived from non-Halal sources. 
Microorganisms 
 All microorganisms such as germs, fungi, toxicant and harmful yeast produced on 
non-Halal environments or using non-Halal ingredients. 
Source: GSO 2055-2 adapted.

6.6. 
It is not allowed to create pork in the certified Halal farm. 
 
6.7. 
It is not allowed to manufacture alcoholic beverages, such as cachaça, in the 
certified Halal farm; 
 
6.8. 
Farm facilities shall be designed and equipped in such a way as to prevent 
unwanted animals from entering the premises.  
 
6.9. 
Any equipment shall also be exempt/not to be constituted of animals such as nails, 
teeth and skin. 
 
6.10. The mark and the logo of the farm shall not infringe the principles of Shariah, for 
example, the mark cannot make reference to other religions or to Haram elements, 
such as bacon, beer or any other that may confuse the consumer. 
 
6.11. Respect for Islamic rules shall be considered throughout the farm activities and 
locations. 
 
6.12. The packaging used to store the products manufactured by the farm shall not be 
toxic, shall be suitable for food and shall be exempt from any non-Halal material 
described in table 3. 
 
6.13. All types of cleaning and maintenance products, such as detergents, sanitizers, 
greases and lubricants used in equipment that come into contact with the products, 
for example, milking machines, shall be free of components or Non-Halal materials 
(prohibited for Shariah), as described in Table 3.  
 
6.14. The water used at any stage of the process shall be free of non-Halal components 
or materials (prohibited for Shariah), as described in Table 3.  
Note. To observe the exemption of non-Halal contaminants in water, the products 
used in the treatment are evaluated. Ex. Origin of activated charcoal present in the 
filter.

6.15. The farm shall have a program/action plan that identifies strategies that reduce 
known risks and hazards, such as water pollution, environmental quality, soil 
consolidation, soil erosion, greenhouse gas emissions. 
 
6.16. The weight of the Halal product declared on the product packaging may not be 
less than its actual weight (see 5.8). 
6.16.1. The company shall carry out sample tests to measure the Halal products 
weight, comparing the weight described on the package with the actual 
weight;   
6.16.2. In case of non-conforming products, where the real weight is inferior to the 
weight of the package, there shall be a segregation, where the product will be 
condemned for commercialization as a Halal product. The company shall the 
correction and corrective action in cases of non-conformities;  
6.16.3. There shall be daily records of these conferences. 
 
 
7. CONSTRUCTION AND LAYOUT OF FACILITIES  
 
7.1. 
Farm facilities shall be designed and constructed to maintain an adequate degree 
of hygiene in order to minimize the likelihood of cross-contamination. 
Note: An example of cross-contamination is contamination between the effluent 
stream and the feed stream. 
 
7.2. 
Farm facilities shall allow the separation of batches or groups of animals, isolating 
sick or recently introduced animals, in order to prevent the introduction or spread 
of zoonotic diseases. 
 
7.3. 
Farm facilities shall provide adequate ventilation, lighting and cleaning to minimize 
exposure of food-producing animals and their products to contaminants and pests. 
 
7.4. 
There shall be restrooms and hand washing facilities in production, processing and 
maintenance areas.

8. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS - EMPLOYEES 
 
8.1. 
All employees of the farm (permanent and temporary) are duly legalised, and the 
company has an adequate management mechanism, which provides for the right 
of work, absence of child labour, safety and first aid in case of emergency.  
 
8.2. 
 The farm maintains a clear and updated list of all employees of the property 
(permanent and temporary), which includes at least: 
8.2.1. Name; 
8.2.2. Gender; 
8.2.3. Date of birth or age; 
8.2.4. Date of admission or period of the contract; 
8.2.5. Salary. 
 
8.3. 
The farm shall have an organization chart and indicate people named and 
competent to answer for: 
8.3.1. Certifications; 
8.3.2. Management of employees; 
8.3.3. Agricultural practices; 
8.3.4. Environment. 
 
8.4. 
The farm shall have a risk assessment for the health and safety of the working 
environment and all employees are trained/conscientized about the proper working 
conditions and use of PPE when necessary.  
 
8.5. 
No forced labour, slave or analogous, trafficking or other involuntary type is used 
in any stage of production and processing of the farm. Furthermore, the spouses 
and children of the workers are not obliged to work, unless they have been 
contracted separately and voluntarily. 
 
8.6. 
 Child labor is forbidden.  
8.6.1. In Brazil, it is considered a minimum age to work 16 years, except for the 
condition of apprenticeship from 14 years. 


=== PAGINA 18/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:18 de 40 
 
 
8.6.2. It is prohibited night work, dangerous or unhealthy to minors of eighteen, and 
of any work to minors under sixteen, except in the condition of apprenticeship, 
from fourteen years. 
8.6.3. Minors of 18 years old do not perform work that may impair their physical, 
mental or moral well-being. They are not exposed to any form of abuse and 
there is no evidence of trafficking or forced labor and connection to it.  
8.6.4. Children at the age of 14 to 16 years can perform light work, provided that 
these labors are not harmful to their health and development, do not interfere 
in their schooling or training and are performed under the supervision of an 
adult. 
 
8.7. 
The company cares to support the continuing education and literacy of workers 
living on site and their families.  
 
8.8. 
In the case of manual transport of cargo, the weight transported shall not exceed 
the safety and health requirements of the worker.  
 
8.9. 
The staff shall maintain an appropriate degree of personal hygiene, behave and 
operate in a manner that is appropriate for the degree of hygiene required. Such a 
situation shall be monitored and controlled by the farm management team, which 
shall have sufficient competence to implement the following requirements: 
a) Establish and communicate personal cleaning, behavior and operating 
practices that are appropriate to the operations performed. 
b) Maintain communication and competence of personnel (including 
temporary staff) who implement and maintain these practices. 
c) Where appropriate, maintain practices to ensure that visitors do not 
represent a source of contamination. 
 
8.10. Depending on the operations, and where appropriate, the company shall have 
certain controls implemented, which are designed for personnel, in order to 
minimize the likelihood of food contamination: 
a) Use of appropriate protective clothing, head covering and footwear that is 
cleaned or changed regularly; 


=== PAGINA 19/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:19 de 40 
 
 
b) Washing hands frequently (for example, after going to the bathroom, before 
handling food, milking or collecting eggs); 
c) Covering wounds on hands or forearms with suitable waterproof bandages; 
d) Prohibition of smoking in areas where food products are handled; 
e) Restriction on personal belongings brought into food handling areas; 
f)  Restriction of entry to the farm; 
g) Training in the use of chemical products (for example, pest control 
products); 
h) Training for specific activities (e.g. milking, harvesting fish, placing birds in 
boxes, handling eggs); 
i) Ensuring effective communication about hygiene practices, e.g. graphic 
display of hand washing technique. 
 
8.11. The farm shall include a description of applicable personal hygiene practices. 
 
8.12. The farm shall establish a system for accidents and emergencies, which all 
employees should be aware of. 
8.12.1. A list of the main emergency contacts, including police, hospital, ambulance 
and firefighters, shall be available; 
8.12.2. The farm shall have first aid kits, and people able to provide first aid. 
8.12.3. Hazardous areas, such as warehouses for flammable products, shall be 
marked with risk signs.  
 
8.13. There shall be adequate and clean premises for farm workers to store and 
consume food, which have access to potable water, clean water for washing 
hands, and sewage system toilets.  
 
 
9. 
ADEQUACY AND MAINTENANCE OF EQUIPMENT 
 
9.1. 
The equipment shall be maintained in good working conditions and be used in 
accordance with the conditions for which it was designed, thus minimizing the 
likelihood of contamination of food by pollutants originating from the equipment. 


=== PAGINA 20/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:20 de 40 
 
 
9.1.1. The farm shall follow the manufacturer's instructions use for the 
maintenance of the equipment that comes into contact with food. 
 
9.2. 
The company shall have some controls implemented depending on operations, 
and where appropriate, to minimize the likelihood of food contamination, such as: 
a) Use equipment that comes into contact with food (e.g. a milk tank) that is: 
• made from materials that do not increase the likelihood of chemical 
contamination of food; 
• designed to allow sanitary inspection, cleaning and, if necessary, 
disinfection; 
• designed to allow complete drainage and, where necessary, the 
prevention of post-disinfection contamination of the environment; 
b) Check, calibrate, maintain or replace equipment regularly and, in all cases, 
in accordance with the manufacturer's instructions; install and maintain 
washing facilities, soap and drying supplies or equipment in the immediate 
vicinity of the entrance with drinking water, or a disinfectant hand cleaner, 
where there is a likelihood of contamination of workers' hands or hand-borne 
food contamination. 
Note: Restrooms are an example of a facility where the risk of contaminating 
workers' hands is particularly high. The milking parlor is an example of a 
facility where the risk of contaminating food from workers' hands is 
particularly high. 
 
 
10. 
CLEANING 
 
10.1. Farm facilities shall maintain the degree of hygiene that is necessary to minimize 
the likelihood of food contamination. Shall maintain the cleanliness of surfaces of 
all facilities and equipment, including shipping containers, that may constitute a 
source of food contamination (e.g. food contact surfaces). Cleaning shall not result 
in food contamination and disinfection shall be effective to achieve the required 
degree of cleanliness. 


=== PAGINA 21/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:21 de 40 
 
 
10.2. The farm shall identify facilities and equipment that need to be cleaned, appoint 
competent personnel to carry out cleaning, and establish procedures for cleaning 
surfaces potentially in contact with food products. 
10.2.1. The procedure shall include the methodology and frequency of cleaning; 
10.2.2. The cleaning products used shall be foreseen by the procedure and duly 
identified; 
10.2.3. The procedure shall consider the quality of the water used to clean 
equipment, which comes into direct contact with the food handling surface. 
 
10.3. Depending on the operations, and when appropriate, the company shall have 
some controls implemented in order to minimize the likelihood of food 
contamination:  
a) Clean the milking equipment (suction lines and teats) after each milking and 
the milk storage containers after each emptying, when there is a probability 
of contamination of the milk by residues or biofilms;  
b) Rinse milking equipment and storage containers with drinking water when 
there is a likelihood of contamination with residues of detergents used for 
cleaning; 
c) Use clean water for disinfection activities;  
d) Clean equipment that may act as vectors of cross-contamination with 
chemical products; 
e) Clean and disinfect reusable egg packaging before use and after returning 
to the farm, in order to prevent egg contamination; 
f) Prevent containers, equipment and facilities that have been used for storing, 
transporting, mixing or spreading potentially hazardous material (e.g. 
medicated feed) from being reused for food or feed unless a cleaning 
procedure has been validated to be effective in removal of hazardous 
substances in the material have been applied; 
g) Clean and, if necessary, disinfect buildings, once all animals in the building 
in question have been moved to another breeding site or slaughterhouse, 
so as to ensure effective maintenance of an appropriate degree of hygiene 
and protection against transmission of animal diseases; 


=== PAGINA 22/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:22 de 40 
 
 
h) Prevent animals from being introduced into the premises during an 
appropriate drying period after cleaning or disinfection; 
i) Maintain feeders and automatic feeders at an adequate level of hygiene.  
 
 
11. 
WORKING ANIMALS 
 
11.1. Working animals used in farm activities shall not increase the likelihood of food 
contamination. Therefore, the farm shall identify and implement measures to 
minimize the likelihood of transferring contaminants from working animals to food, 
directly or indirectly, through food-producing animals.  
 
11.2. If there is a sudden death of a working animal, or suggestive signs of an illness 
that may increase the likelihood of food contamination, the farm shall seek the 
opinion of a veterinarian or someone competent who is responsible for the health 
of the animal and take appropriate action to avoid contact with food and food-
producing animals. 
 
 
12. 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR HALAL ANIMAL BREEDING 
 
12.1. Animals raised on the farm shall be of species permitted for Halal. See prohibited 
species in table 3 of this document.  
 
12.2. If the farm produces animal feed, it shall be free from non-halal elements (listed in 
table 3 of this document), and it shall not be a source of contamination for animals. 
 
12.3. The food and water offered to animals shall not contain non-halal elements (listed 
in table 3 of this document) and transmit contamination to food-producing animals, 
thus increasing the likelihood of food contamination. 
 
12.4. Pastures, including paddocks, shall not be a source of food contamination by 


=== PAGINA 23/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:23 de 40 
 
 
transmitting contaminants (natural, such as toxic plants, or those voluntarily or 
accidentally introduced by human action) to grazing and food-producing animals. 
 
12.5. The farm shall have a document that includes an updated list of animals raised, 
both at individual and batch levels, as well as relevant information (e.g. species, 
identification, age, sex). 
 
12.6. Use of veterinary medicines: 
12.6.1. The use of veterinary medicines shall not increase the likelihood of food 
contamination.  
12.6.2. Veterinary medicines shall be used in accordance with applicable laws and 
regulations. 
12.6.3. The application of the veterinary medicine shall be in accordance with the 
species for which it is intended, and the recommended dosage. The grace 
period shall be respected, considering milk production, egg collection, and 
animals sent to slaughter, and storage conditions (temperature, light). 
12.6.4. There shall be records including documentation associated to the use of 
veterinary medicines, including the veterinary prescription, identification of 
the animals treated, how the medicine was administered, start and end 
dates of application, and grace periods, if applicable. 
12.6.5. If a needle breaks during the application of the veterinary medicine, the 
farm shall take measures to ensure the material is extracted from the 
animal. If this is not viable, the farm shall inform the next stage of the food 
chain, recording in the animal or groups of animals accompanying 
documents. 
 
12.7. Milk production (milking): 
12.7.1. Milking operations shall be carried out in a way that minimizes the 
likelihood of contamination of the milk, for example, by not causing 
discomfort and stress to the animal (discomfort and stress can cause the 
animal to urinate or defecate during milking, and this body waste can enter 
the suction equipment) 


=== PAGINA 24/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:24 de 40 
 
 
12.7.2. Colostrum and milk that do not appear to be normal shall be excluded from 
the food chain. 
12.7.3. The farm shall adopt measures to minimize the likelihood of milk 
contamination during milking, such as: 
a) Before milking, carry out an assessment of the milk by visual 
examination or physical-chemical indicators, after careful observation 
of the cow's behavior and the appearance of its udder and teats, to 
check whether the milk appears normal and will not contaminate the 
food; 
b) Clean, and when necessary, disinfect the teats before milking, using 
appropriated means. 
 
12.8. Egg collection: 
12.8.1. The farm shall take measures to collect eggs as quickly as possible after 
laying. 
12.8.2. Eggs shall be collected, handled, stored and packaged in a way that 
minimizes the likelihood of contamination and damage to the eggs or their 
shells. 
12.8.3. If the farm realizes that the egg intended for human consumption has been 
contaminated during collection, handling or packaging operations, it shall 
adopt appropriated measures to prevent this egg from entering the food 
chain. 
 
12.9. Animals for slaughter 
12.9.1. Only animals that are not likely to cause food contamination shall be sent 
for slaughter, for example, animals that are not sick. 
 
12.10. Aquatic animal husbandry: 
12.10.1. The conditions under which aquatic animals are raised for human 
consumption shall not increase the likelihood of non-Halal contamination 
of the food. 
12.10.2. The farm shall adopt measures to maintain the health of aquatic animals 


=== PAGINA 25/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:25 de 40 
 
 
and the quality of the water in which they are raised, in order to minimize 
the likelihood of food contamination.  
12.10.3. Records should include water and soil analysis, and monitoring attesting 
the suitability in relation to environmental contaminants. 
 
 
13. 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF ANIMAL WELFARE 
 
13.1. The farm shall meet all animal welfare requirements in all its activities during 
animal husbandry (cattle, sheep, poultry, fish, etc.), ensuring an environment that 
contributes to the animal's quality of life, with adequate physical and physiological 
conditions. 
 
13.2. MANAGEMENT: 
13.2.1. Animal handling shall be carried out calmly without causing stress to the 
animals. 
13.2.2. For cattle, the following points shall be met: 
a) Avoid sudden movements and excessive noise; 
b) Use electric prods only as a last resource, and never use them on 
sensitive parts of animals, such as genitals, snout and eyes; 
c) Management shall always be carried out in a group. 
13.2.3. For poultry, catching is the first stage of management, and the following 
points shall be met: 
a) Catching shall never be carried out using a single leg, neck or wing. It 
is recommended that it be performed by the back or both legs; 
b) Carry out the catching during the coldest hours of the day; 
c) Avoid excessive noise; 
d) It is advisable to use a blue light when catching is performed at night, 
in order to promote a calm environment; 
e) When placing poutlry in boxes, make sure there is enough space so 
that one poultry is not under the other. 
 


=== PAGINA 26/40 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION  
 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FARMS 
CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1 
Rev: 03 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:26 de 40 
 
 
13.3. TRANSPORTATION:  
13.3.1. Transport shall have an adequate structure and frequent maintenance 
to ensure that the animals arrive at the slaughterhouse free from injuries 
and stress. 
13.3.2. 
Before being transported, animals shall fast for an appropriate period to 
minimize the likelihood of food contamination. 
13.3.3. 
Vehicles shall not be overcrowded. 
13.3.4. 
Animals shall be sent clean to slaughterhouses. 
13.3.5. 
For cattle, the following points shall be met: 
a) Vehicles shall not have sharp structures; 
b) Vehicles should have non-slip structures to reduce falls and slips; 
c) Batches of cattle transported to the slaughterhouse shall contain 
animals that already live together, avoiding mixing unknown animals. 
13.3.6. 
For poultry, the following points shall be met: 
a) Boxes shall be stacked in a stable, safe manner and to ensure good 
ventilation, so as not to cause heat or cold stress; 
b) Poultry should be transported during the coolest hours of the day; 
c) When loading and unloading vehicles, boxes shall be handled carefully, 
without dropping, throwing, turning or inverting, in order to minimize the 
risk of injury or stress to the birds;  
d) The boxes shall be in a good state of conservation and maintenance, 
clean, and shall not have edges that could cause injuries to the poultry. 
 
13.4. ANIMAL HEALTH MONITORING: 
13.4.1. Animals shall always be healthy to minimize the transmission of zoonotic 
agents and not increase the likelihood of food contamination. To achieve 
this, the farm shall adopt measures for the early detection of diseases, 
and carry out a health assessment of animals recently introduced to the 
farm. 
13.4.2. There shall be documentation including the history of prophylactic 
measures, visits carried out by the veterinarian or equally recognized 
competent person in animal health, and occurrence of diseases.

FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICATION

CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.7.1
