=== SHEET: Report - DETAILED (dimensoes: A2:I66) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO
 
INDUSTRIAL AUDIT REPORT – STAGE 1 (DT 7.5)
Category K |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.5
Rev.: 06
Date: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAJOR | NC - MINOR | OBS | NA | Evidences | 
 | 1. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR THE MANUFACTURE OF HALAL PRODUCTS |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1. The company has its products in accordance with the Halal requirements and a sanitary operating license in accordance to the legal issues of the country / region. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.1 and 6.11). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2. Through the documentary evaluation it is possible to confirm that the final product only contains raw-material, inputs,  processing aids and packaging materials, free of Haram and Najash (Impure) elements and animal derivatives not derived from Halal slaughter (DT 7.5 - Item 6.2 and 6.3). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.3. All raw materials, inputs, processing aids, and packaging materials, used on Halal certified products, were previously informed and approved by FAMBRAS HALAL, and have documentation that demonstrates their lawful origin in the face of Halal, ensuring its Halal Status (DT 7.5 - Item 6.4 and 6.7). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. The packaging materials used in Halal certified products have documented evidence of their atoxicity. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5. All raw materials, processing aids, inputs and packaging materials, have documentation that proves their compliance with physical-chemical standards and certify that they only have allowed substances and with the maximum imits established by the current legislation. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.6). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6. There is an approval and selection procedure for raw materials, inputs, processing aids and packaging materials suppliers, to ensure the food quality and integrity, as well its safety for Halal (DT 7.5 – Item 6.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.7. Ethyl alcohol, when necessary, is used only as an auxiliary / process additive, and there is documentation proving that it is not from the alcoholic beverage industry. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.8.1 and 6.8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8. If the company uses products derived from alcoholic beverage industries in solid form, such as brewer's yeast extract, there are records of the purification procedure carried out to completely eliminate the color, odor and flavor of the alcoholic beverage. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.8.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. Documental it is possible to verify that when the company uses ethyl alcohol (ethanol), as an auxiliary / process additive,  in the process of manufacturing the products present in the scope of certification OR this occurs naturally due to natural fermentation, the residual in the final product is controlled (DT 7.5 - item 6.8.3 and 6.8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.10. The name of the final product, the brand, the logo, the sensory characteristics and the model of the packaging do not infringe the principles of Shariah, for example, the brand does not refer to Haram elements such as bacon, ham, beer, or any other that could confuse the consumer (DT 7.5 - Item 6.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.11. From a general documentary assessment of the process by the religious auditor, it is possible to verify the respect of Islamic rules  throughout the production chain (receipt, preparation, packaging, labeling, storage and transportation) of the items certified by FAMBRAS HALAL (DT 7.5 – Item 6.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.12. All types of cleaning or maintenance products such as detergents, sanitizers, greases and lubricants, were previously informed and approved by FAMBRAS HALAL, and have documented evidence that they are free of non-halal components or materials (forbidden to the Shariah). (DT 7.4 - Item 6.12) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.    GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR HALAL PRODUCTION AREAS |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1. Evaluation only for units manufacturing Halal and non-Halal products (DT 7.5 - Item 7.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.1. The company predicts in its documents, as a hygiene procedure or the Halal Guarantee System (QMS / HAS), the obligation to clean the equipment before starting Halal production, as well as the validation of this procedure. (DT 7.5 - Item 7.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.2.  The company predicts in its documents, as a hygiene procedure or the Halal Guarantee System (QMS / HAS) that the hygiene equipment should not be performed alternately between equipment processing Halal and equipment processing non-Halal products (e.g. Performing an alternating CIP between equipment processing Halal and not Halal). (DT 7.5 - Item 7.2.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2. The company provides in its documents, as a hygiene procedure or the Halal Guarantee System (QMS / HAS) that if production lines have already been used to manufacture products of pork origin, before converting it to the manufacture of Halal products, it must undergo a purification ritual, which consists of subjecting the equipment to the passage of water + cleaning agents, such as detergent and sanitizer, at least 7 times, for the complete removal of color, taste and aroma of the contaminant to occur. (DT 7.5 - Item 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.3. The company provides in its documents, as a a hygiene procedure or the Halal Guarantee System (QMS / HAS), that the conversion of lines between Halal and swine production shall not be carried out continuously. (DT 7.5 - Item 7.3.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.4. If the company already manufactures Halal products containing gelatin or other elements of animal origin (from slaughtered animals),  or identified as high risk for Halal, there is a Muslim and competent supervisor of FAMBRAS HALAL working on the spot during Halal production (DT 7.5 – Item 7.4 and 7.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.5. The company provides in its documents, as a a hygiene procedure or the Halal Guarantee System (QMS / HAS), that all the equipments, production lines, tools, utensils and materials used for Halal products preparation shall be clean and free of impurities,and shall not be made of or contain any type of non-Halal material. (DT 7.5 - Item 7.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.    GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR STORAGE AND TRANSPORTATION OF HALAL PRODUCTS |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1. The company is aware and documents in the Halal Assurance System (HAS) that all products certified by FAMBRAS HALAL shall be identified (being the Halal seal visible on the secondary packaging a sufficient form of identification), and, as applicable,  Halal products shall be separated from other non-Halal products during storage and transportation to prevent them from being mixed with non-Halal products.  (DT 7.5 – Item 8.1, 8.1.4 and 8.2 and DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2. The company documents in the Halal Assurance System (HAS) the evaluation of hazards and the appropriated procedure, with the measures that shall be taken, for example, in case of breakage/leakage of non-Halal product packages in the final product warehouse, to ensure no cross-contamination of the Halal product. (DT 7.5 – Item 8.1.3 and DT 7.3 – Item 11.1 and 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3. The company is aware and documents in the Halal Assurance System (HAS) that it is forbidden to carry Halal products together with products of pork origin and / or their derivative, to avoid cross contamination with Haram products. (DT 7.5 - Item 8.3 and DT 7.3 - Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.4. The company's transportation procedure provides the adequate controls for Halal safety, avoiding cross contamination with non-Halal products, and compliance with hygiene conditions (DT 7.5 - Item 8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.    GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR QUALITY AND SAFETY OF THE HALAL PRODUCTS |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.   The Good Manufacturing Practices system contains at least  (DT 7.5 – Item 10.4): |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.1. Quality policy |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.2. Quality management manual; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.3. Basic Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) requirements; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.4. Sanitation and hygiene procedures; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.5. Qualification and validation program plus evidences; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.6. Example of a production and / or analysis contract (company / customer); |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.7. Self-Inspection / Internal Audit Program |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.8. Organization chart and staff management procedure |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.9. Training program (quality,production, GMP) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.10. Personal hygiene practices |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.11. Facilities, Equipments and Materials requirements |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.12. Documentation and Records control |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.13. Good production practices |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.14. Good Quality control practices |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.15. Water Treatment Procedure |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.16. Waste management procedure; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.17. Maintenance procedure; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2. The company has a traceabilty program of the Halal product (DT 7.5 – Item 10.4 and DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.7 and 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. The company has a recall program, which includes communication with the Halal certifier, and foresees the need to carry it out in the event of contamination that undermines the Halal status of the product. (DT 7.5 – Item 10.4 and 10.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4. The company has a risk analysis, that assures the product's quality and safety, and avoids any cross contamination that jeopardizes the Halal status of the product. (DT 7.5 – Item 10.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. The company has a methodology for the treatment of deviations, complaints and nonconformities (referring to internal and external audits), including effective corrective actions, taken to avoid future occurrences (DT 7.5 – Item 10.7 and 10.8) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5. HALAL ASSURANCE SYSTEM (HAS) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1.  The company has a Halal Assurance System documented according to the internal condition of the company, being developed in a format compatible with the quality management system, and in order to ensure the Halal status of the certified products (DT 7.5 - Item 10.1 and DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 and 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2. The company establishes a Halal policy (whether or not it is linked to another company policy) that indicates its commitment with the production of Halal products, being this one duly signed by the top management of the company (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.1, 7.1 and 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3. There is a team responsible for Halal Management, which is presented in the Halal Guarantee System (QMS - HAS), where the responsibilities and autonomy are described. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.2, 8.1 and 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4. The team is composed of the necessary authorities (including the Halal supervisor, if applicable), that is directly involved on Halal production process, and has the autonomy to ensure the Halal condition of the products and to answer for the audit findings (DT 7.3 - Item 8.2 and 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5. The company has a planning for Halal training scheduled to take place at least once a year, and they are mandatory to all personnel involved in Halal process, including new employees (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 and 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. The content of Halal training covers the Halal, Haram and Mashbooh concept, the market that the company intends to reach out with the certification, and internal controls established to guarantee the certification. Furthermore, for employees involved in monitoring PCCHs, training includes the importance of their activity regarding the Halal safety. (DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 and 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. The Halal Assurance System (HAS) registration document describes the composition and flowchart of each of FAMBRAS HALAL certified products. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.4, 10.1 and 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8. The Hazards and Halal Critical Control Points are identified, validated and recorded in the company's HAS, through a risk matrix that provides an analysis of  the probability of occurrence of the hazard versus the severity of the occurrence of the hazard, taking into account cross-contamination with Haram products at each stage of the flow chart. (DT 7.3 - Item  6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 and 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. The Halal Assurance System (HAS) developed by the company predicts the need of the complete segregation of Halal and non-Halal raw materials and products, as well its identification, at any and all stages of production flow (receipt, preparation, packaging, labeling, storage and transportation) to avoid any cross-contamination. (DT 7.5 - Item 7.1, 7.2.3 and 7.2.7 and DT 7.3 - Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10. The preventive measures for the occurrence of risk and corrective measures in case of deviations are clearly defined in the company's HAS. As well as the procedures to be adopted for the management of Halal Critical Control Points are well documented, including monitoring records, those responsible and actions in case of deviations. (DT 7.3 - Item 11.4 and 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. The company describes in the registration document of the Halal Assurance System a methodology for HAS internal audit. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.6 and 12.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12. The HAS internal audit is planned (for the first year of certification) or has been carried out, at least once a year, or more often if necessary. (DT 7.3 - Item 12.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  |

 | 4.        Normas de referencia: |  |  |  |  |  |  |

=== SHEET: Informe DETALLADO (dimensoes: A2:I66) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS HALAL 
 
INFORME DE AUDITORIA INDUSTRIAL – ETAPA 1 (DT 7.5)
Categoria K |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.5
Rev.: 06
Fecha: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAYOR | NC - MENOR | OBS | NA | Evidencias | 
 | 1.    REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA FABRICAÇÃO DE PRODUTOS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1.  La empresa tiene sus productos conformes con los requisitos Halal y una licencia de funcionamiento sanitaria que se ajusta a las cuestiones legales del país/región  (DT 7.5 - Item 6.1 y 6.11). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2. Mediante la evaluación de la documentación, es posible confirmar que el producto final sólo contiene materias primas, insumos, auxiliares de proceso y materiales de envasado exentos de elementos Haram, Najash (Impuro) y derivados animales que no proceden de sacrificios Halal. (DT 7.5 – Item 6.2 e 6.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.3. Todas las materias primas, auxiliares de proceso, insumos y materiales de empaque han sido previamente informados y aprobados por FAMBRAS HALAL, y cuentan con documentación que demuestra su origen lícito en términos Halal, garantizando su Estatus Halal  (DT 7.5 - Item 6.4 y 6.7). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. Los materiales de empaque utilizados en los productos con certificación Halal disponen de documentación que demuestra su atoxicidad (DT 7.5 - Item 6.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5. Todas las materias primas, auxiliares de proceso, insumos y materiales de envasado cuentan con documentación que acredita su conformidad con las normas microbiológicas y físico-químicas, y que demuestran que sólo contienen sustancias permitidas y dentro de los límites máximos establecidos por la legislación vigente. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.6). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6. Existe un procedimiento para la aprobación y selección de proveedores de materias primas, insumos, auxiliares de proceso y materiales de envasado, con el fin de garantizar la calidad e integridad de los alimentos, así como su inocuidad para Halal (DT 7.5 – Item 6.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.7. El alcohol etílico, cuando es necesario, sólo se utiliza como auxiliar/aditivo del proceso, y existe documentación que demuestra que no procede de industrias de bebidas alcohólicas. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.8.1 e 6.8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8.  Si la empresa utiliza productos derivados de las industrias de bebidas alcohólicas en forma sólida, como el extracto de levadura de cerveza, existen registros del procedimiento de purificación llevado a cabo para eliminar por completo el color, el olor y el sabor de la bebida alcohólica. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.8.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. Se puede demostrar documentalmente que cuando la empresa utiliza alcohol etílico (etanol) como auxiliar/aditivo en el proceso de fabricación de los productos en el alcance de la certificación O este ocurre naturalmente debido a la fermentación natural, el residuo en el producto final es controlado (DT 7.5 - Item 6.8.3 y 6.8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.10. El nombre del producto final, la marca, el logotipo, las características sensoriales y el modelo de empaque no infringen los principios de la Sharia, por ejemplo, la marca no alude a elementos Haram como el tocino, el jamón, la cerveza o cualquier otra cosa que pueda confundir al consumidor (DT 7.5 - Item 6.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.11. A partir de una evaluación documental general del proceso por parte del auditor religioso, es posible verificar el cumplimiento de las normas islámicas en todas las fases de la cadena de producción (recepción, preparación, envasado, etiquetado, almacenamiento y transporte) de los artículos certificados por FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.5 – Item 6.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.12. Todo tipo de productos de limpieza o mantenimiento, como detergentes, sanitizantes, grasas y lubricantes, han sido previamente informados y aprobados por FAMBRAS HALAL, y cuentan con documentación que demuestra que están libres de componentes o materiales no Halal (prohibidos por la Sharia) (DT 7.5 – Item 6.12) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.    REQUISITOS GENERALES PARA LAS ÁREAS DE PRODUCCIÓN HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1. Evaluación únicamente para las plantas que procesan productos Halal y no Halal (DT 7.5 - Item 7.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.1. Los documentos de la empresa, como el procedimiento de limpieza o el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/HAS), estipulan que los equipos deben limpiarse antes de iniciar la producción halal, y que este procedimiento debe validarse.  (DT 7.5 - Item 7.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.2. La empresa establece en sus documentos, como procedimiento de higienización o del Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/ HAS), que la limpieza de los equipos no debe realizarse alternativamente entre equipos que procesan productos Halal y equipos que procesan productos no Halal (por ejemplo, realizar un CIP alternativo entre equipos que procesan productos Halal y no Halal). (DT 7.5 - Item 7.2.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2.  La empresa prevé en sus documentos, como procedimiento de limpieza o del Sistema de Garantía Halal (QAS/ HAS), que si las líneas de producción ya han sido utilizadas para fabricar productos porcinos, antes de convertirlas para fabricar productos Halal, es necesario que hayan sido sometidas a un ritual de purificación, que consiste en someter el equipo a agua + agentes de limpieza, como detergente e higienizante, al menos 7 veces, de forma que se elimine completamente el color, sabor y aroma del contaminante. (DT 7.5 - Item 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.3. Los documentos de la empresa, como los procedimientos de limpieza o el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/ HAS), establecen que la conversión de líneas entre la producción halal y la porcina no debe realizarse de forma continua (DT 7.4 - Item 7.3.2) (DT 7.5 - Item 7.3.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.4. Si la empresa fabrica productos Halal que contienen carne, gelatina u otros elementos de origen animal (procedentes de animales sacrificados), o identificados como de alto riesgo para el Halal, hay un supervisor musulmán competente de FAMBRAS HALAL trabajando in situ durante la producción Halal.l (DT 7.5 – Item 7.4 e 7.4.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.5. La empresa estipula en sus documentos, como el procedimiento de higienización o el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/ HAS), que todos los equipos, líneas de limpieza, herramientas, utensilios y materiales utilizados para la elaboración de productos Halal deben estar limpios y libres de impurezas, y no deben estar fabricados ni contener ningún tipo de material no Halall. (DT 7.5 - Item 7.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.    REQUISITOS GENERALES PARA EL ALMACENAMIENTO Y TRANSPORTE DE LOS PRODUCTOS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1.La empresa es consciente y documenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) que los productos certificados por FAMBRAS HALAL deben estar identificados (basta con que el sello sea visible en el envase secundario del producto Halal) y, en su caso, separados de otros productos no Halal durante su almacenamiento y transporte, para evitar que se mezclen con productos no Halal (DT 7.5 – Item 8.1, 8.1.4 y 8.2 y DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2. La empresa documenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) la evaluación de riesgos y el procedimiento adecuado, con las medidas que deben adoptarse, por ejemplo, en caso de rotura/filtración de envases de productos no Halal en el almacén de productos finales, para garantizar que el producto Halal no sufra contaminación cruzada. (DT 7.5 – Item 8.1.3 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.1 e 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3.  La empresa es consciente y documenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) que está prohibido transportar juntos productos Halal y productos de origen porcino y/o sus derivados, para evitar la contaminación cruzada con productos Haram (DT 7.5 – Item 8.3 y DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.4. El procedimiento de transporte de la empresa incluye los controles adecuados para garantizar la inocuidad Halal, evitar la contaminación cruzada con productos no Halal y cumplir las condiciones de higiene (DT 7.5 – Item 8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.   REQUISITOS GENERALES PARA LA CALIDAD E INOCUIDAD DE PRODUCTOS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1. El sistema de Buenas Prácticas de Manufactura contiene como mínimo (DT 7.5 – Item 10.4): |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.1. Política de calidad; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.2. Manual de Gestión de la Calidad; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.3. Manual de Buenas Prácticas de Manufactura (BPM); |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.4. Procedimiento de limpieza y sanitización; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.5. Programa de calificación y validación; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.6. Ejemplo de contrato de producción y/o análisis (empresa/cliente); |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.7. Programa de autoinspección y auditorías de calidad; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.8. Organigrama y  programa de gestión del personal |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.9. Programa de capacitación (calidad, producción, BPM) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.10. Prácticas de higiene personal |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.11. Requisitos de instalaciones, equipos y materiales |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.12. Control de la documentación y los registros |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.13. Buenas prácticas de producción |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.14. Buenas prácticas de control de calidad |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.15.Procedimiento de tratamiento del agua |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.16. Procedimiento de manejo de residuos; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.17. Procedimiento de mantenimiento; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2. La empresa cuenta con un programa de trazabilidad de productos Halal. (DT 7.5 – Item 10.4 y DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.7 y 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. La empresa dispone de un programa de retiro de productos, que incluye la comunicación con el certificador Halal, y prevé la necesidad de llevarlo a cabo en caso de contaminación que afecte al estatus Halal del producto. (DT 7.5 – Item 10.4 y 10.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4. A empresa possui uma análise de risco, que garante a qualidade e segurança do produto, e evita qualquer contaminação cruzada que coloque em risco o status Halal do produto. (DT 7.5 – Item 10.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. La empresa dispone de una metodología para tratar las desviaciones, reclamaciones y no conformidades (relativas a auditorías internas y externas), incluidas las medidas correctivas eficaces adoptadas para evitar que se produzcan en el futuro. (DT 7.5 – Item 10.7 y 10.8) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.    SISTEMA DE GARANTÍA HALAL/ HALAL ASSURANCE SYSTEM (HAS) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1.  La empresa dispone de un Sistema de Garantía Halal documentado de acuerdo con las condiciones internas de la empresa, que se ha desarrollado en un formato compatible con el sistema de gestión de la calidad y de forma que garantice el estatus Halal de los productos certificados (DT 7.5 - Item 10.1 y DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 y 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2.  La empresa establece una política Halal (relacionada o no con otra política de la empresa) que indica su compromiso con la fabricación de productos Halal, debidamente firmada por la alta dirección de la empresa (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.1, 7.1 e 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3. Existe un equipo responsable de la Gestión Halal, que se presenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC - HAS), donde se describen las responsabilidades y autonomías correspondientes (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.2, 8.1 e 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4.  El equipo está formado por las autoridades necesarias (incluido el supervisor Halal, si aplica), que participan directamente en el proceso de producción Halal, y tiene autonomía para garantizar el estatus Halal de los productos y responder a los hallazgos encontrados durante las auditorías. (DT 7.3 – Item 8.2 e 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5.  La empresa cuenta con un cronograma de capacitación Halal al menos una vez al año, obligatorias para todas las personas implicadas en el proceso Halal, incluidos los nuevos empleados  (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 e 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. El contenido de los programas de capacitación Halal aborda el concepto de Halal, Haram y Mashbooh, el mercado al que la empresa pretende llegar con la certificación y los controles internos establecidos para garantizar la certificación. Además, para los empleados que participan en el control del PCCH, la capacitación incluye la importancia de su actividad en términos de inocuidad Halal. (DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 e 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. El documento de registro del Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) describe la composición y el diagrama de flujo de cada uno de los productos certificados por FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.4, 10.1 e 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8. Los peligros y puntos críticos de control Halal se identifican y registran en el HAS de la empresa utilizando una matriz de riesgo que analiza la probabilidad de que se produzca el evento frente a la gravedad de que se produzca, teniendo en cuenta la contaminación cruzada con productos Haram en cada etapa del diagrama de flujo del proceso. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 e 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. El Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) elaborado por la empresa estipula la necesidad de una separación completa entre las materias primas y los productos Halal y no Halal, así como su identificación, en todas y cada una de las etapas del flujo de fabricación (recepción, preparación, envasado, etiquetado, almacenamiento y transporte), con el fin de evitar cualquier contaminación cruzada (DT 7.5 - Item 7.1, 7.2.3 e 7.2.7 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10. Las medidas preventivas para la ocurrencia de riesgos y las medidas correctivas en caso de desviaciones están claramente definidas en la HAS de la empresa. Los procedimientos que deben adoptarse para gestionar los puntos críticos de control Halal están bien documentados, incluidos los registros de seguimiento, los responsables y las acciones en caso de desviaciones   (DT 7.3 – Item 11.4 e 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. La empresa describe en el documento de registro del Sistema de Garantía Halal una metodología para la auditoría interna del HAS  (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.6 e 12.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12. La auditoría interna del HAS está prevista (para el primer año de certificación) o se ha realizado al menos una vez al año, o con mayor frecuencia si es necesario. (DT 7.3 – Item 12.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
=== SHEET: ALCANCE AUDITADO (dimensoes: A1:E142) ===

=== SHEET: Relatório DETALHADO (dimensoes: A2:I66) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO
 
RELATÓRIO DE AUDITORIA INDUSTRIAL – ESTÁGIO 1 (DT 7.5)
Categoria K |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.5
Rev.: 06
Data: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAIOR | NC - MENOR | OBS | NA | Evidências | 
 | 1.    REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA FABRICAÇÃO DE PRODUTOS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1. A empresa possui seus produtos em conformidade com os requisitos Halal e um alvará de funcionamento sanitário em conformidade com as questões legais do país/ região. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.1 e 6.11). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2. Através da avaliação da documentação é possível confirmar que o produto final só contém matérias-primas, insumos, auxiliares de processo e materiais de embalagem isentos de elementos Haram, Najash (Impuros) e derivados animais não provenientes do abate Halal (DT 7.5 – Item 6.2 e 6.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.3. Todas as matérias primas, auxiliares de processo, insumos e materiais de embalagem, usados nos produtos certificados Halal, foram previamente informados e aprovados pela FAMBRAS HALAL, e possuem documentação que demonstrem sua origem lícita diante do Halal, garantindo seu Status Halal (DT 7.5 - Item 6.4 e 6.7). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. Os materiais de embalagem usados nos produtos certificados Halal possuem documentação que comprovem sua atoxidade. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5. Todas as matérias primas, auxiliares de processo, insumos e materiais de embalagem,  possuem documentação que comprove sua conformidade com os padrões físico-químicos, e ateste que possuem apenas substâncias permitidas e dentro dos limites máximos estabelecidos pela legislação vigente. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.6). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6. Existe um procedimento para homologação e seleção de fornecedores de matérias primas, insumos, auxiliares de processo e materiais de embalagem, de forma a garantir a qualidade e integridade do alimento, bem como sua segurança para o Halal. (DT 7.5 – Item 6.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.7. Álcool etílico, quando necessário, é utilizado somente como auxiliar/ aditivo de processo, e  existe documentação que comprova que este não é proveniente de indústrias de bebidas alcoólicas. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.8.1 e 6.8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8. Caso a empresa utilize produtos derivados de indústrias de bebidas alcoólicas em forma sólida, como extrato de levedura de cerveja, há registros do procedimento de purificação realizado para eliminação completa da cor, odor e sabor da bebida alcoólica. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.8.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. Documentalmente é possível verificar que quando a empresa utilizar álcool etílico (etanol), como auxiliar/ aditivo de processo, no processo de fabricação dos produtos presentes no escopo de certificação OU este ocorrer naturalmente devido a fermentação natural, o residual no produto final é controlado. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.8.3 e 6.8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.10. O nome do produto final, a marca, o logo, as características sensoriais e o modelo da embalagem não infringem os princípios da Shariah, por exemplo a marca não faz alusão a elementos Haram, como bacon, presunto, cerveja, ou qualquer outro que possa confundir o consumidor. (DT 7.5 - Item 6.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.11. A partir de uma avaliação geral documental do processo, pelo auditor religioso, é possível verificar o respeito as regras islâmicas em toda a fase da cadeia de produção (recebimento, preparação, embalagem, rotulagem, armazenamento e transporte) dos itens certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.5 – Item 6.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.12. Todos os tipos de produtos de limpeza, ou manutenção, tais como detergentes, sanitizantes, graxas e lubrificantes, foram previamente informados e aprovados pela FAMBRAS HALAL, e possuem documentações que demonstram que são livres de componentes ou materiais não-Halal (proibidos para a Shariah). (DT 7.5 – Item 6.12) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.    REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA AS ÁREAS DE PRODUÇÃO HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1. Avaliação apenas para unidades que fabricam produtos Halal e não Halal (DT 7.5 - Item 7.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.1. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), a obrigatoriedade de limpeza dos equipamentos antes do início da produção Halal, bem como a validação deste procedimento.  (DT 7.5 - Item 7.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.2. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que a higienização dos equipamentos não deve ser realizada alternadamente entre equipamentos que processam Halal e equipamentos que processam produtos não Halal (Ex. Realizar um CIP alternado entre equipamentos que processam Halal e não Halal). (DT 7.5 - Item 7.2.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que se as linhas de produção já tiverem sido utilizadas para fabricar produtos de origem suína, antes de converte-la para fabricação de produtos Halal é necessário que a mesma tenha passado por um ritual de purificação, o qual consiste em submeter os equipamentos a passagem de água + agentes de limpeza, como detergente e sanitizante, no mínimo 7 vezes, para que ocorra a remoção completa de cor, sabor e aroma do contaminante. (DT 7.5 - Item 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.3. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que a conversão das linhas entre produção Halal e suína não deve ser realizada de forma contínua. (DT 7.5 - Item 7.3.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.4. Caso a empresa fabrique produtos Halal que contém gelatina ou outros elementos de origem animal (proveniente de animais abatidos), ou identificados como alto risco para o Halal, existe um supervisor, muçulmano e competente, da FAMBRAS HALAL trabalhando no local durante a produção Halal (DT 7.5 – Item 7.4 e 7.4.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.5. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que todos os equipamentos, linhas de produção, ferramentas, utensílios e materiais  utilizados para a preparação de produtos Halal devem ser limpos e livres de impurezas, e não devem ser fabricados ou conter qualquer tipo de material não Halal. (DT 7.5 - Item 7.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.    REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA ARMAZENAGEM E TRANSPORTE DE PRODUTOS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1. A empresa está ciente e documenta no Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) que todos os produtos certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL devem estar identificados (sendo suficiente ter o selo visível na embalagem secundária do produto Halal) e, conforme aplicabilidade, separados de outros produtos não Halal durante o armazenamento e transporte, para prevenir que estes sejam misturados com produtos não Halal. (DT 7.5 – Item 8.1, 8.1.4 e 8.2 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2. A empresa documenta no Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) a avaliação dos perigos e o procedimento adequado, com as medidas que devem ser praticadas, por exemplo em caso de rompimento/ vazamento de embalagens de produtos não Halal no armazém de produto final, para assegurar a não contaminação cruzada do produto Halal. (DT 7.5 – Item 8.1.3 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.1 e 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3. A empresa está ciente e documenta no Sistema de garantia Halal (HAS) que é proibido transportar junto produtos Halal e produtos de origem suína e/ ou seus derivados, para evitar contaminação cruzada com produtos Haram. (DT 7.5 – Item 8.3 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.4. O procedimento de transporte da empresa prevê os controles adequados para segurança Halal, evitando a contaminação cruzada com produtos não Halal, e o atendimento das condições de higiene (DT 7.5 – Item 8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.   REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA QUALIDADE E SEGURANÇA DOS PRODUTOS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1. A sistemática de Boas Práticas de Fabricação contém no mínimo (DT 7.5 – Item 10.4): |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.1. Política da qualidade; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.2. Manual da qualidade; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.3. Manual de Boas Práticas de Fabricação (BPF); |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.4. Procedimento de sanitização e higiene; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.5. Programa de qualificação e validação; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.6. Exemplo de um contrato de produção e/ou análise (empresa/ cliente); |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.7. Programa de auto inspeção e auditorias de qualidade; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.8. Organograma e programa de gestão do pessoal |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.9. Programa de treinamento (qualidade, produção, BPF) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.10. Práticas de higiene pessoal |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.11. Requisitos das Instalações, Equipamentos e Materiais |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.12. Controle de Documentação e Registros |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.13. Boas práticas de produção |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.14. Boas práticas de controle de qualidade |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.15. Procedimento de tratamento de Água |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.16. Procedimento de manejo de resíduos; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.17. Procedimento de manutenção; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2. A empresa possui um programa de rastreabilidade do produto Halal. (DT 7.5 – Item 10.4 e DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.7 e 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. A empresa possui um programa de recolhimento, o qual contempla a comunicação com a certificadora Halal, e preve a necessidade de realizá-lo mediante contaminação que desabone o status Halal do produto. (DT 7.5 – Item 10.4 e 10.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4. A empresa possui uma análise de risco, que garante a qualidade e segurança do produto, e evita qualquer contaminação cruzada que coloque em risco o status Halal do produto. (DT 7.5 – Item 10.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. A empresa possui uma metodologia de tratamento de desvios, reclamações e não-conformidades (referentes a auditorias internas e externas), incluindo ações corretivas eficazes, adotadas para evitar ocorrências futuras. (DT 7.5 – Item 10.7 e 10.8) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.    SISTEMA DE GARANTIA HALAL/ HALAL ASSURANCE SYSTEM (HAS) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1.  A empresa possui um Sistema de Garantia Halal documentado de acordo com a condição interna da empresa, sendo este desenvolvido em um formato compatível com o sistema de gestão da qualidade, e de forma a garantir o status Halal dos produtos certificados (DT 7.5 - Item 10.1 e DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 e 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2.  A empresa estabelece uma política Halal (seja ela interligada ou não com outra política da empresa) que indique o seu compromisso com a produção de produtos Halal, estando esta devidamente assinada pela alta direção da empresa. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.1, 7.1 e 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3. Existe uma equipe responsável pela Gestão Halal, sendo esta apresentada no Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ – HAS), onde estão descritas as devidas responsabilidades e autonomias. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.2, 8.1 e 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4.  A equipe é composta pelas autoridades necessárias (incluindo o supervisor Halal, se aplicável), que estão diretamente envolvidas no processo de produção Halal, e possui autonomia para assegurar a condição Halal dos produtos e responder pelos apontamentos encontrados durante as auditorias (DT 7.3 – Item 8.2 e 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5. A empresa possui um planejamento para realização de treinamentos Halal programados para ocorrer no mínimo uma vez ao ano, e os mesmos são obrigatorios para todas as pessoas envolvidas no processo Halal, incluindo novos funcionários. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 e 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. O conteúdo dos treinamentos Halal contemplam o conceito Halal, Haram e Mashbooh, mercado que a empresa pretende atingir com a certificação, e controles internos estabelecidos para garantir a certificação. Ainda, para os  funcionários envolvidos no monitoramento dos PCCH, os treinamentoss incluem a importância da sua atividade diante da segurança do Halal. (DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 e 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. O documento de registro do Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) descreve a composição e o fluxograma de cada um dos produtos certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.4, 10.1 e 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8. Os Perigos e Pontos Críticos de Controle Halal são identificados, validados e registrados no HAS da empresa, através de uma matriz de risco que proporciona a análise da probabilidade de ocorrência do perigo versus a severidade da ocorrência do perigo, levando em consideração a contaminação cruzada com produtos Haram em cada etapa do fluxograma. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 e 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. O Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) elaborado pela empresa prevê a necessidade de separação completa entre as matérias primas e produtos Halal e não Halal, bem como sua identificação, em toda e qualquer etapa do fluxo de fabricação (recebimento, preparação, embalagem, rotulagem, armazenamento e transporte), a fim de evitar qualquer contaminação cruzada (DT 7.5 - Item 7.1, 7.2.3 e 7.2.7 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10. As medidas preventivas para a ocorrência do risco e as medidas corretivas em caso de desvios estão claramente definidas no HAS da empresa. Bem como as tratativas a serem adotadas para gestão dos Pontos Críticos de Controle Halal estão bem documentadas, incluindo os registros dos monitoramentos, os responsáveis e as ações no caso de desvios.  (DT 7.3 – Item 11.4 e 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. A empresa descreve no documento de registro do Sistema de Garantia Halal uma metodologia para auditoria interna HAS. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.6 e 12.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12. A auditoria interna do HAS está prevista (para o primeiro ano de certificação) ou foi realizada, pelo menos uma vez ao ano, ou mais vezes, se necessário. (DT 7.3 – Item 12.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  |

=== PAGINA 1/29 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR 
INDUSTRIALIZED FOOD AND FOOD ADDITIVE CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.1 
Rev.: 14 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:1 de 29 
 
1. OBJECTIVE 
This document establishes the general requirements for industrialized food and food 
additives companies, that fits in categories C1, C2, C3, C4 and K (GSO 2055-2 and SMIIC 
02), that are Halal certified by FAMBRAS Halal. 
 
 
2. APPLICATION FIELD  
This document applies to all FAMBRAS HALAL industrialized food and food additives 
certified companies that fits in categories C1, C2, C3, C4 and K (GSO 2055-2 e SMIIC 02). 
 
 
3. REFERENCE DOCUMENTS  
• GSO 2055-1:2015 – HALAL products - Part one General Requirements for 
HALAL Food. Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf countries; 
• GSO 2538:2017 - The maximum limits for residues of ethyl alcohol (ethanol) 
in food; Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf countries; 
• GSO 2500:2022 – Additives Permitted for Use in Food Stuffs – Gulf countries; 
• GSO 05/ FDS 995/ 2013 – Sweeteners Permitted in Food – Gulf countries; 
• GSO 707:2021 - Flavourings Permitted for Use in Food Stuffs – Gulf countries; 
• GSO 2500:2022/Amd1:2023 – Gulf countries; 
• GSO 9:2022 - Labeling of Prepackaged Food Stuffs – Gulf countries; 
• MS 1500:2019 HALAL food - production, preparation, handling and storage - 
general guidelines – JAKIM Malaysian Standard; 
• MS 1500:2009 HALAL food - production, preparation, handling and storage - 
general guidelines – JAKIM Malaysian Standard; 
• Malaysian Protocol for the Halal Meat and Poultry Productions, 2008; 
• MS 2200-1:2008 - Islamic Consumer Goods - Part 1 - Cosmetic and Personal 
Care General Guidelines; 
• MS 2200-2:2013 - Islamic Consumer Goods - Part 2: Usage of animal bone, 
skin and hair - General Guidelines; 
• MS 2400-1:2018 - Halal supply chain management system - Part 1 - 
Transportation;

=== PAGINA 3/29 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR 
INDUSTRIALIZED FOOD AND FOOD ADDITIVE CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.1 
Rev.: 14 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:3 de 29 
 
• CODEX STAN 192/195 – General Standard for Food Additives – CODEX 
ALIMENTARIUS 
 
 
4. GENERAL PROVISIONS  
All industrialized food and food additives that aim to obtains the Halal certification by 
FAMBRAS HALAL for categories C1, C2, C3, C4 and K (GSO 2055-2 and SMIIC 02), shall 
meet the requirements established in this document. 
In this document, the word “shall” indicates a requirement and the word “should”, a 
recommendation. 
 
 
5. DEFINITIONS 
 
 
Shariah  
The word Shariah (Islamic jurisprudence) refers to the totality of the orders of Allah 
(God) that regulate all aspects of the life of a Muslim (MS 1900:2005), meaning, everything 
that is described in the holy Quran, that aside the God's revelations to Prophet Muhammad 
(SAWS). In addition to the Qur'an, the Sunnah, i.e. all practices and sayings of the Prophet 
Muhammad (SAW) are also part of the Shariah. 
 
 
Halal 
The word 'Halal' means lawful, the same as allowed. Halal designated products are 
those which consumption is allowed by God, according to Shariah.  
Halal is then the basis of all that is lawful, in food, in social life, in acts practiced 
(conduct), in justice, in clothes, in finances, etc., it is a system based on the principles and 
values that benefit the humanity.  
The Holy Quran teaches us in 5th Surah, 4th verse: "Lawful for you are all good 
foods". 
 
 
Halal slaughter (Tazkeya) 
Halal slaughter is an Islamic ritual that shall be done quickly, so it does not allow the 
animal suffering, in which the trachea, esophagus and both arteries and jugular vein must 


=== PAGINA 4/29 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR 
INDUSTRIALIZED FOOD AND FOOD ADDITIVE CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.1 
Rev.: 14 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:4 de 29 
 
be cut to hasten the bleeding and the animal´s death.  
The slaughter ritual shall be practiced ethically and with respect for the animals to be 
slaughtered, following the Islamic laws of the Holy Quran. One of them consists of the 
Tasmiyah, which consist in invoking the phrase Bismillah, Allahu Akbar (In the name of God, 
God is the greatest), immediately before the slaughter. Bismillah is one of the ways to seek 
God's help before any action to be performed by a Muslim. There is no text in Islamic 
jurisprudence that confirms exactly the meaning of the Tasmiyah, but Islamic scholars claim 
that it would be a way of thanking God for the food offered by Him, since the sacrifice of 
animals is performed to ensure the sustenance of human beings and not for fun or sadism. 
 
 
Haram  
The word Haram means illicit, impure, illegal. Refers to what is unacceptable, 
detrimental to the health of the human body and its morals, as determined in the Qur'an and 
Hadith (confirmation of the acts, affirmations, opinions and the way of Prophet Muhammad 
lived (Peace and blessings of Allaah be upon him). 
 
 
Mashbouh 
There is a large number of industrialized products that use additives and ingredients 
that can bring dangers to spiritual and physical health, as they belong to the Mashbouh 
(questionable/ doubtful) category, which are: 
Elements that can be from numerous sources of extraction, such as: 
a) Glycerin which may be of vegetable or animal origin; 
b) L-Cysteine a non-essential amino acid can be obtained from human hair, pork, 
duck feather, oil and from vegetable. 
Processed products of Halal origin, for example:  
a) Clarified fruit juice with pork;  
b) Products such as yogurt, biscuit, cereal bar, that uses gelatin and mono and 
diglycerides; 
c) Flavours with alcohol; 
d) Cheeses, monosodium glutamate (flavor enhancer) and other products 
fermented with enzymes; 
e) Among others.  
 


=== PAGINA 5/29 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR 
INDUSTRIALIZED FOOD AND FOOD ADDITIVE CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.1 
Rev.: 14 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:5 de 29 
 
 
Makruh 
Inappropriate. Not recommended (encouraged) according to Shariah. 
 
 
 
Najasah (Impurities)  
Najasah means everything that is considered impure, such as feces, urine, prostatic 
fluid, a discharge from the urethra, sperm, pus, vomit, and excrement of any animal whose 
meat is not allowed for consumption.  
 
 
Halal Mizen (weight/measure) 
Islam is concerned in protecting consumer rights by obtaining the correct measure 
and/or weight of the product. Therefore, any practice or process applied ineffectively 
constitutes a fraudulent act. The Holy Quran mentions: “Woe to those who give less [than 
due], Who, when they take a measure from people, take in full. But if they give by measure 
or by weight to them, they cause loss.” (Surah 83:1-3). 
Considering this, when we also talk about commercialization, we shall transpose the 
same concept of justice that is so strongly defended in Islam, as it is in the holy Quran: “O 
my people, give full measure and weight in justice and do not deprive the people of their due 
and do not commit abuse on the earth, spreading corruption.” (Surah 11:85). 
 
 
Halal Certificate 
It is a faithful guarantee document issued by a Halal certifying institution, recognized 
by Islamic countries, to attest that the company, process and products, follow the legal 
requirements and criteria determined by Shariah. 
 
 Genetically Modified Organisms (GMO) 
Organisms obtained by gene transfer from other living species to a plant, animal or 
micro-organism by genetic engineering technologies, thus altering the natural 
characteristics of that organism. 
 
 Food additives  
Any substance that it is not normally consumed as a direct food, or it is not commonly 
used as a component of a food, and it does not add nutritional value. The food additive shall

not aggregate or influence in existing properties when added to the product for technological 
reasons (including organic purposes) during manufacture, processing, preparation, 
packaging, transport or conservation of this food (directly or indirectly) or in its by-product. 
 
 Identification of the categories GSO 
The certified products are classified according to the categories presented in table 1 
– Gulf standard, according to GSO 2055-2. 
The Gulf countries referenced in this document are those that are part of the GCC 
(GCC Accreditation Center) and follow the standards established by the GSO. They are: 
Saudi Arabia, Oman, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Yemen and the United Arab Emirates.  
FAMBRAS Halal may operate in all categories indicated in the table, however, for the 
Gulf countries the recognition is applicable only for the categories marked in blue. For other 
categories, upon demand and necessity, FAMBRAS HALAL may request recognition.

 Identification of the categories SMIIC 
Certified products are classified according to the categories presented in table 2 - 
SMIIC 02:2019 standard. 
SMIIC stands for Standards and Metrology Institute for Islamic Countries, being an 
intergovernmental organization, that aims to establish common standards to be 
implemented throughout the region of OIC - Organization of the Islamic Cooperation, which 
aims to ensure consumer protection and interoperability of products, and also to strengthen 
the market position of OIC member states in the global market. 
The countries that are part of OIC are: Afghanistan, Albania, Azerbaijan, Bangladesh, 
United Arab Emirates, Brunei, Darussalam, Algeria, Indonesia, Morocco, Palestine, Gabon, 
Iraq, Iran, Cameroon, Qatar, Kyrgyzstan, Kuwait, Libya, Lebanon, Maldives, Malaysia, 
Egypt, Nigeria, Oman, Uzbekistan, Pakistan, Senegal, Somalia, Sudan, Syria, Saudi Arabia, 
Tajikistan, Tunisia, Turkey, Turkmenistan, Uganda, Jordan, Yemen, Bosnia and 
Herzegovina.

FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO

6. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR HALAL PRODUCTS MANUFACTURING 
 
 
The manufacturer shall demonstrate compliance with all legal issues applicable to 
the country and region; 
 
 
All FAMBRAS HALAL certified products, and its raw materials, process aids, inputs 
and packaging material shall be free of all Haram (non-Halal) elements described 
in table 3, and of any impurity (Najasah), see item 5.7.  
In the table of attachment 1 are listed some Halal critical points that shall be 
considered, for each product type.  
 
 
All products certified by FAMBRAS HALAL, and their raw materials, processing 
aids, supplies and packaging do not contain animal derivatives that have not been 
slaughtered in Halal (according to Shariah). 
 
 
All products certified by FAMBRAS HALAL and their respective raw materials, 
inputs, process aids, and packaging materials shall be guaranteed through their 
Halal status with official documents that demonstrate their legal origin, except 
when they origin is 100% natural, such as raw milk, honey, fruits, vegetables and 
others - as assessed by FAMBRAS Halal. 
6.4.1. There shall be a supplier approval process for the selection and approval of raw 
materials, inputs, process aids and packaging materials, in order to ensure the 
quality and integrity of the food, as well as its safety for Halal. 
 
 
All products certified by FAMBRAS HALAL and its raw materials, inputs, 
processing aids and packaging materials shall not contain toxic substances and 
dangerous pollutants that could be considered as harmful to health.

Table 3. Non-Halal Table of Products 
Species 
Non-Halal 
Animals: 
The animals that may not be slaughtered according to Islamic Rules, the 
slaughtered animals of polytheists, irreligious, seculars, atheists, Magis, apostates 
and other disbelievers other than Christians and Jews, dead animals, suffocated 
animals, fatally beaten animals (Mawqouza), falling animals (Motaradiah), butted 
animals (Nateehah), animals eaten by beast of prey and animals on whose 
slaughtering the name of Allah is not mentioned, the animals slaughtered on idols, 
the animals slaughtered not in approach of Allah, or the animals contaminated with 
prohibited animals of harm, if eaten. 
Pigs, domestic donkeys, mules, elephants, monkeys, dogs, foxes, cats and the like. 
Predators such as fanged beasts of prey and the like such as lions, cheetahs, 
bears, except for hyenas. 
Birds of prey with sharp claws used for attacking and the like such as eagles, 
falcons, hawks, sparrows, peregrines, kites and owls. 
Rodents, reptiles, pests and the like such as mice, rats, centipedes, serpents, 
snakes, monitors, lizards, geckos, chameleons, hedgehogs, martins, bats, 
squirrels, polecats, moles and Coleoptera (except for dabb lizards and jerboas) 
Scorpions and all types of insects, worms and animals prohibited to be killed by 
Rules and the like such as ants, bees, woodpeckers and hoopoes, except for 
locusts and unavoidable bee parts falling in honey. 
Odious creatures and the like such as shells, larva and other similar animals. 
Animals fed with unclean items, unless they are confined and fed with permissible 
feeding according to Islamic Rules for at least three days. 
Aquatic 
Toxicant or harmful species, unless toxicant or harmful substances are removed 
during preparation. 
Plantation: 
Plants, their products and their toxicant, harmful or narcotic derivatives, unless 
toxicant or harmful substances are removed during preparation. 
Beverages: 
Intoxicating beverages or those containing alcohols, narcotic, calming, toxicant or 
harmful substances. 
GMO: 
All elements produced by genetic modification from a prohibited specie or more 
than one species, of them one specie is prohibited. 
Blood and Blood 
Derivatives from a 
Human or Animal 
Blood and all its derivatives are prohibited and all body liquids from a human or 
animal origin such as vomiting and urine. 
Food Additives: 
All toxicant, harmful or calming additives, products and their products and 
derivatives or derived from non-Halal materials. 
Enzymes: 
All enzymes derived from non-Halal sources. 
Microorganisms: 
All microorganisms such as germs, fungi, toxicant and harmful yeast produced on 
non-Halal environments or using non-Halal ingredients. 
Source: GSO 2055-2 adapted. 
 
 
 


=== PAGINA 14/29 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR 
INDUSTRIALIZED FOOD AND FOOD ADDITIVE CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.1 
Rev.: 14 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:14 de 29 
 
 
 
All products certified by FAMBRAS HALAL and its raw materials, inputs, 
processing aids and packaging materials shall meet the microbiological and 
physical-chemical standards established for foods and shall contain only allowed 
substances and within the maximum limits established by current legislation. 
 
 
All raw materials, inputs, processing aids and packaging materials, used in certified 
products, shall be previously informed and approved by FAMBRAS HALAL. 
 
 
Ethyl alcohol / Ethanol: 
6.8.1. All products certified by FAMBRAS HALAL, and its raw materials, inputs, 
process aids or any other element that comes into direct contact with the Halal 
product are not / shall not contain alcoholic beverages (khamr), or derivatives 
physically separated from the alcoholic beverages. 
6.8.2. Products derived from the alcoholic beverage industry in solid form, such as 
brewer's yeast extract, can be used since they undergo a purification process 
according to Shariah, which consists of the complete removal of color, odor, 
and flavor of the alcoholic beverage. 
6.8.3. Ethyl alcohol (ethanol) can occur naturally in food, due to natural fermentation, 
however, it is necessary that these do not exceed the maximum limits of ethanol 
established by FAMBRAS HALAL, which are 0.1% for final product and 0.5% 
for raw materials (products that will not be consumed directly). The addition of 
alcoholic beverages, alcoholic products, and ethyl alcohol (ethanol) during 
manufacture is prohibited. 
6.8.3.1. For products destined for the Gulf (see 5.11), the limit shall meet the 
requirements of GSO 2538: 2017, transcribed in Table 4. 
6.8.4. In some cases, ethyl alcohol (ethanol) is indispensable in the manufacture, 
when is used as auxiliary / process additive. In this case, ethanol is allowed, 
since the source is not from alcoholic beverages (eg wine), and the final product 
does not contain more than 0,1% ethyl alcohol and 0,5% for raw materials (if 
they are not for direct consumption). 
6.8.4.1. For products destined for the Gulf (see 5.11), the limit shall meet the 
requirements of GSO 2538: 2017, transcribed in table 4 above.

The name of the final product, the brand, the logo, the sensory characteristics, and 
the model of the packaging do not infringe the principles of Shariah, for example 
the brand does not refer to Haram elements such as bacon, ham, beer, or any 
other that could confuse the consumer. 
 
 The Islamic rules shall be applied at all stages of the chain of products certified by 
FAMBRAS HALAL, including receipt, preparation, packaging, labeling, storage, 
and transportation; 
 
 The official authorities may take all necessary procedures to verify the conformity 
of products with the special requirements of Halal products and may take 
appropriate measures in accordance with local legislation. 
 
 All types of cleaning or maintenance products such as detergents, sanitizers, 
greases, and lubricants shall be previously informed and approved by FAMBRAS 
HALAL and shall be free of non-Halal components or materials (prohibited by 
Shariah). 
 


=== PAGINA 16/29 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR 
INDUSTRIALIZED FOOD AND FOOD ADDITIVE CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.1 
Rev.: 14 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:16 de 29 
 
 
 The weight of the Halal product declared on the product packaging cannot be less 
than its actual weight (see 5.8). 
6.13.1. 
The company shall carry out sample tests to measure the weight of Halal 
products, comparing the weight described on the packaging with the actual 
weight; 
6.13.2. 
In the case of non-compliant products, where the actual weight is less than the 
weight of the packaging, there shall be segregation, where the product will be 
condemned for sale as a Halal product. The company shall record the correction 
and corrective action in cases of non-conformities; 
6.13.3. 
There shall be daily records of these conferences. 
 
 
7. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR HALAL PRODUCTION AREAS 
 
 
All Halal products shall be completely separated from non-Halal products, at any 
and all stages of the manufacturing flow (receipt, preparation, packaging, labeling, 
storage, and transportation), to avoid cross-contamination. 
 
 
For units that manufacture Halal and non-Halal products: 
7.2.1. The equipment shall be sanitized before the start of Halal production, and the 
hygiene procedure is duly validated; 
7.2.2. It is recommended that the day of production begins with the manufacture of 
Halal products; 
7.2.3. It is necessary to identify the lines that process the Halal, in order to prevent 
cross contamination; 
7.2.4. It is not allowed to carry out the sanitization alternately between equipment that 
processes Halal and equipment that processes non-Halal products; 
7.2.5. The sampling practices shall ensure that cross-contamination of the Halal 
certified product or its raw materials, inputs and process aids does not occur 
with any non-Halal product. For example, use a sample collection utensil in a 
Halal product that was not properly sanitized, and was also previously used to 
collect some non-Halal product. 


=== PAGINA 17/29 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR 
INDUSTRIALIZED FOOD AND FOOD ADDITIVE CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.1 
Rev.: 14 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:17 de 29 
 
 
7.2.6. Validation tests may be carried out on the first Halal production following non-
Halal production, whether on the product and / or equipment, as determined by 
the certification body; 
7.2.7. Halal and non-Halal raw materials shall be segregated and identified. 
 
 
If the production lines have already been used to make products of pork origin, 
before converting it for the manufacture of Halal products it is necessary that the 
same goes through a ritual of purification, which consists of subjecting the 
equipment to the passage of water + cleaning agents such as detergent and 
sanitizer, at least 7 times, for the complete removal of color, taste, and aroma from 
the contaminant. 
7.3.1. If the certified company's standard procedure does not add up to 7 product passes 
(adding water, cleaning and rinsing), it shall be repeated as many times as 
necessary until totalization, or additional rinsings may be performed only with 
water, so that in open equipment, the uninterrupted flow of water occurs for at 
least 30 seconds, followed by total water evacuation. This procedure will be 
considered as 1 (one) rinse. 
7.3.2. The conversion of the lines between Halal production and swine shall not be 
carried out continuously. 
 
 
All FAMBRAS HALAL certified products containing meat, gelatin, or other 
elements of animal origin (from slaughtered animals) shall have a FAMBRAS 
HALAL supervisor working on site during Halal production, which may be fixed or 
per campaign. Other types of products may have the same obligation when the 
company manufactures Halal and non-Halal products, and they are identified as 
high risk for Halal, and this need will be defined by FAMBRAS HALAL. 
7.4.1. The supervisor shall be Muslim, mentally healthy, with at least 18 years old, and 
competent to perform his role efficiently, demonstrating knowledge and 
understanding of the Islamic fundamentals and requirements referring to Halal. 
 
 
All equipment, production lines, tools, utensils, and associated materials used for 
the preparation of products certified by FAMBRAS HALAL shall be clean and free

FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR

DT 7.1 
Rev.: 14 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:18 de 29 
 
 
of impurities and shall not be manufactured or contain any type of non-Halal 
material, such as bone, nail, or teeth (stainless steel is preferable). 
 
 
8. 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR STORAGE AND TRANSPORTATION OF 
HALAL PRODUCTS 
 
 
If the company has final products with different Halal status, the Halal products 
shall be properly segregated and identified according to it. 
8.1.1. For powder products stored in flexible packaging (such as flexible plastic 
packaging, raffia and kraft paper bag), there shall have a significant physical 
segregation (average of 50 cm) between products with different status, to 
ensure no cross contamination occur. When stored on shelves, the Halal 
products can not be placed below non-Halal products. 
8.1.2. For solid and liquid products stored in rigid packages (drums, cylinders, cans, for 
example) a physical segregation with a significant distance is not mandatory, 
however, Halal and non-Halal products should not lean against each other (even 
when already packaged in primary, secondary and palletized). It is highly 
recommended that Halal products when stored on shelves do not stay below non-
Halal products. 
8.1.3. The Halal certified company shall implement a hazard evaluation and take the 
appropriate procedure to determine what measures should be taken in case of non-
Halal packaging breakdown/ leakage in the final product warehouse, with the aim 
of ensuring no cross-contamination with the Halal product. 
8.1.4. Regarding the identification of products, it may be applied only on Halal products, 
being the Halal seal visible on the secondary packaging a sufficient form of 
identification. 
 
 
Preferably transport all FAMBRAS HALAL products certified from non-Halal 
products. 
8.2.1. For Islamic / Islamic-majority countries, mixed transport of Halal and non-Halal 
products is prohibited. 


=== PAGINA 19/29 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR 
INDUSTRIALIZED FOOD AND FOOD ADDITIVE CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.1 
Rev.: 14 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:19 de 29 
 
 
8.2.2. In non-Islamic countries, mixed transport of Halal and non-Halal products is 
permitted, since both have the same origin, for example Halal and non-Halal 
bovine canned meat or non-Halal apple juice with Halal grape juice, unless when 
there is a Halal import rule in the country that determines otherwise. In these 
cases, the Halal product shall contain individual identification, being sufficient to 
ensure that there is no exchange between Halal and non-Halal product. 
 
 
It is expressly prohibited to carry Halal products and products of pork origin and/ 
or their derivatives. 
 
 
The means of transport should be adequate to avoid cross-contamination with non-
Halal products, besides satisfying the conditions of hygiene. 
 
 
9. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR LABELING HALAL PRODUCTS 
 
 
The labeling of the Halal certified product shall comply with the relevant laws of the 
country of origin and, in the case of exportation, with the country of destination. 
 
 
The Halal seal shall be applied according to the instruction: IT 4.1 FAMBRAS 
HALAL SEAL USE INSTRUCTION. 
9.2.1. Every media that apply the Halal seal, such as packaging, advertising materials, 
reports and e-mail signatures, shall be previously approved by FAMBRAS 
HALAL. 
Note 1: To obtain this approval, it is mandatory that the client sends an e-mail to 
the auditor responsible for his/her process, with the materials arts that will apply 
the Halal seal, for evaluation and subsequent authorization of its use. 
9.2.2. Packaging and media can not use the FAMBRAS HALAL seal before obtaining 
the certification body approval. 
 
 
Advertising of the Halal product can not contradict the principles of Shariah, such 
as, showing elements considered indecent.

FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO

Advertising materials and packaging of Halal products shall not contain misleading 
information to the consumer, nor Haram elements. 
 
 
10. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR QUALITY AND SAFETY OF THE HALAL 
PRODUCT 
 
10.1.  The company shall have a Halal Guarantee System (HAS), as provided by DT 7.3. 
In the table of attachment 1 are listed some Halal critical points that shall be 
considered, for each product type.  
 
 The layout of the facilities allows: The proper flow of the process; Adequate flow of 
employees; Good Hygiene and Manufacturing Practices. 
 
 All products certified by FAMBRAS Halal and its raw materials, inputs and process 
aids shall be produced in suitable sanitary facilities. 
 
 Hygiene and food safety are prerequisites for the production of the Halal product, 
in this way the company shall have a Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) 
system, which contains at least: 
a) Sanitation of facilities, equipment, furniture and utensils; 
b) Water control; 
c) Hygiene and health of the manipulators; 
d) Waste management; 
e) Preventive maintenance and calibration of equipment; 
f) Integrated vector and pest control; 
g) Selection of raw materials, ingredients and packaging; 
h) Traceability and collection program. 
 
 The company shall have a Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Point (HACCP) 
program. 
 
 The recall program shall include communication with the Halal certification body,

as provided for in procedure PR 7.1 - Conditions for granting, maintenance, 
extension, reduction, suspension, cancellation, termination and renewal of halal 
certification. 
10.6.1. 
The recall program shall foresee the need to carry it out in the event of 
contamination that undermines the product Halal status.  
 
 Methodology for the treatment of deviations, complaints, and nonconformities 
(referring to internal and external audits), including the corrective actions taken to 
avoid future occurrences. 
 
 Nonconformities from previous process/ audits shall be resolved, and evidence 
presented whenever requested.

=== PAGINA 1/30 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO 
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE ALIMENTOS INDUSTRIALIZADOS E ADITIVOS  
DT 7.1 
Rev.: 14 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:1 de 30 
 
1. 
OBJETIVO 
Este documento estabelece os requisitos gerais para as empresas de alimentos 
industrializados e aditivos, os quais se enquadram nas categorias C1, C2, C3, C4 e K (GSO 
2055-2 e SMIIC 02), que certificarem-se Halal com a FAMBRAS HALAL.  
 
 
2. 
CAMPO DE APLICAÇÃO 
Este documento é aplicável a todas as empresas certificadas pela FAMBRAS HALAL 
de alimentos industrializados e aditivos, os quais se enquadram nas categorias C1, C2, C3, 
C4 e K (GSO 2055-2 e SMIIC 02). 
 
3. 
DOCUMENTOS DE REFERÊNCIA 
• GSO 2055-1:2015 – HALAL products - Part one General Requirements for 
HALAL Food. Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf countries; 
• GSO 2538:2017 - The maximum limits for residues of ethyl alcohol (ethanol) 
in food; Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf countries; 
• GSO 2500:2022 – Additives Permitted for Use in Food Stuffs – Gulf countries; 
• GSO 05/ FDS 995/ 2013 – Sweeteners Permitted in Food – Gulf countries; 
• GSO 707:2021 - Flavourings Permitted for Use in Food Stuffs – Gulf countries; 
• GSO 2500:2022/Amd1:2023 – Gulf countries; 
• GSO 9:2022 - Labeling of Prepackaged Food Stuffs – Gulf countries; 
• Malaysian Protocol for the Halal Meat and Poultry Productions, 2008; 
• MS 2200-1:2008 - Islamic Consumer Goods - Part 1 - Cosmetic and Personal 
Care General Guidelines; 
• MS 2200-2:2013 - Islamic Consumer Goods - Part 2: Usage of animal bone, 
skin and hair - General guidelines; 
• MS 2400-1:2018 - Halal supply chain management system - Part 1 - 
Transportation; 
• MS 2400-2:2018 - Halal supply chain management system - Part 2 -
Warehousing; 
• MS 2400-3:2018 - Halal supply chain management system - Part 3 - Retailing;  
• MS 2424:2012 - Halal Pharmaceuticals - General Guidelines;

=== PAGINA 3/30 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO 
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO

• CODEX STAN 192/195 – General Standard for Food Additives – CODEX 
ALIMENTARIUS 
 
 
4. 
DISPOSIÇÕES GERAIS 
Todos os alimentos industrializados e aditivos que almejam obter a certificação Halal 
da FAMBRAS HALAL para as categorias C1, C2, C3, C4 e K (GSO 2055-2 e SMIIC 02), 
devem atender aos requisitos estabelecidos neste documento.  
Neste documento, a palavra “deve” indica um requisito e a palavra “poderia”, uma 
recomendação. 
 
 
5. 
DEFINIÇÕES 
 
5.1. 
Shariah  
A palavra Shariah (jurisprudência islâmica) se refere a totalidade das ordens de Allah 
(Deus) que regulam todos os aspectos da vida de um muçulmano (MS 1900:2005), ou seja, 
tudo o que está descrito no alcorão sagrado, que fora as revelações de Deus ao profeta 
Muhammad (SAWS). Além do alcorão, a sunnah, ou seja, todas as práticas e ditos do 
profeta Muhammad (SAW) também fazem parte da shariah.  
 
5.2. 
Halal 
A palavra “Halal” significa lícito, o mesmo que permitido. Produtos designados Halal 
são aqueles os quais o consumo é permitido por Deus, de acordo com a Shariah.  
Halal é então a base de tudo o que é lícito, na alimentação, no social, nos atos 
praticados (conduta), na justiça, na vestimenta, nas finanças, etc., é um sistema baseado 
nos princípios e valores que beneficiem a humanidade.  
O Alcorão Sagrado nos ensina na Sura 5a, verso 4: "Foram-vos permitidas todas as 
coisas sadias”.  
 
5.3. 
Abate Halal (Tazkeya) 
O abate Halal é um ritual islâmico de degola que deve ser feito de forma rápida, de 
modo a não permitir o sofrimento do animal, no qual deve ser cortada a traqueia, o esôfago 


=== PAGINA 4/30 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO 
REQUISITOS GERAIS DA FAMBRAS HALAL PARA CERTIFICAÇÃO 
DE ALIMENTOS INDUSTRIALIZADOS E ADITIVOS  
DT 7.1 
Rev.: 14 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:4 de 30 
 
e ambas as artérias e a veia jugular para apressar o sangramento e a morte do animal.  
Este ritual deve ser praticado de forma ética e com respeito aos animais a serem 
abatidos, seguindo as leis islâmicas do Alcorão Sagrado. Uma delas consiste na Tasmiyah, 
ou seja, invocar a frase Bismillah, Allahu Akbar (Em nome de Deus, Deus é maior), 
imediatamente antes da degola. A Bismillah é uma das formas de buscar a ajuda de Deus 
antes de qualquer ação a ser realizada por um muçulmano. Não há na jurisprudência 
islâmica um texto que confirme exatamente o significado da Tasmiyah, mas eruditos 
islâmicos afirmam que seria uma forma de agradecer a Deus pelo alimento, já que o 
sacrifício dos animais é realizado para garantir o sustento dos seres humanos e não por 
diversão ou sadismo. 
 
5.4. 
Haram  
A palavra Haram significa ilícito, impuro, ilegal. Refere-se ao que é inaceitável, 
prejudicial à saúde do corpo humano e sua moral, conforme determinado no Alcorão e 
Hadith (confirmação dos atos, afirmações, opiniões e modo de vida do profeta Muhammad 
(Que a paz e as bênçãos de Allah estejam sobre ele). 
 
5.5. Mashbouh 
Existem inúmeros produtos industrializados que utilizam aditivos e ingredientes que 
podem trazer perigos a saúde espiritual e material, pois pertencem a categoria Mashbouh 
(questionável/ duvidoso), que são: 
Elementos que podem ser de inúmeras origens de extração, como: 
a) 
Glicerina que pode ser de origem vegetal ou animal; 
b) 
L-Cisteína um aminoácido não essencial pode ser obtido de cabelo humano, 
pelo de porco, pena de pato, petróleo e vegetal. 
Produtos de origem Halal processados, por exemplo:  
a) 
Suco de fruta clarificado com gelatina;  
b) 
Produtos como iogurte, biscoito, barra de cereal, que usam gelatina e mono 
e diglicerídeos;  
c) 
Aromas com álcool;  
d) 
Queijos, glutamato monossódico (realçador de sabor) e outros produtos 
fermentados com enzimas; 
e) 
Outros. 


=== PAGINA 5/30 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO

Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:5 de 30 
 
5.6. 
Makruh 
Inapropriado. Não recomendado (encorajado) de acordo com a Shariah. 
 
5.7. 
Najasah (Impurezas)  
Najasah significa tudo que é considerado impuro, como fezes, urina, fluido prostático, 
uma descarga da uretra, esperma, pus, vômito, e excrementos de qualquer animal cuja 
carne não é permitida para consumo.  
 
5.8. 
Halal Mizen (peso/ medida)  
O Islam se preocupa com a proteção dos direitos do consumidor buscando obter a 
medida e/ou peso correto no produto. Desta forma, qualquer prática ou processo aplicado 
de forma ineficaz, configura ato fraudulento. O Alcorão sagrado menciona: “Ai dos 
fraudadores, aqueles que, quando alguém lhes mede algo, exigem a medida exata/plena. 
Porém, quando eles medem ou pesam para os demais, fraudam-nos.” (Surata 83:1-3).  
Tendo isto em consideração, quando falamos também sobre comercialização, deve-
se transpor o mesmo conceito de justiça que é tão fortemente defendido no Islam, como 
está no sagrado alcorão: “Ó povo meu, disponde da medida e do peso com equidade; não 
defraudeis os humanos em seus bens e não pratiqueis a devassidão na terra, como 
corruptores.” (Surata 11:85). 
 
5.9. 
Certificado Halal 
É um documento fiel de garantia emitido por uma instituição certificadora Halal, 
reconhecida por países islâmicos, para atestar que a empresa, processo e produtos, 
seguem os requisitos legais e critérios determinados pela Shariah. 
 
5.10. Organismos geneticamente modificados (OGM) 
Organismos obtidos através da transferência de gene de outras espécies vivas para 
uma planta, animal ou microrganismo por tecnologias de engenharia genética, alterando 
assim as características natas daquele organismo. 
  
5.11. Aditivos alimentares  
Qualquer substância que normalmente não é consumida como alimento direto, não 
é comumente usada como componente de um alimento, e não agrega valor nutricional. O

aditivo alimentar não deve agregar propriedades ou influenciar nas propriedades já 
existentes, quando adicionado ao produto por razões tecnológicas (incluindo propósitos 
orgânicos) durante a fabricação, processamento, preparação, embalagem, transporte ou 
conservação deste alimento (de forma direta ou indireta) ou em seu subproduto. 
 
5.12. Identificação das categorias GSO 
Os produtos certificados são classificados conforme categorias apresentadas na 
tabela 1 – norma Golfo, conforme GSO 2055-2. Os países do Golfo, referenciados neste 
documento são aqueles que fazem parte da GAC (GCC Accreditation Center) e seguem os 
standards estabelecidos pelo GSO, sendo estes Arábia Saudita, Omã, Bahrein, Kuwait, 
Qatar, Iêmen e Emirados Árabes Unidos.  
A FAMBRAS Halal poderá atuar em todas as categorias indicadas na tabela, no 
entanto, para os países do Golfo o reconhecimento é aplicável somente para as categorias 
sinalizadas em azul, sendo para as demais categorias, mediante demanda e necessidade, 
a FAMBRAS HALAL poderá pleitear o reconhecimento.
