 | Tipo de auditoría: |  | Certificación Inicial/ Mantenimiento/ Renovación - Etapa 1 |  |  |  |  | 
 | Fecha de auditoría: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | Número de proceso: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2. Datos del equipo de auditoría: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | Auditor líder: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | Experto técnico: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | Auditor religioso: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | Observadores u otros: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3. Principales objetivos de la auditoría: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | • Evaluar el cumplimiento de la empresa con los requisitos Halal; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | • Contribuir al desarrollo de la empresa y del mercado certificado Halal. |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4. Norma de referencia: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | • DT 7.4 - Requisitos Generales para la certificación de productos FAMBRAS HALAL - Categoría K (Químicos en general y agentes de limpieza) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | · Describir las normas aplicables:
GSO 2055-1/2015, GSO 993/2015 - Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf countries
UAE UAE.S 2055-1/2015, UAE.S 993/2022 - MOIAT - United Arab Emirated
MS 1500/2009, MS 1900/2005, MS 2300/2009 - JAKIM Malaysian Standard
MUIS-HC-S001/2005, MUIS-HC-S002/2007 - MUIS - Singapore
KEPKABAN BPJPH - Number 20: 2023, PEPRES BPJPH - Number 6: 2023, KEPKABAN BPJPH - Number 77: 2023; KEPKABAN BPJPH - Number 78: 2023 – Indonesia, KMA BPJPH Number 748: 2021 - Indonesia
OIC/ SMIIC 1:2019, OIC/ SMIIC 22:2021 - OIC countries |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | • Legislaciones colombianas; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5. Obligaciones del auditado: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | • En caso de que el cliente opte por la inclusión de los países del Golfo, la auditoría de la Etapa 01 no debe, bajo ninguna circunstancia, realizarse el mismo día que la auditoría de la Etapa 02; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | • La empresa debe estar funcionando normalmente, fabricando productos Halal en el momento de la auditoría de la Etapa 02; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | • Proporcionar alimentos Halal (alimentos sin carne de cerdo y sus derivados y alimentos preparados con bebidas alcohólicas) al equipo auditor; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | • Proporcionar un espacio para que los auditores oren; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | • Respetar el tiempo de oración; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | • Proporcionar una sala aislada para la reunión del equipo auditor; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | • Proporcionar todo el Equipo de Protección Personal (EPP) requerido para los auditores y la seguridad alimentaria. |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | Auditor |  | Talla de ropa |  |  | Zapato |  | 
 | 6. Consideraciones: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | • La duración de la auditoría debe tener en cuenta el cálculo del tiempo de auditoría previamente, y por cada 1 hombre/día se consideran 8 horas, pudiendo durar 9 horas, ya que se acuerda previamente con el cliente; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | • La auditoría será realizada en español. |  |  |  |  |  |  |

 | 3.	Principais objetivos da auditoria: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | •	Avaliar a conformidade da empresa frente aos requerimentos Halal; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | •	Contribuir para o desenvolvimento da empresa certificada Halal. |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.	Normas de referência: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | •	DT 7.4 - Requisitos gerais da FAMBRAS HALAL para certificação de produtos - Categoria K (Químicos em geral e agentes de limpeza); |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | •Descreva as normas aplicáveis: 
GSO 2055-1/2015, GSO 993/2015 - Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf countries

 | •Legislações brasileiras. |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5. Obrigações do auditado: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | •No caso de o cliente optar para a Inclusão de países do Golfo, a auditoria de Estágio 01 não deverá, sob hipótese alguma, ser realizada no mesmo dia da auditoria de Estágio 02; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | •	A empresa deverá estar funcionando normalmente, fabricando produtos Halal, no momento da auditoria de Estágio 2; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | •	Fornecer alimentação Halal (alimentos isentos de suínos e seus derivados e alimentos preparados sem bebidas alcoólicas) para a equipe auditora; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | •	Fornecer um ambiente para os auditores rezarem; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | •	Respeitar o horário da reza; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | •	Fornecer uma sala isolada para a reunião da equipe auditora; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | •	Providenciar todos os EPI’s necessários para segurança dos auditores e dos alimentos: |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | Auditor |  | Tamanho de roupa |  |  | Calçado |  |

 | •	O idioma da auditoria será português. |  |  |  |  |  |  |

=== SHEET: Report - DETAILED (dimensoes: A2:I65) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO
 
INDUSTRIAL AUDIT REPORT – STAGE 1 (DT 7.4)
Category K |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.3
Rev.: 06
Date: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAJOR | NC - MINOR | OBS | NA | Evidences | 
 | 1. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR THE MANUFACTURE OF HALAL PRODUCTS |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1. The company has its products in accordance with the Halal requirements and a sanitary operating license in accordance to the legal issues of the country / region. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.1 and 6.11). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2. Through the documentary evaluation it is possible to confirm that the final product only contains raw-material, inputs,  processing aids and packaging materials, free of Haram and Najash (Impure) elements and animal derivatives not derived from Halal slaughter (DT 7.4 - Item 6.2 and 6.3). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.3. All raw materials, inputs, processing aids, and packaging materials, used on Halal certified products, were previously informed and approved by FAMBRAS HALAL, and have documentation that demonstrates their lawful origin in the face of Halal, ensuring its Halal Status (DT 7.4 - Item 6.4 and 6.7). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. The packaging materials used in Halal certified products have documented evidence of their atoxicity. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5. All raw materials, processing aids, inputs and packaging materials, have documentation that proves their compliance with physical-chemical standards and certify that they only have allowed substances and with the maximum imits established by the current legislation. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.6). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6. There is an approval and selection procedure for raw materials, inputs, processing aids and packaging materials suppliers, to ensure the food quality and integrity, as well its safety for Halal (DT 7.4 – Item 6.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.7. Ethyl alcohol, when necessary, is used only as an auxiliary / process additive, and there is documentation proving that it is not from the alcoholic beverage industry. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.8.1 and 6.8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8. If the company uses products derived from alcoholic beverage industries in solid form, such as brewer's yeast extract, there are records of the purification procedure carried out to completely eliminate the color, odor and flavor of the alcoholic beverage. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.8.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. Documental it is possible to verify that when the company uses ethyl alcohol (ethanol), as an auxiliary / process additive,  in the process of manufacturing the products present in the scope of certification OR this occurs naturally due to natural fermentation, the residual in the final product is controlled (DT 7.4 - item 6.8.3 and 6.8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.10. The name of the final product, the brand, the logo, the sensory characteristics and the model of the packaging do not infringe the principles of Shariah, for example, the brand does not refer to Haram elements such as bacon, ham, beer, or any other that could confuse the consumer (DT 7.4 - Item 6.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.11. From a general documentary assessment of the process by the religious auditor, it is possible to verify the respect of Islamic rules  throughout the production chain (receipt, preparation, packaging, labeling, storage and transportation) of the items certified by FAMBRAS HALAL (DT 7.4 – Item 6.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.12. All types of cleaning or maintenance products such as detergents, sanitizers, greases and lubricants, were previously informed and approved by FAMBRAS HALAL, and have documented evidence that they are free of non-halal components or materials (forbidden to the Shariah). (DT 7.4 - Item 6.12) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.    GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR HALAL PRODUCTION AREAS |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1. Evaluation only for units manufacturing Halal and non-Halal products (DT 7.4 - Item 7.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.1. The company predicts in its documents, as a hygiene procedure or the Halal Guarantee System (QMS / HAS), the obligation to clean the equipment before starting Halal production, as well as the validation of this procedure. (DT 7.4 - Item 7.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.2.  The company predicts in its documents, as a hygiene procedure or the Halal Guarantee System (QMS / HAS) that the hygiene equipment should not be performed alternately between equipment processing Halal and equipment processing non-Halal products (e.g. Performing an alternating CIP between equipment processing Halal and not Halal). (DT 7.4 - Item 7.2.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2. The company provides in its documents, as a hygiene procedure or the Halal Guarantee System (QMS / HAS) that if production lines have already been used to manufacture products of pork origin, before converting it to the manufacture of Halal products, it must undergo a purification ritual, which consists of subjecting the equipment to the passage of water + cleaning agents, such as detergent and sanitizer, at least 7 times, for the complete removal of color, taste and aroma of the contaminant to occur. (DT 7.4 - Item 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.3. The company provides in its documents, as a a hygiene procedure or the Halal Guarantee System (QMS / HAS), that the conversion of lines between Halal and swine production shall not be carried out continuously. (DT 7.4 - Item 7.3.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.4. If the company already manufactures Halal products containing meat, gelatin or other elements of animal origin (from slaughtered animals),  or identified as high risk for Halal, there is a Muslim and competent supervisor of FAMBRAS HALAL working on the spot during Halal production (DT 7.4 – Item 7.4 and 7.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.5. The company provides in its documents, as a a hygiene procedure or the Halal Guarantee System (QMS / HAS), that all the equipments, production lines, tools, utensils and materials used for Halal products preparation shall be clean and free of impurities,and shall not be made of or contain any type of non-Halal material. (DT 7.4 - Item 7.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.    GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR STORAGE AND TRANSPORTATION OF HALAL PRODUCTS |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1. The company is aware and documents in the Halal Assurance System (HAS) that all products certified by FAMBRAS HALAL shall be identified (being the Halal seal visible on the secondary packaging a sufficient form of identification), and, as applicable,  Halal products shall be separated from other non-Halal products during storage and transportation to prevent them from being mixed with non-Halal products.  (DT 7.4 – Item 8.1, 8.1.4 and 8.2 and DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2. The company documents in the Halal Assurance System (HAS) the evaluation of hazards and the appropriated procedure, with the measures that shall be taken, for example, in case of breakage/leakage of non-Halal product packages in the final product warehouse, to ensure no cross-contamination of the Halal product. (DT 7.4 – Item 8.1.3 and DT 7.3 – Item 11.1 and 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3. The company is aware and documents in the Halal Assurance System (HAS) that it is forbidden to carry Halal products together with products of pork origin and / or their derivative, to avoid cross contamination with Haram products. (DT 7.4 - Item 8.3 and DT 7.3 - Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.4. The company's transportation procedure provides the adequate controls for Halal safety, avoiding cross contamination with non-Halal products, and compliance with hygiene conditions (DT 7.4 - Item 8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.    GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR QUALITY AND SAFETY OF THE HALAL PRODUCTS |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.   The Good Manufacturing Practices system contains at least  (DT 7.4 – Item 10.4): |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.1. Quality management manual; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.2. Basic Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) requirements; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.3. Activities related to Health, Sanitation, Hygiene, Clothing and Conduct; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.4. Treatment for Complaints; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.5. Treatment for return; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.6. Self-Inspection / Internal Audit Program |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.7. Documentation and Records control |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.8. Personnel Management |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.9. Facilities Requirements |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.10. Water Installations and control |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.11. Auxiliary Area Requirements |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.12. Receiving and Storage Systematics |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.13. Sampling procedure |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.14. Production / Elaboration procedures |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.15. Quality Control Methodology |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.16. Retention Samples procedure |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2. The company has a traceability program of the Halal product (DT 7.4 – Item 10.4 and DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.7 and 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. The company has a recall program, which includes communication with the Halal certifier, and foresees the need to carry it out in the event of contamination that undermines the Halal status of the product. (DT 7.4 – Item 10.4 and 10.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4. The company has a risk analysis, that assures the product's quality, the employees’ safety, and avoids any cross contamination that jeopardizes the Halal status of the product. (DT 7.4 – Item 10.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. The company has a methodology for the treatment of deviations, complaints and nonconformities (referring to internal and external audits), including effective corrective actions, taken to avoid future occurrences (DT 7.4 – Item 10.7 and 10.8) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5. HALAL ASSURANCE SYSTEM (HAS) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1.  The company has a Halal Assurance System documented according to the internal condition of the company, being developed in a format compatible with the quality management system, and in order to ensure the Halal status of the certified products (DT 7.4 - Item 10.1 and DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 and 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2. The company establishes a Halal policy (whether or not it is linked to another company policy) that indicates its commitment with the production of Halal products, being this one duly signed by the top management of the company (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.1, 7.1 and 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3. There is a team responsible for Halal Management, which is presented in the Halal Guarantee System (QMS - HAS), where the responsibilities and autonomy are described. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.2, 8.1 and 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4. The team is composed of the necessary authorities (including the Halal supervisor, if applicable), that is directly involved on Halal production process, and has the autonomy to ensure the Halal condition of the products and to answer for the audit findings (DT 7.3 - Item 8.2 and 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5. The company has a planning for Halal training scheduled to take place at least once a year, and they are mandatory to all personnel involved in Halal process, including new wmployees (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 and 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. The content of Halal training covers the Halal, Haram and Mashbooh concept, the market that the company intends to reach out with the certification, and internal controls established to guarantee the certification. Furthermore, for employees involved in monitoring PCCHs, training includes the importance of their activity regarding the Halal safety. (DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 and 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. The Halal Assurance System (HAS) registration document describes the composition and flowchart of each of FAMBRAS HALAL certified products. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.4, 10.1 and 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8. The Hazards and Halal Critical Control Points are identified, validated and recorded in the company's HAS, through a risk matrix that provides an analysis of  the probability of occurrence of the hazard versus the severity of the occurrence of the hazard, taking into account cross-contamination with Haram products at each stage of the flow chart. (DT 7.3 - Item  6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 and 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. The Halal Assurance System (HAS) developed by the company predicts the need of the complete segregation of Halal and non-Halal raw materials and products, as well its identification, at any and all stages of production flow (receipt, preparation, packaging, labeling, storage and transportation) to avoid any cross-contamination. (DT 7.4 - Item 7.1, 7.2.3 and 7.2.7 and DT 7.3 - Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10. The preventive measures for the occurrence of risk and corrective measures in case of deviations are clearly defined in the company's HAS. As well as the procedures to be adopted for the management of Halal Critical Control Points are well documented, including monitoring records, those responsible and actions in case of deviations. (DT 7.3 - Item 11.4 and 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. The company describes in the registration document of the Halal Assurance System a methodology for HAS internal audit. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.6 and 12.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12. The HAS internal audit is planned (for the first year of certification) or has been carried out, at least once a year, or more often if necessary. (DT 7.3 - Item 12.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
=== SHEET: SCOPE AUDITED (dimensoes: A1:E142) ===

=== SHEET: Relatório DETALHADO (dimensoes: A2:I65) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO
 
RELATÓRIO DE AUDITORIA INDUSTRIAL – ESTÁGIO 1 (DT 7.4)
Categoria K |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.3
Rev.: 06
Data: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAIOR | NC - MENOR | OBS | NA | Evidências | 
 | 1.    REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA FABRICAÇÃO DE PRODUTOS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1. A empresa possui seus produtos em conformidade com os requisitos Halal e um alvará de funcionamento sanitário em conformidade com as questões legais do país/ região. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.1 e 6.11). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2. Através da avaliação da documentação é possível confirmar que o produto final só contém matérias-primas, insumos, auxiliares de processo e materiais de embalagem isentos de elementos Haram, Najash (Impuros) e derivados animais não provenientes do abate Halal (DT 7.4 – Item 6.2 e 6.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.3. Todas as matérias primas, auxiliares de processo, insumos e materiais de embalagem, usados nos produtos certificados Halal, foram previamente informados e aprovados pela FAMBRAS HALAL, e possuem documentação que demonstrem sua origem lícita diante do Halal, garantindo seu Status Halal (DT 7.4 - Item 6.4 e 6.7). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. Os materiais de embalagem usados nos produtos certificados Halal possuem documentação que comprovem sua atoxidade. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5. Todas as matérias primas, auxiliares de processo, insumos e materiais de embalagem,  possuem documentação que comprove sua conformidade com os padrões físico-químicos, e ateste que possuem apenas substâncias permitidas e dentro dos limites máximos estabelecidos pela legislação vigente. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.6). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6. Existe um procedimento para homologação e seleção de fornecedores de matérias primas, insumos, auxiliares de processo e materiais de embalagem, de forma a garantir a qualidade e integridade do alimento, bem como sua segurança para o Halal. (DT 7.4 – Item 6.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.7. Álcool etílico, quando necessário, é utilizado somente como auxiliar/ aditivo de processo, e  existe documentação que comprova que este não é proveniente de indústrias de bebidas alcoólicas. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.8.1 e 6.8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8. Caso a empresa utilize produtos derivados de indústrias de bebidas alcoólicas em forma sólida, como extrato de levedura de cerveja, há registros do procedimento de purificação realizado para eliminação completa da cor, odor e sabor da bebida alcoólica. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.8.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. Documentalmente é possível verificar que quando a empresa utilizar álcool etílico (etanol), como auxiliar/ aditivo de processo, no processo de fabricação dos produtos presentes no escopo de certificação OU este ocorrer naturalmente devido a fermentação natural, o residual no produto final é controlado. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.8.3 e 6.8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.10. O nome do produto final, a marca, o logo, as características sensoriais e o modelo da embalagem não infringem os princípios da Shariah, por exemplo a marca não faz alusão a elementos Haram, como bacon, presunto, cerveja, ou qualquer outro que possa confundir o consumidor. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.11. A partir de uma avaliação geral documental do processo, pelo auditor religioso, é possível verificar o respeito as regras islâmicas em toda a fase da cadeia de produção (recebimento, preparação, embalagem, rotulagem, armazenamento e transporte) dos itens certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.4 – Item 6.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.12. Todos os tipos de produtos de limpeza, ou manutenção, tais como detergentes, sanitizantes, graxas e lubrificantes, foram previamente informados e aprovados pela FAMBRAS HALAL, e possuem documentações que demonstram que são livres de componentes ou materiais não-Halal (proibidos para a Shariah). (DT 7.4 – Item 6.12) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.    REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA AS ÁREAS DE PRODUÇÃO HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1. Avaliação apenas para unidades que fabricam produtos Halal e não Halal (DT 7.4 - Item 7.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.1. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), a obrigatoriedade de limpeza dos equipamentos antes do início da produção Halal, bem como a validação deste procedimento.  (DT 7.4 - Item 7.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.2. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que a higienização dos equipamentos não deve ser realizada alternadamente entre equipamentos que processam Halal e equipamentos que processam produtos não Halal (Ex. Realizar um CIP alternado entre equipamentos que processam Halal e não Halal). (DT 7.4 - Item 7.2.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que se as linhas de produção já tiverem sido utilizadas para fabricar produtos de origem suína, antes de convertê-la para fabricação de produtos Halal, é necessário que a mesma tenha passado por um ritual de purificação, o qual consiste em submeter os equipamentos a passagem de água + agentes de limpeza, como detergente e sanitizante, no mínimo 7 vezes, para que ocorra a remoção completa de cor, sabor e aroma do contaminante. (DT 7.4 - Item 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.3. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que a conversão das linhas entre produção Halal e suína não deve ser realizada de forma contínua. (DT 7.4 - Item 7.3.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.4. Caso a empresa fabrique produtos Halal que contém carne, gelatina ou outros elementos de origem animal (proveniente de animais abatidos), ou identificados como alto risco para o Halal, existe um supervisor, muçulmano e competente, da FAMBRAS HALAL trabalhando no local durante a produção Halal (DT 7.4 – Item 7.4 e 7.4.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.5. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que todos os equipamentos, linhas de produção, ferramentas, utensílios e materiais  utilizados para a preparação de produtos Halal devem ser limpos e livres de impurezas, e não devem ser fabricados ou conter qualquer tipo de material não Halal. (DT 7.4 - Item 7.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.    REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA ARMAZENAGEM E TRANSPORTE DE PRODUTOS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1. A empresa está ciente e documenta no Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) que todos os produtos certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL devem estar identificados (sendo suficiente ter o selo visível na embalagem secundária do produto Halal) e, conforme aplicabilidade, separados de outros produtos não Halal durante o armazenamento e transporte, para prevenir que estes sejam misturados com produtos não Halal. (DT 7.4 – Item 8.1, 8.1.4, 8.2 e 8.3 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2. A empresa documenta no Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) a avaliação dos perigos e o procedimento adequado, com as medidas que devem ser praticadas, por exemplo em caso de rompimento/ vazamento de embalagens de produtos não Halal no armazém de produto final, para assegurar a não contaminação cruzada do produto Halal. (DT 7.4 – Item 8.1.3 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.1 e 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3. A empresa está ciente e documenta no Sistema de garantia Halal (HAS) que é proibido transportar junto produtos Halal e produtos de origem suína e/ ou seus derivados, para evitar contaminação cruzada com produtos Haram. (DT 7.4 – Item 8.4 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.4. O procedimento de transporte da empresa prevê os controles adequados para segurança Halal, evitando a contaminação cruzada com produtos não Halal, e o atendimento das condições de higiene (DT 7.4 – Item 8.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.   REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA QUALIDADE E SEGURANÇA DOS PRODUTOS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1. A sistemática de Boas Práticas de Fabricação contém no mínimo (DT 7.4 – Item 10.4): |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.1. Manual de gestão da qualidade; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.2. Requisitos básicos de Boas Práticas de Fabricação (BPF); |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.3. Atividades relacionadas a Saúde, Sanitização, Higiene, Vestuário e Conduta; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.4. Tratativa para Reclamações; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.5. Tratativa para Devolução |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.6. Programa de Auto Inspeção / Auditoria Interna |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.7. Controle de Documentação e Registros |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.8. Gestão de Pessoal |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.9. Requisitos das Instalações |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.10. Sistemas e Instalações de Água |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.11. Requisitos das Áreas Auxiliares |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.12.  Sistemática de Recebimento e Armazenamento |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.13.  Sistemática de Amostragem de Materiais |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.14. Sistemática de Produção/Elaboração |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.15. Metodologia de Controle da Qualidade |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.16. Sistemática para Amostras de Retenção |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2. A empresa possui um programa de rastreabilidade do produto Halal. (DT 7.4 – Item 10.4 e DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.7 e 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. A empresa possui um programa de recolhimento, o qual contempla a comunicação com a certificadora Halal, e preve a necessidade de realizá-lo mediante contaminação que desabone o status Halal do produto. (DT 7.4 – Item 10.4 e 10.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4. A empresa possui uma análise de risco, que garante a qualidade do produto, a segurança dos manipuladores e evita qualquer contaminação cruzada que coloque em risco o status Halal do produto. (DT 7.4 – Item 10.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. A empresa possui uma metodologia de tratamento de desvios, reclamações e não-conformidades (referentes a auditorias internas e externas), incluindo ações corretivas eficazes, adotadas para evitar ocorrências futuras. (DT 7.4 – Item 10.7 e 10.8) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.    SISTEMA DE GARANTIA HALAL/ HALAL ASSURANCE SYSTEM (HAS) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1.  A empresa possui um Sistema de Garantia Halal documentado de acordo com a condição interna da empresa, sendo este desenvolvido em um formato compatível com o sistema de gestão da qualidade, e de forma a garantir o status Halal dos produtos certificados (DT 7.4 - Item 10.1 e DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 e 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2.  A empresa estabelece uma política Halal (seja ela interligada ou não com outra política da empresa) que indique o seu compromisso com a produção de produtos Halal, estando esta devidamente assinada pela alta direção da empresa. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.1, 7.1 e 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3. Existe uma equipe responsável pela Gestão Halal, sendo esta apresentada no Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ – HAS), onde estão descritas as devidas responsabilidades e autonomias. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.2, 8.1 e 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4.  A equipe é composta pelas autoridades necessárias (incluindo o supervisor Halal, se aplicável), que estão diretamente envolvidas no processo de produção Halal, e possui autonomia para assegurar a condição Halal dos produtos e responder pelos apontamentos encontrados durante as auditorias (DT 7.3 – Item 8.2 e 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5. A empresa possui um planejamento para realização de treinamentos Halal programados para ocorrer no mínimo uma vez ao ano, e os mesmos são obrigatorios para todas as pessoas envolvidas no processo Halal, incluindo novos funcionários. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 e 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. O conteúdo dos treinamentos Halal contemplam o conceito Halal, Haram e Mashbooh, mercado que a empresa pretende atingir com a certificação, e controles internos estabelecidos para garantir a certificação. Ainda, para os  funcionários envolvidos no monitoramento dos PCCH, os treinamentoss incluem a importância da sua atividade diante da segurança do Halal. (DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 e 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. O documento de registro do Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) descreve a composição e o fluxograma de cada um dos produtos certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.4, 10.1 e 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8. Os Perigos e Pontos Críticos de Controle Halal são identificados, validados e registrados no HAS da empresa, através de uma matriz de risco que proporciona a análise da probabilidade de ocorrência do perigo versus a severidade da ocorrência do perigo, levando em consideração a contaminação cruzada com produtos Haram em cada etapa do fluxograma. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 e 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. O Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) elaborado pela empresa prevê a necessidade de separação completa entre as matérias primas e produtos Halal e não Halal, bem como sua identificação, em toda e qualquer etapa do fluxo de fabricação (recebimento, preparação, embalagem, rotulagem, armazenamento e transporte), a fim de evitar qualquer contaminação cruzada (DT 7.4 - Item 7.1, 7.2.3 e 7.2.7 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10. As medidas preventivas para a ocorrência do risco e as medidas corretivas em caso de desvios estão claramente definidas no HAS da empresa. Bem como as tratativas a serem adotadas para gestão dos Pontos Críticos de Controle Halal estão bem documentadas, incluindo os registros dos monitoramentos, os responsáveis e as ações no caso de desvios.  (DT 7.3 – Item 11.4 e 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. A empresa descreve no documento de registro do Sistema de Garantia Halal uma metodologia para auditoria interna HAS. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.6 e 12.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12. A auditoria interna do HAS está prevista (para o primeiro ano de certificação) ou foi realizada, pelo menos uma vez ao ano, ou mais vezes, se necessário. (DT 7.3 – Item 12.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  |

=== SHEET: Informe- DETALLADO (dimensoes: A2:I65) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS CERTIFICACIÓN HALAL 
 
INFORME DE AUDITORIA INDUSTRIAL – ETAPA 1 (DT 7.4)
Categoría K |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.3
Rev.: 06
Fecha: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAYOR | NC - MENOR | OBS | NA | Evidencias | 
 | 1.    REQUISITOS GENERALES PARA LA FABRICACIÓN DE PRODUCTOS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1.  La empresa tiene sus productos conformes con los requisitos Halal y una licencia de funcionamiento sanitaria que se ajusta a las cuestiones legales del país/región. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.1 y 6.11). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2. Mediante la evaluación de la documentación, es posible confirmar que el producto final contiene únicamente materias primas, insumos, auxiliares de proceso y materiales de envasado libres de elementos Haram, Najash (Impuros) y derivados animales no procedentes del sacrificio Halal (DT 7.4 – Item 6.2 y 6.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.3. Todas las materias primas, auxiliares de proceso, insumos y materiales de envase utilizados en los productos con certificación Halal han sido previamente informados y aprobados por FAMBRAS HALAL, y cuentan con documentación que demuestra su origen lícito ante Halal, garantizando su Estatus Halal (DT 7.4 - Item 6.4 y 6.7). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. Los materiales de empaque utilizados en los productos con certificación Halal disponen de documentación que demuestra su atoxicidad (DT 7.4 - Item 6.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5. Todas las materias primas, auxiliares de proceso, insumos y materiales de envasado cuentan con documentación que acredita su conformidad con las normas microbiológicas y físico-químicas, y que demuestran que sólo contienen sustancias permitidas y dentro de los límites máximos establecidos por la legislación vigente. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.6). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6.  Existe un procedimiento para la aprobación y selección de proveedores de materias primas, insumos, auxiliares de proceso y materiales de envasado, con el fin de garantizar la calidad e integridad de los alimentos, así como su inocuidad para Halal. (DT 7.4 – Item 6.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.7. El alcohol etílico, cuando es necesario, sólo se utiliza como auxiliar/aditivo del proceso, y existe documentación que demuestra que no procede de industrias de bebidas alcohólicas (DT 7.4 - Item 6.8.1 e 6.8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8. Si la empresa utiliza productos derivados de las industrias de bebidas alcohólicas en forma sólida, como el extracto de levadura de cerveza, existen registros del procedimiento de purificación llevado a cabo para eliminar por completo el color, el olor y el sabor de la bebida alcohólica (DT 7.4 - Item 6.8.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. Se puede demostrar documentalmente que cuando la empresa utiliza alcohol etílico (etanol) como auxiliar/aditivo en el proceso de fabricación de los productos en el alcance de la certificación O este ocurre naturalmente debido a la fermentación natural, el residuo en el producto final es controlad. (DT 7.4 - Item 6.8.3 y 6.8.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.10. El nombre del producto final, la marca, el logotipo, las características sensoriales y el modelo de empaque no infringen los principios de la Sharia, por ejemplo, la marca no alude a elementos Haram como el tocino, el jamón, la cerveza o cualquier otra cosa que pueda confundir al consumidor (DT 7.4 - Item 6.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.11. . A partir de una evaluación documental general del proceso por parte del auditor religioso, es posible verificar el cumplimiento de las normas islámicas en todas las fases de la cadena de producción (recepción, preparación, envasado, etiquetado, almacenamiento y transporte) de los artículos certificados por FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.4 – Item 6.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.12. Todo tipo de productos de limpieza o mantenimiento, como detergentes, sanitizantes, grasas y lubricantes, han sido previamente informados y aprobados por FAMBRAS HALAL, y cuentan con documentación que demuestra que están libres de componentes o materiales no Halal (prohibidos por la Sharia) (DT 7.4 – Item 6.12) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.    REQUISITOS GENERALES PARA LAS ÁREAS DE PRODUCCIÓN HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.  Evaluación únicamente para las plantas que procesan productos Halal y no Halal (DT 7.4 - Item 7.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.1. Los documentos de la empresa, como el procedimiento de higienización o el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/ HAS), estipulan que los equipos deben limpiarse antes de iniciar la producción halal, y que este procedimiento debe validarse   (DT 7.4 - Item 7.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.2. La empresa establece en sus documentos, como procedimiento de higienización o del Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/ HAS), que la limpieza de los equipos no debe realizarse alternativamente entre equipos que procesan productos Halal y equipos que procesan productos no Halal (por ejemplo, realizar un CIP alternativo entre equipos que procesan productos Halal y no Halal) (DT 7.4 - Item 7.2.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2. La empresa prevé en sus documentos, como procedimiento de limpieza o del Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/ HAS), que si las líneas de producción ya han sido utilizadas para fabricar productos porcinos, antes de convertirlas para fabricar productos Halal, es necesario que hayan sido sometidas a un ritual de purificación, que consiste en someter el equipo a agua + agentes de limpieza, como detergente e higienizante, al menos 7 veces, de forma que se elimine completamente el color, sabor y aroma del contaminante. (DT 7.4 - Item 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.3. Los documentos de la empresa, como los procedimientos de limpieza o el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/ HAS), establecen que la conversión de líneas entre la producción halal y la porcina no debe realizarse de forma continua (DT 7.4 - Item 7.3.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.4. Si la empresa fabrica productos Halal que contienen carne, gelatina u otros elementos de origen animal (procedentes de animales sacrificados), o identificados como de alto riesgo para el Halal, hay un supervisor musulmán competente de FAMBRAS HALAL trabajando in situ durante la producción Halal. (DT 7.4 – Item 7.4 e 7.4.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.5.La empresa estipula en sus documentos, como el procedimiento de higienización o el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/ HAS), que todos los equipos, líneas de limpieza, herramientas, utensilios y materiales utilizados para la elaboración de productos Halal deben estar limpios y libres de impurezas, y no deben estar fabricados ni contener ningún tipo de material no Halal. (DT 7.4 - Item 7.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.    REQUISITOS GENERALES PARA EL ALMACENAMIENTO Y TRANSPORTE DE LOS PRODUCTOS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1. La empresa es consciente y documenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) que los productos certificados por FAMBRAS HALAL deben estar identificados (basta con que el sello sea visible en el envase secundario del producto Halal) y, en su caso, separados de otros productos no Halal durante su almacenamiento y transporte, para evitar que se mezclen con productos no Halal (DT 7.4 – Item 8.1, 8.1.4, 8.2 y 8.3 y DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2. La empresa documenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) la evaluación de riesgos y el procedimiento adecuado, con las medidas que deben adoptarse, por ejemplo, en caso de rotura/filtración de envases de productos no Halal en el almacén de productos finales, para garantizar que el producto Halal no sufra contaminación cruzada  (DT 7.4 – Item 8.1.3 y DT 7.3 – Item 11.1 y 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3. La empresa es consciente y documenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) que está prohibido transportar juntos productos Halal y productos de origen porcino y/o sus derivados, para evitar la contaminación cruzada con productos Haram. (DT 7.4 – Item 8.4 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.4. El procedimiento de transporte de la empresa incluye los controles adecuados para garantizar la inocuidad Halal, evitar la contaminación cruzada con productos no Halal y cumplir las condiciones de higiene (DT 7.4 – Item 8.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.   REQUISITOS GENERALES PARA LA CALIDAD E INOCUIDAD DE PRODUCTOS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1. El sistema de Buenas Prácticas de Manufactura contiene como mínimo (DT 7.4 – Item 10.4): |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.1. Manual de Gestión de la Calidad; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.2. Requisitos básicos de Buenas Prácticas de Manufactura (BPM); |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.3. Actividades relacionadas con la salud, el saneamiento, la higiene, la vestimenta y la conducta; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.4. Gestión de reclamaciones; |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.5. Tratamiento de las devoluciones |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.6. Programa de Auto Inspección / Auditoria Interna |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.7. Control de la Documentación y  Registros |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.8. Gestión del Personal |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.9. Requisitos de las instalaciones |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.10. Sistemas e Instalaciones de Agua |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.11. Requisitos de las Áreas Auxiliares |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.12.  Metodología para la Recepción y Almacenamiento |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.13.  Metodología para el muestreo de materiales |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.14. Metodología de Producción/Elaboración |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.15. Metodología para el Control de Calidad |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.16. Metodología para las muestras de Retención |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2. La empresa posee un programa de trazabilidad del producto Halal. (DT 7.4 – Item 10.4 e DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.7 y 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. La empresa cuenta con un programa de retirada del mercado, que incluye la comunicación con el certificador Halal, y prevé la necesidad de llevarla a cabo en caso de contaminación que altere el estatus Halal del producto. (DT 7.4 – Item 10.4 y 10.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4. La empresa cuenta con un análisis de riesgos, que garantiza la calidad del producto, la seguridad de los manipuladores y evita cualquier contaminación cruzada que pueda poner en peligro el estatus Halal del producto. (DT 7.4 – Item 10.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. La empresa cuenta con una metodología para gestionar las desviaciones, reclamaciones y no conformidades (relativas a auditorías internas y externas), incluidas las medidas correctivas eficaces adoptadas para evitar que se produzcan en el futuro. (DT 7.4 – Item 10.7 e 10.8) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.    SISTEMA DE GARANTÍA HALAL/ HALAL ASSURANCE SYSTEM (HAS) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1.  La empresa dispone de un Sistema de Garantía Halal documentado de acuerdo con las condiciones internas de la empresa, que se ha desarrollado en un formato compatible con el sistema de gestión de la calidad y de forma que garantice el estatus Halal de los productos certificados (DT 7.4 - Item 10.1 y DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 y 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2.  La empresa establece una política Halal (relacionada o no con otra política de la empresa) que indica su compromiso con la fabricación de productos Halal, debidamente firmada por la alta dirección de la empresa (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.1, 7.1 ye 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3.  Existe un equipo responsable de la Gestión Halal, que se presenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC - HAS), donde se describen las responsabilidades y autonomías correspondientes (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.2, 8.1 e 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4.  El equipo está formado por las autoridades necesarias (incluido el supervisor Halal, si aplica), que participan directamente en el proceso de producción Halal, y tiene autonomía para garantizar el estatus Halal de los productos y responder a los hallazgos encontrados durante las auditorías.(DT 7.3 – Item 8.2 e 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5. La empresa cuenta con un cronograma de capacitación Halal al menos una vez al año, obligatorias para todas las personas implicadas en el proceso Halal, incluidos los nuevos empleados (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 y 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. El contenido de los programas de capacitación Halal aborda el concepto de Halal, Haram y Mashbooh, el mercado al que la empresa pretende llegar con la certificación y los controles internos establecidos para garantizar la certificación. Además, para los empleados que participan en el control del PCCH, la capacitación incluye la importancia de su actividad en términos de inocuidad Halal. (DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 y 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. El documento de registro del Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) describe la composición y el diagrama de flujo de cada uno de los productos certificados por FAMBRAS HALAL (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.4, 10.1 y 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8.Los peligros y puntos críticos de control Halal se identifican y registran en el HAS de la empresa utilizando una matriz de riesgo que analiza la probabilidad de que se produzca el evento frente a la gravedad de que se produzca, teniendo en cuenta la contaminación cruzada con productos Haram en cada etapa del diagrama de flujo del proceso. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 y 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. El Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) elaborado por la empresa estipula la necesidad de una separación completa entre las materias primas y los productos Halal y no Halal, así como su identificación, en todas y cada una de las etapas del flujo de fabricación (recepción, preparación, envasado, etiquetado, almacenamiento y transporte), con el fin de evitar cualquier contaminación cruzada (DT 7.4 - Item 7.1, 7.2.3 y 7.2.7 y DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10.  Las medidas preventivas para la ocurrencia de riesgos y las medidas correctivas en caso de desviaciones están claramente definidas en la HAS de la empresa. Los procedimientos que deben adoptarse para gestionar los puntos críticos de control Halal están bien documentados, incluidos los registros de seguimiento, los responsables y las acciones en caso de desviaciones  (DT 7.3 – Item 11.4 y 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. La empresa describe en el documento de registro del Sistema de Garantía Halal una metodología para la auditoría interna del HAS (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.6 y 12.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12.  La auditoría interna del HAS está prevista (para el primer año de certificación) o se ha realizado al menos una vez al año, o con mayor frecuencia si es necesario (DT 7.3 – Item 12.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  |

=== PAGINA 1/33 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FEED 
PRODUCTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.9 
Rev.: 07 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:1 de 33 
 
1. OBJECTIVE 
This document establishes the general requirements of FAMBRAS HALAL, for 
certification of Feed production companies, category D (GSO 2055-2 and SMIIC 02). 
 
 
2. APPLICATION FIELD  
This document applies to all FAMBRAS HALAL certified companies of categories D 
(GSO 2055-2 and SMIIC 02) (Livestock feed and livestock feed produce production). 
 
 
3. REFERENCE DOCUMENTS 
• GSO 2055-1:2015 – HALAL products - Part one General Requirements for HALAL 
Food. Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf; 
• GSO 2538:2021 - The maximum limits for residues of ethyl alcohol (ethanol) in food; 
Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf; 
• GSO CAC/GL 81:2015 - Guidance for governments on prioritizing hazards in 
livestock feed; 
• GSO 2142:2011 - General Requirements for Genetically Modified Processed Food 
and Livestock feed; 
• GSO 716:1997 - Animal livestock feeding stuffs - soy bean meal; 
• GSO 1072:2002 - Raw livestock feedstuffs, livestock feed additives and prepared 
livestock feed for poultry and farm animals; 
• GSO CAC RCP 54:2007 - Recommended code of practice on good animal livestock 
feeding. 
• GSO 2578:2021 – Halal Feedstuff; 
• MS 1500:2019 HALAL food - production, preparation, handling and storage - general 
guidelines. JAKIM Maylasian Standard – Malasya; 
• MS 1500:2009 HALAL food - production, preparation, handling and storage - general 
guidelines. JAKIM Maylasian Standard – Malasya; 
• MS 2200-2:2013 - Islamic Consumer Goods - Part 2: Usage of animal bone, skin and 
hair - General guidelines; 


=== PAGINA 2/33 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FEED 
PRODUCTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.9 
Rev.: 07 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:2 de 33 
 
• MS 2400-1:2018 - Halal supply chain management system - Part 1 - Transportation; 
• MS 2400-2:2018 - Halal supply chain management system - Part 2 -Warehousing; 
• MS 2400-3:2018 - Halal supply chain management system - Part 3 - Retailing;  
• MS 2565:2014 - Halal Packaging - General Guidelines. 
• KEPKABAN BPJPH - Number 20: 2023 - Decree of the Head of Halal Product 
Assurance Organizing Agency – Indonesia; 
• KMA BPJPH Number 748: 2021 - Products that must be certified Halal – Indonesia; 
• MUIS-HC-S001:2005 General Guidelines for the Handling and Processing of Halal 
Food. MUIS - Singapura. 
• OIC/ SMIIC 1:2019 General Requirements for Halal Food; 
• IT 4.1 – Instruction for using the FAMBRAS HALAL seal; 
• ISO/TS 22002-6:2016 - Prerequisite programmes on food safety — Part 6: Livestock 
feed and livestock feed produce production; 
• DECREE No. 6,296, OF DECEMBER 11, 2007; 
• NORMATIVE INSTRUCTION No. 54, OF DECEMBER 17, 2018. 
 
 
4. 
GENERAL PROVISIONS  
All products that aspire to obtain the Halal certification from FAMBRAS HALAL and 
fit into the category D (GSO 2055-2 and SMIIC 02), Livestock feed and feed produce, shall 
meet the requirements set out in this document. 
In this document, the word “shall” indicates a requirement and the word “should”, a 
recommendation. 
 
 
5. 
DEFINITIONS 
 
5.1. 
Shariah  
The word Shariah (Islamic jurisprudence) refers to the totality of the orders of Allah 
(God) that regulate all aspects of the life of a Muslim (MS 1900:2005), meaning, everything 
that is described in the holy Quran, that aside the God's revelations to Prophet Muhammad 


=== PAGINA 3/33 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FEED 
PRODUCTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.9 
Rev.: 07 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:3 de 33 
 
(SAWS). In addition to the Qur'an, the Sunnah, i.e. all practices and sayings of the Prophet 
Muhammad (SAW) are also part of the Shariah.  
 
5.2. 
Halal 
The word 'Halal' means lawful, the same as allowed. Halal designated products are 
those which consumption is allowed by God, according to Shariah.  
Halal is then the basis of all that is lawful, in food, in social life, in acts practiced 
(conduct), in justice, in clothes, in finances, etc., it is a system based on the principles and 
values that benefit the humanity.  
The Holy Quran teaches us in 5th Surah, 4th verse: "Lawful for you are all good 
foods". 
 
5.3. 
Halal slaughter (Tazkeya) 
Halal slaughter is an Islamic ritual that shall be done quickly, so it does not allow the 
animal suffering, in which the trachea, esophagus and both arteries and jugular vein shall 
be cut to hasten the bleeding and the animal´s death.  
The slaughter ritual shall be practiced ethically and with respect for the animals to be 
slaughtered, following the Islamic laws of the Holy Quran. One of them consists of the 
Tasmiyah, which consist in invoking the phrase Bismillah, Allahu Akbar (In the name of God, 
God is the greatest), immediately before the slaughter. Bismillah is one of the ways to seek 
God's help before any action to be performed by a Muslim. There is no text in Islamic 
jurisprudence that confirms exactly the meaning of the Tasmiyah, but Islamic scholars claim 
that it would be a way of thanking God for the food offered by Him, since the sacrifice of 
animals is performed to ensure the sustenance of human beings and not for fun or sadism. 
 
5.4. 
Haram  
The word Haram means illicit, impure, illegal. Refers to what is unacceptable, 
detrimental to the health of the human body and its morals, as determined in the Qur'an and 
Hadith (confirmation of the acts, affirmations, opinions and the way of Prophet Muhammad 
lived (Peace and blessings of Allaah be upon him). 
 
 


=== PAGINA 4/33 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FEED 
PRODUCTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.9 
Rev.: 07 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:4 de 33 
 
5.5. 
Mashbouh 
There is a large number of industrialized products that use additives and ingredients 
that can bring dangers to spiritual and physical health, as they belong to the Mashbouh 
(questionable/ doubtful) category, which are: 
Elements that can be from numerous sources of extraction, such as: 
a) Glycerin which may be of vegetable or animal origin; 
b) L-Cysteine a non-essential amino acid can be obtained from human hair, pork, 
duck feather, oil and from vegetable. 
Processed products of Halal origin, for example:  
a) Clarified fruit juice with pork;  
b) Products such as yogurt, biscuit, cereal bar, that uses gelatin and mono and 
diglycerides; 
c) Flavours with alcohol; 
d) Cheeses, monosodium glutamate (flavor enhancer) and other products 
fermented with enzymes; 
e) Among others.  
 
5.6. 
Makruh 
Inappropriate. Not recommended (encouraged) according to Shariah. 
 
5.7. 
Najasah (Impurities)  
Najasah means everything that is considered impure, such as feces, urine, prostatic 
fluid, a discharge from the urethra, sperm, pus, vomit, and excrement of any animal whose 
meat is not allowed for consumption.  
 
5.8. 
Halal Mizen (weight/ measurement) 
Islam cares about the protection of consumer rights by seeking to obtain the right 
measure and/or weight in the product. This way, any practice or process applied 
ineffectively, configures fraudulent act. The holy Quran mentions: "Woe unto fraudsters, 
those who, when one measures them something, require the exact/full measure. But when 
they measure or weigh on others, they defraud them." (Sura 83:1-3).  
With this in mind, when we also talk about commercialization, we should transpose 


=== PAGINA 5/33 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FEED 
PRODUCTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.9 
Rev.: 07 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:5 de 33 
 
the same concept of justice that is so strongly defended in Islam, as it is in the holy Quran: 
"Oh my people, have measure and weight with equity; do not defraud humans in their goods, 
and do not practice debauchery on earth as corruptors." (Sura 11:85). 
 
5.9. 
Halal Certificate 
It is a faithful guarantee document issued by a Halal certifying institution, recognized 
by Islamic countries, to attest that the company, process and products, follow the legal 
requirements and criteria determined by Shariah. 
 
5.10. Genetically Modified Organisms (GMO) 
Organisms obtained by gene transfer from other living species to a plant, animal or 
micro-organism by genetic engineering technologies, thus altering the natural 
characteristics of that organism. 
 
 
5.11. Livestock feed 
Any single or multiple product, processed, semi-processed or raw, intended for 
livestock feeding animals involved in food production. 
 
5.12. Livestock feed produce  
Any single or multiple product, processed, semi-processed or raw, intended for food 
animals not involved in food production. 
 
5.13. Halal feed products 
Feeding materials or their inputs that are allowed raw materials or prepared in 
accordance with the provisions of Islamic Sharia. 
 
5.14. Livestock feed additive:  
Any ingredient added intentionally, usually not consumed as livestock feed by itself, 
with or without nutritional value, which affects the characteristics of feed produce or livestock 
feed. Microorganisms, enzymes, acidity regulators, trace elements, vitamins and other 
products fall within the scope of this definition, depending on the purpose of use and the 
method of administration. 


=== PAGINA 6/33 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FEED 
PRODUCTION CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.9 
Rev.: 07 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:6 de 33 
 
5.15. Livestock feed ingredient:  
A component or component of any combination or mixture that makes up a food, with 
or without nutritional value in the animal's diet, including food additives. The ingredients are 
of vegetable, animal or aquatic origin or other organic or inorganic substances. 
 
5.16. Animal waste 
Animal waste can be subdivided into animal farm waste, waste from large animal 
slaughterhouses, and waste from poultry slaughterhouse. Being the waste from animal farm: 
poultry, cow, and sheep manure. The waste from large animal slaughterhouses: complete 
carcasses or parts of them, or their organs unfit for human consumption, skins, hair, wool, 
horns, hooves, ruminal and intestinal contents, bladder, uterus, dead animals, animal 
manure in slaughterhouses. And the waste from poultry slaughterhouse: complete 
carcasses or parts of them, or their organs unfit for human consumption, dead poultry. 
 
5.17. Identification of the categories GSO 
The certified products are classified according to the categories presented in table 1 
– Gulf standard, according to GSO 2055-2. 
The Gulf countries referenced in this document are those that are part of the GCC 
(GCC Accreditation Center) and follow the standards established by the GSO. They are: 
Saudi Arabia, Oman, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Yemen and the United Arab Emirates.  
FAMBRAS Halal may operate in all categories indicated in the table, however, for the 
Gulf countries the recognition is applicable only for the categories marked in blue. For other 
categories, upon demand and necessity, FAMBRAS HALAL may request recognition.

5.18. Identification of the categories SMIIC 
Certified products are classified according to the categories presented in table 2 - 
SMIIC 02:2019 standard. 
SMIIC stands for Standards and Metrology Institute for Islamic Countries, being an 
intergovernmental organization, that aims to establish common standards to be 
implemented throughout the region of OIC - Organization of the Islamic Cooperation, which 
aims to ensure consumer protection and interoperability of products, and also to strengthen 
the market position of OIC member states in the global market. 
The countries that are part of OIC are: Afghanistan, Albania, Azerbaijan, Bangladesh, 
United Arab Emirates, Brunei, Darussalam, Algeria, Indonesia, Morocco, Palestine, Gabon, 
Iraq, Iran, Cameroon, Qatar, Kyrgyzstan, Kuwait, Libya, Lebanon, Maldives, Malaysia, 
Egypt, Nigeria, Oman, Uzbekistan, Pakistan, Senegal, Somalia, Sudan, Syria, Saudi Arabia, 
Tajikistan, Tunisia, Turkey, Turkmenistan, Uganda, Jordan, Yemen, Bosnia and 
Herzegovina.

FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO

6. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR THE PRODUCTION OF HALAL FEED PRODUCE 
AND LIVESTOCK FEED  
 
6.1. 
The manufacturer shall show compliance with the legal issues applicable to the 
country and region; 
6.1.1. Establishments that produce products intended for animal livestock feed using 
medicinal products shall be registered in the ABPM with specific authorization for 
the activity. 
 
6.2. 
All feed produce and livestock feed certified by FAMBRAS HALAL, and their raw 
materials, process aids, ingredients and packaging shall be free of any non-Halal 
element described in Table 3, and of any impurity (Najasah), see item 5.7. 
In the table of attachment 1 are listed some Halal critical points that shall be 
considered, for each product type.  
 
6.3. 
All feed produce and livestock feed certified by FAMBRAS HALAL, and their raw 
materials, process aids, ingredients and packaging does not contain animal 
derivatives that have not been slaughtered in a Halal manner (according to 
Shariah). 
 
6.4. 
All feed produce and livestock feed certified by FAMBRAS HALAL and their 
respective raw materials, ingredients, process aids, and packaging materials shall 
be guaranteed in the face of their Halal status with official documents 
demonstrating their lawful origin, except when they are of 100% natural origin, such 
as corn, wheat, soybeans and others – as assessed by FAMBRAS Halal. 
6.4.1. There shall be a supplier approval process for the selection and approval of raw 
materials, inputs, process aids and packaging materials, in order to ensure the 
quality and integrity of the food, as well as its safety for Halal.

Animals 
Animals not slaughtered in a Halal manner – according to Islamic rules; Animals 
slaughtered by polytheists, irreligious, secular, atheists, magic, apostasy and other 
nonbelievers except for Christians and Jews; Naturally dead animals; Smothered 
animals; Animals fatally beaten (Mawqouza); Animals killed as a result of falling 
(Motaradiah); Animals killed by headbutt (Nateehah); Animals eaten by prey animals; 
Animals in whose slaughter the name of God (Allaah) is not mentioned; Animals 
slaughtered by idolatry; 
Pig; Domestic donkeys; Mules; Elephants; Monkeys; Dogs; Foxes, cats and similars. 
Predators such as prey animals and similars, such as lions, cheetahs, bears. 
Birds of prey with sharp claws used to attack and the like, such as eagles, falcons, 
sparrows, peregrines, parrots and owls. 
Rodents, reptiles, pests and the like, such as rats, centipedes, snakes, snakes, 
varanus, lizards, geckos, chameleons, hedgehogs, marten, bats, squirrels, bulls, mole 
and coleopterans (except dabb lizards and jerboas). 
Scorpions, all kinds of insects, worms and animals that are prohibited by law from 
being killed, such as ants, bees, woodpeckers and sparing (or boubela/ hoopoes), with 
the exception of locusts (the cochineal due to its similarity to the locust) and inevitable 
parts of bees such as honey. 
Repulsive and similar creatures, such as shell animals, larvae and other similar 
animals. 
Animals fed unclean items, unless they are confined and fed in the last three days with 
food allowed by the Islamic Rules.

Toxic animals or harmful species, unless toxic substances or harmful substances are 
removed during preparation. 
Plants 
Plants and their products/parts are toxic, harmful, poisonous or narcotic, unless 
harmful or poisonous substance is removed during preparation. 
Beverages 
Alcoholic beverages (contains ethanol), intoxicating beverages or those containing 
alcohols, narcotic, poisonous, toxic or harmful substances.

species (non-halal).

products from 
animals or

origin, such as vomiting and urine. 
Food additives 
All toxic, harmful additives and their derivatives. 
Products and derivatives of non-Halal materials. 
Enzymes 
All enzymes derived from non-Halal sources. 
Microorganisms 
Microorganisms such as germs, fungi, toxins and harmful yeast produced in non-Halal 
environments or using non-Halal ingredients.

=== PAGINA 15/33 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FEED 
PRODUCTION CERTIFICATION

Rev.: 07 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:15 de 33 
 
 
6.5. 
All feed produce and livestock feed certified by FAMBRAS HALAL, and their raw 
materials, ingredients, process aids and packaging materials shall be obtained 
from safe sources, shall not contain toxic substances and hazardous pollutants 
which may be considered harmful to health. 
 
6.6. 
The ingredients of feed produce or livestock feed may be genetically modified, 
provided that it is not nutritionally disadvantageous, or has different effects on 
human health or animal health. 
6.6.1. It shall be clearly identified on the label when genetically modified ingredients 
are used. 
 
6.7. 
All products certified by FAMBRAS HALAL and its raw materials, inputs, 
processing aids and packaging materials shall meet the physical-chemical 
standards established for drugs and medications and shall contain only allowed 
substances and within the maximum limits established by current legislation. 
 
6.8. 
All raw materials, inputs, processing aids and packaging materials, used in certified 
products, shall be previously informed and approved by FAMBRAS HALAL. 
 
6.9. 
Ethyl alcohol/ Ethanol: 
 
6.9.1. All products certified by FAMBRAS HALAL, and their raw materials, 
ingredients, process aids or any other element that directly contacts the Halal 
product are not/ may not contain alcoholic beverages (khamr) or derived 
physically separated from alcoholic beverages. 
 
6.9.2. Products derived from solid form alcoholic beverage industry, such as brewer's 
yeast extract, may be used provided that they undergo a purification process 
according to Shariah, which consists of the complete removal of color, odor and 
taste of the alcoholic beverage. 
 
6.9.3. Ethyl alcohol (ethanol) may occur naturally in food due to natural fermentation, 
however, it is necessary that these do not exceed the maximum ethanol limits 


=== PAGINA 16/33 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FEED 
PRODUCTION CERTIFICATION  
DT 7.9 
Rev.: 07 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:16 de 33 
 
 
established by FAMBRAS HALAL, which is 0.5%.  
6.9.4. In some cases, ethyl alcohol (ethanol) is indispensable in manufacturing, which 
is used as an auxiliary/process additive. In this case, ethanol is permitted, 
provided that the source of obtaining alcohol is not alcoholic beverages (e.g. 
wine), and the product marketed as livestock feed and animal livestock feed 
does not contain more than 0.5% ethyl alcohol. 
 
6.10. The name of the final product, the brand, the logo, the sensory characteristics and 
the packaging model do not infringe the principles of Shariah. For example, the 
brand does not allude to Haram elements such as bacon, ham, beer or any other 
that might confuse the consumer; 
 
6.11. The Islamic Rules shall be applied at all stages of the FAMBRAS HALAL certified 
livestock feed and livestock feed production chain, including receipt, preparation, 
packaging, labelling, storage and transport;  
 
6.12. Official authorities may take all necessary procedures to verify the conformity of 
products with the special requirements of Halal products and may take appropriate 
measures in accordance with local legislation. 
 
6.13. All types of cleaning products, or maintenance, such as detergents, sanitizers, 
greases, and lubricants shall be previously informed and approved by FAMBRAS 
HALAL and shall be free of non-Halal components or materials (prohibited for 
Shariah). 
 
6.14. The weight of the Halal product declared on the product packaging may not be 
less than its actual weight (see 5.8). 
6.14.1. The company shall carry out sample tests to measure the Halal products 
weight, comparing the weight described on the package with the actual 
weight;   
6.14.2. In case of non-conforming products, where the real weight is inferior to the 
weight of the package, there shall be a segregation, where the product will be

condemned for commercialization as a Halal product. The company shall the 
correction and corrective action in cases of non-conformities;  
6.14.3. There shall be daily records of these conferences. 
 
 
 
7. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR THE PRODUCTION AREAS OF HALAL FEED 
PRODUCE AND LIVESTOCK FEED  
 
7.1. 
All Halal certified feed produce, and livestock feed shall be completely separated 
from non-Halal products, at any and all stages of the manufacturing flow (receipt, 
preparation, packaging, labeling, storage, and transportation), to avoid cross-
contamination. 
 
7.2. 
All Halal certified feed produce, and livestock feed shall be prepared or processed 
in independent production lines, and Halal feed may be prepared or processed in 
production lines previously used for Halal feed, since necessary measures are 
taken to clean and disinfect production lines to avoid any contact or contamination 
between Halal and non-Halal feed. 
 
7.3. 
For companies manufacturing halal and non-halal livestock feed: 
7.3.1. The equipment shall be sanitized before the start of Halal production, and the 
cleaning procedure is duly validated; 
7.3.2. It is recommended that the production day begin with the manufacture of 
livestock feed produce and/or halal livestock feed; 
7.3.3. It is necessary to identify the lines that process Halal in order to prevent cross-
contamination; 
7.3.4. Cleaning is not authorized alternately between equipment that processes Halal 
and equipment that processes non-Halal; 
7.3.5. Practices applied in sampling shall ensure that there is no cross-contamination 
between feed produce and halal livestock feed, or its raw materials, ingredients 
and process aids, with any non-Halal product. For example, use a utensil to 
sample feed produce and livestock feed that has not been properly sanitized, and 


=== PAGINA 18/33 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FEED 
PRODUCTION CERTIFICATION  
DT 7.9 
Rev.: 07 
Date: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:18 de 33 
 
 
was also previously used to collect some non-Halal product. 
7.3.6. Validation tests may be carried out in the first Halal production following non-Halal 
production, be they in the product and/or equipment, as determined by the 
certification body; 
7.3.7. Halal and non-Halal raw materials shall be segregated and identified. 
 
7.4. 
If the production lines have already been used to manufacture products of pork 
origin, before converting it to the manufacture of feed produce and halal livestock 
feed, it is necessary that the same goes through a ritual of purification, which 
consists of subjecting the equipment to the passage of water + cleaning agents, 
such as detergent and sanitizer, at least 7 times, for the complete removal of color, 
taste and odor of the contaminant. 
7.4.1. If the standard procedure of the certified company does not add up to 7 product 
passes (adding water, cleaning, and rinsing), it shall be repeated as many times 
as necessary until totalization, or additional rinsings may be carried out only 
with water, so that, in open equipment, uninterrupted flow of water occurs for at 
least 30 seconds, followed by total evacuation of water. This procedure will be 
considered as 1 (one) rinse. 
7.4.2. The conversion of lines between Halal and swine production shall not be carried 
out continuously. 
 
7.5. 
All FAMBRAS HALAL certified products containing meat, gelatin, or other 
elements of animal origin (from slaughtered animals) shall have a FAMBRAS 
HALAL supervisor working on site during Halal production, which may be fixed or 
per campaign. Other types of products may have the same obligation when the 
company manufactures Halal and non-Halal products, and they are identified as 
high risk for Halal, and this need will be defined by FAMBRAS HALAL. 
7.5.1. The supervisor shall be Muslim, mentally healthy, with at least 18 years old, and 
competent to perform his role efficiently, demonstrating knowledge and 
understanding of the Islamic fundamentals and requirements referring to Halal. 
 
7.6. 
All equipment, production lines, tools, utensils, and associated materials used for 


=== PAGINA 19/33 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
 
 
 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FEED 
PRODUCTION CERTIFICATION  
DT 7.9
