=== SHEET: Report - Requirements (dimensoes: A2:I63) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO
 
AUDIT REPORT OF FEED PRODUCTION (CATEGORY D) – 
STAGE 1 (DT 7.9) |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.13
Rev.: 06
Date: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAJOR | NC - MINOR | OBS | NA | Evidences | 
 | 1.    GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR THE PRODUCTION OF HALAL FEED PRODUCE AND LIVESTOCK FEED |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1.    The company has a sanitary license in accordance with the legal issues of the country / region. If the feed contains drugs, registration with MAPA is evidenced. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.1 and 6.12). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2. Through the evaluation of the flowchart and technical sheets, it is possible to confirm that the final product only contains raw materials, ingredients, processing aids and packaging materials previously informed and approved, thus it is free of Haram, Najash (Impure) elements and animal derivatives not from Halal slaughter (DT 7.9 - Item 6.2 and 6.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.3. All raw materials, ingredients, process aids, and packaging materials used in Halal certified products, were previously informed and approved, have documentation demonstrating their lawful origin  in face of  Halal, ensuring its Halal Status (DT 7.9 - Item 6.4 and 6.8) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. The packaging materials used in Halal certified products have documentation to prove their toxicity. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5. If feed and animal feed ingredients are genetically modified, they are not nutritionally disadvantageous, or have different effects on human health or animal health. In addition, these must come from legal sources for Halal, as well as be identified on the label. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.6 and 6.6.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6. All raw materials, processing aids, inputs and packaging materials, have documentation that proves their compliance with physical-chemical standards and certify that they only have allowed substances and with the maximum imits established by the current legislation. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.7). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.7. There is an approval and selection procedure for raw materials, inputs, processing aids and packaging materials suppliers, to ensure the food quality and integrity, as well its safety for Halal (DT 7.9 – Item 6.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8. Ethyl alcohol, when necessary, is used only as an auxiliary/process additive, and there is documentation that proves that it does not come from alcoholic beverage industries. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.9.1 and 6.9.4). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. If the company uses products derived from alcoholic beverage industries in solid form, such as brewer's yeast extract, there are records of the purification procedure carried out to completely eliminate the color, odor and flavor of the alcoholic beverage. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.9.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.10. It is documented that it is possible to verify that when the company uses ethyl alcohol (ethanol), as an auxiliary/ processing aid, in the manufacturing process of the products present in the scope of certification OR this occurs naturally due to natural fermentation, the residual in the final product is controlled. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.9.3 and 6.9.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.11. The name of the final product, the brand, the logo, the sensory characteristics and the packaging model do not violate Shariah principles, for example the brand does not refer to Haram elements, such as bacon, ham, beer, or any other that may confuse the consumer. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.12. Based on a general documentary assessment of the process, by the religious auditor, it is possible to verify compliance with Islamic rules throughout the production chain (receipt, preparation, packaging, labeling, storage and transport) of items certified by FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.11) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.12. All types of cleaning or maintenance products, such as detergents, sanitizers, greases and lubricants, were previously informed and approved by FAMBRAS HALAL, have documentation showing that they are free of non-halal components or materials (prohibited by Shariah) (DT 7.9 – Item 6.13) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR THE PRODUCTION AREAS OF HALAL FEED PRODUCE AND LIVESTOCK FEED |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1. Evaluation only for units that produces Halal and non-Halal animal food and/ or animal feed (DT 7.9 - Item 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.1. The company foresees in its documents, as a hygiene procedure or the Halal Assurance System (QMS / HAS), the mandatory cleaning of equipment before the beginning of Halal production , as well as the validation of this procedure. (DT 7.9 - Item 7.3.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.2. The company provides in its documents, as a hygiene procedure or the Halal Assurance System (QMS / HAS), that the equipment hygiene should not be performed alternately between equipment that process Halal and equipment that processes non-Halal products (Ex. Perform an alternate CIP between equipment that processes Halal and non-Halal). (DT 7.9 - Item 7.3.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2. The company foresees in its documents, as a hygiene procedure or the Halal Assurance System (QMS / HAS), that if the production lines have already been used to manufacture products of swine origin, before converting it to manufacture Halal products it is necessary that it has undergone a purification ritual, which consists of subjecting the equipment to the passage of water + cleaning agents, such as detergent and sanitizer, in the minimum 7 times, so that the complete removal of color, flavor and aroma of the contaminant occurs. (DT 7.9 - Item 7.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.3. The company provides in its documents, as a a hygiene procedure or the Halal Guarantee System (QMS / HAS), that the conversion of lines between Halal and swine production shall not be carried out continuously. (DT 7.9 - Item 7.4.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.4. If the company already manufactures Halal products containing meat, gelatin or other elements of animal origin (from slaughtered animals),  or identified as high risk for Halal, there is a Muslim and competent supervisor of FAMBRAS HALAL working on the spot during Halal production (DT 7.1 – Item 7.5 and 7.5.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.5. The company provides in its documents, as a a hygiene procedure or the Halal Guarantee System (QMS / HAS), that all the equipments, production lines, tools, utensils and materials used for Halal products preparation shall be clean and free of impurities,and shall not be made of or contain any type of non-Halal material. (DT 7.9 - Item 7.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.   GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR STORAGE AND TRANSPORTATION OF HALAL FEED PRODUCE AND LIVESTOCK FEED |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1. The company provides in its documents a system foreseen for storage that is adequate to the intended use of the material, taking into account the conditions of ventilation, humidity and temperature. And in the case of animal food and medicated feed, the necessary controls are provided (DT 7.9 - Items 8.1 and 8.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2. The company is aware and recordss in the Halal Assurance System (HAS) that all products certified by FAMBRAS HALAL shall be identified (being the Halal seal visible on the secondary packaging a sufficient form of identification), and as applicable separately from other non-Halal products and non-conform products, during storage and transport, to prevent them from being mixed with non-Halal products. (DT 7.9 - Items 8.3, 8.3.4, 8.4, 8.5 and 8.7 and DT 7.3 - Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3. The company documents in the Halal Assurance System (HAS) the evaluation of hazards and the appropriated procedure, with the measures that shall be taken, for example, in case of breakage/leakage of non-Halal product packages in the final product warehouse, to ensure no cross-contamination of the Halal product (DT 7.9 – Item 8.3.3 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.1 e 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.4. The company documents the stock rotation programs, eg PVPS, FIFO system (DT 7.9 – Item 8.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.5. The company is aware and records in the Halal Assurance System (HAS) that it is prohibited to transport Halal products and products of porcine origin and / or their derivatives, to avoid cross contamination with Haram products (DT 7.9 - Item 8.8 and DT 7.3 - Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.6. The company's transportation procedure includes adequate controls for Halal safety, avoiding cross contamination with non-Halal products, and compliance with hygiene conditions. In the case of bulk transport, the risk of previous cargo is still considered (DT 7.9 - Items 8.9 and 8.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.  GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR QUALITY AND SAFETY OF HALAL FEED PRODUCE AND LIVESTOCK FEED |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.   The Good Manufacturing Practices system contains at least (DT 7.9 - Item 10.5): |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.1.   Good Manufacturing Practices Manual and / or Quality Manual |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | a)   Cleaning of facilities, equipment, furniture and utensils |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | b)  Water control |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | c) Hygiene and health of handlers |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | d) Waste management |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | e) Preventive maintenance and equipment calibration |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | f)   Integrated vector and pest control |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | g)   Selection of raw materials, ingredients and packaging |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | h) Cross contamination prevention measure. |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | i) Methodology for handling deviations, complaints and non-conformities. |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2. The company has a Halal product traceability program. (DT 7.9 – Item 10.5 and DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.7 and 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. If applicable, the company follows the Good Agricultural Practices (GAP), and has a Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Point (HACCP) program. (DT 7.9 - Item 10.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4. The company has a recall program, which includes communication with the Halal certifier, and foresees the need to carry it out in the event of contamination that undermines the Halal status of the product. (DT 7.9 – Item 10.7) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. If the company manufactures animal feed with veterinary medicine, a system for controlling the production and marketing of these products is evidenced, in addition to the need to keep the files available for auditing for a minimum period of two years (DT 7.9 - Item 10.9) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.6. There are records that animal food and animal feed meet established levels for pesticides, persistent organic pollutants, pathogens and toxins such as mycotoxins, and undesirable substances that may give rise to risks to the health of consumers (DT 7.9 - Item 10.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.    HALAL ASSURANCE SYSTEM (HAS) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1.  The company has a Halal Assurance System documented according to the internal condition of the company, being developed in a format compatible with the quality management system, and in order to ensure the Halal status of the certified products (DT 7.9 - Item 10.1 and DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 and 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2. The company establishes a Halal policy (whether or not it is linked to another company policy) that indicates its commitment with the production of Halal products, being this one duly signed by the top management of the company (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.1, 7.1 and 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3. There is a team responsible for Halal Management, which is presented in the Halal Guarantee System (QMS - HAS), where the responsibilities and autonomy are described. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.2, 8.1 and 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4. The team is composed of the necessary authorities (including the Halal supervisor, if applicable), that is directly involved on Halal production process, and has the autonomy to ensure the Halal condition of the products and to answer for the audit findings (DT 7.3 - Item 8.2 and 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5. The company has a planning for Halal training scheduled to take place at least once a year, and they are mandatory to all personnel involved in Halal process, including new wmployees (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 and 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. The content of Halal training covers the Halal, Haram and Mashbooh concept, the market that the company intends to reach out with the certification, and internal controls established to guarantee certification. Furthermore, for employees involved in monitoring PCCHs, training includes the importance of their activity regarding the Halal safety. (DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 and 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. The Halal Assurance System (HAS) registration document describes the composition and flowchart of each of FAMBRAS HALAL certified products. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.4, 10.1 and 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8. The Hazards and Halal Critical Control Points are identified, validated and recorded in the company's HAS, through a risk matrix that provides an analysis of  the probability of occurrence of the hazard versus the severity of the occurrence of the hazard, taking into account cross-contamination with Haram products at each stage of the flow chart. (DT 7.3 - Item  6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 and 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. The Halal Assurance System (HAS) developed by the company predicts the need of the complete segregation of Halal and non-Halal raw materials and products, as well its identification, at any and all stages of production flow (receipt, preparation, packaging, labeling, storage and transportation) to avoid any cross-contamination. (DT 7.9 - Item 7.1, 7.2.3 and 7.2.7 and DT 7.3 - Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10. The preventive measures for the occurrence of risk and corrective measures in case of deviations are clearly defined in the company's HAS. As well as the procedures to be adopted for the management of Halal Critical Control Points are well documented, including monitoring records, those responsible and actions in the case of deviations. (DT 7.3 – Item 11.4 and 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. The company describes in the registration document of the Halal Assurance System a methodology for HAS internal audit. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.6 and 12.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12. The HAS internal audit is planned (for the first year of certification) or has been carried out, at least once a year, or more often if necessary. (DT 7.3 - Item 12.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  |

=== SHEET: Informe DETALLADO (dimensoes: A2:I63) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS CERTIFICACIÓN HALAL 
 
INFORME DE AUDITORIA DE ALIMENTOS PARA ANIMALES Y CONCENTRADO ANIMAL (CATEGORIA D) – ETAPA 1 (DT 7.9) |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.13
Rev.: 06
Fecha: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAYOR | NC - MENOR | OBS | NA | Evidencias | 
 | 1.    REQUISITOS GENERALES PARA LA PRODUCCIÓN DE CONCENTRADOS Y ALIMENTOS PARA ANIMALES HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1. La empresa dispone de una licencia sanitaria que cumple los requisitos legales del país/región. Si el pienso contiene medicamentos, ha sido registrado en el MAPA (Ministerio de Agricultura y Pecuaria). (DT 7.9 - Item 6.1 y 6.12). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2. Evaluando el diagrama de flujo y las fichas técnicas, es posible confirmar que el producto final sólo contiene materias primas, ingredientes, auxiliares tecnológicos y materiales de envasado que han sido previamente informados y aprobados, por lo que está libre de elementos Haram, Najash (Impuro) y derivados animales que no procedan de sacrificio Halal.(DT 7.9 – Item 6.2 y 6.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.3. Todas las materias primas, ingredientes, auxiliares tecnológicos y materiales de envasado utilizados en los productos con certificación Halal han sido previamente informados y aprobados por FAMBRAS HALAL, y disponen de documentación que demuestra su origen lícito frente a Halal, garantizando su Condición Halal. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.4 y 6.8). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. Los materiales de envasado utilizados en los productos con certificación Halal disponen de documentación que demuestra su no toxicidad. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5.En caso de que los ingredientes de los piensos y alimentos para animales están modificados genéticamente, no deben ser desfavorables desde el punto de vista nutricional ni tener efectos perjudiciales para la salud humana o animal. También deben proceder de fuentes legítimas Halal y estar identificados en la etiqueta. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.6 y 6.6.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6. Todas las materias primas, auxiliares de proceso, insumos y materiales de envasado disponen de documentación que demuestra su cumplimiento de las normas físico-químicas y microbiológicas, y atestigua que sólo contienen sustancias permitidas y dentro de los límites máximos establecidos por la legislación vigente.(DT 7.9 - Item 6.7). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.7. Existe un procedimiento de aprobación y selección de proveedores de materias primas, insumos, auxiliares de proceso y materiales de envasado, con el fin de garantizar la calidad e integridad de los alimentos, así como su seguridad para Halal. (DT 7.9 – Item 6.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8. El alcohol etílico, cuando es necesario, sólo se utiliza como auxiliar/aditivo del proceso, y existe documentación que demuestra que no procede de industrias de bebidas alcohólicas. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.9.1 y 6.9.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. Si la empresa utiliza productos derivados de las industrias de bebidas alcohólicas en forma sólida, como el extracto de levadura de cerveza, existen registros del procedimiento de purificación llevado a cabo para eliminar por completo el color, el olor y el sabor de la bebida alcohólica. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.9.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.10. La evidencia documental demuestra que cuando la empresa utiliza alcohol etílico (etanol) como auxiliar de proceso/aditivo en el proceso de fabricación de los productos en el alcance de la certificación O esto ocurre naturalmente debido a la fermentación natural, el residuo en el producto final es controlado. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.9.3 y 6.9.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.11. El nombre del producto final, la marca, el logotipo, las características sensoriales y el modelo de envase no infringen los principios de la Sharia, por ejemplo, la marca no alude a elementos haram como el tocino, el jamón, la cerveza ni nada que pueda confundir al consumidor. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.12. A partir de una evaluación documental general del proceso por parte del auditor religioso, es posible verificar el cumplimiento de las normas islámicas en todas las fases de la cadena de producción (recepción, preparación, envasado, etiquetado, almacenamiento y transporte) de los productos certificados por FAMBRAS HALAL.(DT 7.9 – Item 6.11) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.13. Todo tipo de productos de limpieza o mantenimiento, como detergentes, desinfectantes, grasas y lubricantes, han sido previamente informados y aprobados por FAMBRAS HALAL, con documentación que demuestra que están libres de componentes o materiales no Halal (prohibidos por la ley islámica). (DT 7.9 – Item 6.13) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2. REQUISITOS GENERALES PARA LAS ÁREAS DE PRODUCCIÓN DE PIENSOS Y CONCENTRADOS HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.Evaluación exclusiva para las unidades que fabrican concetrados y/o alimentos Halal y no Halal (DT 7.9 - Item 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.1. Los documentos de la empresa, como el procedimiento de higienización o el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/ HAS), estipulan que los equipos deben limpiarse antes de iniciar la producción halal, y que este procedimiento debe validarse.  (DT 7.9 - Item 7.3.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.2.La empresa establece en sus documentos, como procedimiento de higienización o del Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/ HAS), que la higienización de los equipos no debe realizarse alternativamente entre equipos que procesan productos Halal y equipos que procesan productos no Halal (por ejemplo, realizar un CIP alternativo entre equipos que procesan productos Halal y no Halal). (DT 7.9 - Item 7.3.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2. La empresa estipula en sus documentos, como el procedimiento de higiene o el sistema de garantía Halal (SGC/ HAS), que si las líneas de producción ya se han utilizado para fabricar productos derivados del cerdo, antes de reconvertirlas para fabricar productos Halal deben someterse a un ritual de purificación, que consiste en someter el equipo a agua + agentes de limpieza, como detergente y desinfectante, al menos 7 veces, de modo que se eliminen por completo el color, el sabor y el aroma del contaminante. (DT 7.9 - Item 7.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.3. La empresa establece en sus documentos, como el procedimiento de higienización o el sistema de garantía Halal (SGC/ HAS), que la conversión de las líneas entre producción Halal y porcina no debe realizarse de forma continua. (DT 7.9 - Item 7.4.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.4.    Si la empresa fabrica productos Halal que contienen carne, huesos u otros elementos de origen animal (procedentes de animales sacrificados), o identificados como de alto riesgo para Halal, hay un supervisor musulmán competente de FAMBRAS HALAL trabajando in situ durante la producción Halal. (DT 7.9 – Item 7.5 y 7.5.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.5. La empresa estipula en sus documentos, como el procedimiento de higienización o el Sistema de Garantía Halal (SGC/ HAS), que todos los equipos, líneas de producción, herramientas, utensilios y materiales utilizados para la elaboración de productos Halal deben estar limpios y libres de impurezas, y no deben estar fabricados ni contener ningún tipo de material no Halal. (DT 7.9 - Item 7.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.    REQUISITOS GENERALES PARA EL ALMACENAMIENTO Y TRANSPORTE DE CONCENTRADO Y ALIMENTOS HALAL PARA ANIMALES |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1. Los documentos de la empresa contemplan un sistema de almacenamiento adecuado al uso previsto del material, teniendo en cuenta las condiciones de ventilación, humedad y temperatura. Y en el caso de los concentados para animales y los concentrados medicamentosos, se aplican los controles necesarios (DT 7.9 - Items 8.1 y 8.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2.  La empresa es consciente y documenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) que todos los productos certificados por FAMBRAS HALAL deben ser identificados (es suficiente con tener el sello visible en el embalaje secundario del producto Halal), y según la aplicabilidad separados de otros productos no Halal y productos no conformes, durante el almacenamiento y transporte, para evitar que se mezclen con productos no Halal. (DT 7.9 – Items 8.3, 8.3.4, 8.4, 8.5 y 8.7 y DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3. La empresa documenta la evaluación de riesgos y el procedimiento adecuado en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS), con las medidas que deben adoptarse, por ejemplo, en caso de rotura/filtración de envases de productos no Halal en el almacén de productos finales, para garantizar que el producto Halal no sufra contaminación cruzada. (DT 7.9 – Item 8.3.3 y DT 7.3 – Item 11.1 y 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.4. La empresa documenta los programas de rotación de existencias, por ejemplo, el sistema PVPS, FIFO (DT 7.9 – Item 8.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.5. La empresa es consciente y documenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) que está prohibido transportar juntos productos Halal y productos de origen porcino y/o sus derivados, para evitar la contaminación cruzada con productos Haram. (DT 7.9 – Item 8.8 y DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.6. El procedimiento de transporte de la empresa prevé controles adecuados de la seguridad Halal, evitando la contaminación cruzada con productos no Halal y el cumplimiento de las condiciones de higiene. En el caso del transporte a granel, también se tiene en cuenta el riesgo de las cargas anteriores(DT 7.9 – Items 8.9 y 8.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.   REQUISITOS GENERALES DE CALIDAD Y SEGURIDAD DE LOS CONCETRADOS Y ALIMENTOS PARA ANIMALES HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.   El sistema de Buenas Prácticas de Manufactura contiene al menos (DT 7.9 – Item 10.5): |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.1.   Manual de Buenas Prácticas de Manufactura y/o Manual de Calidad |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | a)   Limpieza de instalaciones, equipos, mobiliario y utensilios |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | b)   Control de agua |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | c) Higiene y salud de los manipuladores |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | d) Gestión de residuos |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | e) Mantenimiento preventivo y calibración de equipos |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | f)   Control integrado de vectores y plagas |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | g)   Selección de materias primas, ingredientes y envases |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | h) Medida para evitar la contaminación cruzada. |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | i) Metodología de tratamiento de las desviaciones, reclamaciones y no conformidades. |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2.La empresa cuenta con un programa de trazabilidad de productos Halal. (DT 7.9 – Item 10.5 y DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.7 y 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. Si aplica, la empresa sigue las Buenas Prácticas Agrícolas (BPA) y cuenta con un programa de Análisis de Peligros y Puntos de Control Crítico (APPCC). (DT 7.9 – Item 10.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4. La empresa dispone de un programa de retirada, que incluye la comunicación con el certificador Halal, y prevé la necesidad de llevarla a cabo en caso de contaminación que desvirtúe el estatus Halal del producto.(DT 7.9 – Item 10.7) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. Si la empresa fabrica concentrados para animales con medicamentos veterinarios, se establece un sistema de control de la producción y comercialización de estos productos, así como la necesidad de mantener los archivos disponibles para su auditoría durante un mínimo de dos años.(DT 7.9 - Item 10.9). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.6. Existen registros de que los concentrados y alimentos para animales cumplen los niveles establecidos para plaguicidas, contaminantes orgánicos persistentes, patógenos y toxinas, como las micotoxinas, y sustancias indeseables que podrían dar lugar a riesgos para la salud de los consumidores. (DT 7.9 - Item 10.10). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.    SISTEMA DE GARANTIA HALAL/ HALAL ASSURANCE SYSTEM (HAS) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1.  La empresa dispone de un Sistema de Garantía Halal documentado de acuerdo con las condiciones internas de la empresa, que se ha desarrollado en un formato compatible con el sistema de gestión de la calidad, y de tal forma que garantiza el estatus Halal de los productos certificados.(DT 7.9 - Item 10.1 y DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 y 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2.  La empresa establece una política Halal ( relacionada o no con otra política de la empresa) que indica su compromiso con la fabricación de productos Halal, debidamente firmada por la alta dirección de la empresa.(DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.1, 7.1 y 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3.  Existe un equipo responsable de la Gestión Halal, que se presenta en el Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS-SGC), donde se describen las responsabilidades y autonomías correspondientes. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.2, 8.1, 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4.  El equipo está formado por las autoridades necesarias (incluido el supervisor Halal, si aplica), que participan directamente en el proceso de producción Halal, y tiene autonomía para garantizar el estatus Halal de los productos y responder a los hallazgos encontradas durante las auditorías. (DT 7.3 – Item 8.2 y 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5. La empresa ha previsto sesiones de capacitación Halal al menos una vez al año, obligatorias para todos los implicados en el proceso Halal, incluidos los nuevos empleados. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 y 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. El contenido de los programas de formación Halal abarca el concepto de Halal, Haram y Mashbooh, el mercado al que la empresa pretende dirigirse con la certificación y los controles internos establecidos para garantizar la certificación. Además, para los empleados que participan en la supervisión del PCCH, la formación incluye la importancia de su actividad en términos de seguridad Halal. (DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 y 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. El documento de registro del Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) describe la composición y el diagrama de flujo de cada uno de los productos certificados por FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.4, 10.1 y 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8. Los Peligros y los Puntos Críticos de Control Halal se identifican, validan y registran en el HAS de la empresa utilizando una matriz de riesgos que analiza la probabilidad de que se produzca el peligro frente a la gravedad de que se produzca, teniendo en cuenta la contaminación cruzada con productos Haram en cada etapa del diagrama de flujo. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 y 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. El Sistema de Garantía Halal (HAS) elaborado por la empresa estipula la necesidad de una separación completa entre materias primas y productos halal y no halal, así como su identificación, en todas y cada una de las fases del flujo de fabricación (recepción, preparación, envasado, etiquetado, almacenamiento y transporte), con el fin de evitar cualquier contaminación cruzada. (DT 7.9 - Item 7.1, 7.2.3 y 7.2.7 y DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10. Las medidas preventivas para la aparición de riesgos y las medidas correctivas en caso de desviaciones están claramente definidas en la HAS de la empresa. Los procedimientos que deben adoptarse para gestionar los puntos críticos de control Halal están bien documentados, incluidos los registros de seguimiento, los responsables y las acciones en caso de desviaciones.  (DT 7.3 – Item 11.4 y 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. La empresa describe en el documento de registro del Sistema de Garantía Halal una metodología para la auditoría interna de HAS.(DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.6 y 12.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12. La auditoría interna de HAS está prevista (para el primer año de certificación) o se ha realizado al menos una vez al año, o con mayor frecuencia si es necesario. (DT 7.3 – Item 12.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  |

=== SHEET: Relatório DETALHADO (dimensoes: A2:I63) ===
 |  | FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO
 
RELATÓRIO DE AUDITORIA DE ALIMENTOS PARA ANIMAIS E RAÇÃO ANIMAL (CATEGORIA D) – ESTÁGIO 1 (DT 7.9) |  |  |  |  | FM 7.7.4.13
Rev.: 06
Data: 01/04/2024 | 
 | Item | C | NC - MAIOR | NC - MENOR | OBS | NA | Evidências | 
 | 1.    REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA A PRODUÇÃO DE ALIMENTOS PARA ANIMAIS E RAÇÃO ANIMAL HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.1. A empresa possui um alvará de funcionamento sanitário em conformidade com as questões legais do país/ região. Caso a ração contenha medicamentos é evidenciado o registro junto ao MAPA. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.1 e 6.12). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.2. Através da avaliação do fluxograma e fichas técnicas é possível confirmar que o produto final só contém matérias-primas, ingredientes, auxiliares de processo e materiais de embalagem, previamente informados e aprovados, desta forma está isento de elementos Haram, Najash (Impuros) e derivados animais não provenientes do abate Halal (DT 7.9 – Item 6.2 e 6.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.3. Todos as matérias-primas, ingredientes, auxiliares de processo, e materiais de embalagem usados nos produtos certificados Halal, foram previamente informados e aprovados pela FAMBRAS HALAL,e possuem documentação que demonstrem sua origem lícita diante do Halal,  garantindo seu Status Halal (DT 7.9 - Item 6.4 e 6.8). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.4. Os materiais de embalagem usados nos produtos certificados Halal possuem documentação que comprovem sua atoxidade. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.5). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.5. Caso ingredientes dos alimentos para animais e ração animal sejam geneticamente modificados, estes não são nutricionalmente desvantajoso, ou tem efeitos diversos para a saúde humana, ou para a saúde animal. Além disso estes devem provir de fontes lícitas para o Halal, bem como serem identificados no rótulo. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.6 e 6.6.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.6. Todas as matérias primas, auxiliares de processo, insumos e materiais de embalagem,  possuem documentação que comprove sua conformidade com os padrões físico-químicos e microbiológicos, e ateste que possuem apenas substâncias permitidas e dentro dos limites máximos estabelecidos pela legislação vigente. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.7). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.7. Existe um procedimento para homologação e seleção de fornecedores de matérias primas, insumos, auxiliares de processo e materiais de embalagem, de forma a garantir a qualidade e integridade do alimento, bem como sua segurança para o Halal. (DT 7.9 – Item 6.4.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.8. Álcool etílico, quando necessário, é utilizado somente como auxiliar/ aditivo de processo, e  existe documentação que comprova que este não é proveniente de indústrias de bebidas alcoólicas. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.9.1 e 6.9.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.9. Caso a empresa utilize produtos derivados de indústrias de bebidas alcoólicas em forma sólida, como extrato de levedura de cerveja, há registros do procedimento de purificação realizado para eliminação completa da cor, odor e sabor da bebida alcoólica. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.9.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.10. Documentalmente é possível verificar que quando a empresa utilizar álcool etílico (etanol), como auxiliar/ aditivo de processo, no processo de fabricação dos produtos presentes no escopo de certificação OU este ocorrer naturalmente devido a fermentação natural, o residual no produto final é controlado. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.9.3 e 6.9.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.11. O nome do produto final, a marca, o logo, as características sensoriais e o modelo da embalagem não infringem os princípios da Shariah, por exemplo a marca não faz alusão a elementos Haram, como bacon, presunto, cerveja, ou qualquer outro que possa confundir o consumidor. (DT 7.9 - Item 6.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.12. A partir de uma avaliação geral documental do processo, pelo auditor religioso, é possível verificar o respeito as regras islâmicas em toda a fase da cadeia de produção (recebimento, preparação, embalagem, rotulagem, armazenamento e transporte) dos itens certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.9 – Item 6.11) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 1.13. Todos os tipos de produtos de limpeza, ou manutenção, tais como detergentes, sanitizantes, graxas e lubrificantes, foram previamente informados e aprovados pela FAMBRAS HALAL,  possuem documentações que demonstram que estes são livres de componentes ou materiais não-halal (proibidos para a Shariah). (DT 7.9 – Item 6.13) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2. REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA AS ÁREAS DE PRODUÇÃO DE ALIMENTOS PARA ANIMAIS E RAÇÃO ANIMAL HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1. Avaliação apenas para unidades que fabricam alimentos para animais e/ ou ração animal  Halal e não Halal (DT 7.9 - Item 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.1. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), a obrigatoriedade de limpeza dos equipamentos antes do início da produção Halal, bem como a validação deste procedimento.  (DT 7.9 - Item 7.3.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.1.2. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que a higienização dos equipamentos não deve ser realizada alternadamente entre equipamentos que processam Halal e equipamentos que processam produtos não Halal (Ex. Realizar um CIP alternado entre equipamentos que processam Halal e não Halal). (DT 7.9 - Item 7.3.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.2. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que se as linhas de produção já tiverem sido utilizadas para fabricar produtos de origem suína, antes de converte-la para fabricação de produtos Halal é necessário que a mesma tenha passado por um ritual de purificação, o qual consiste em submeter os equipamentos a passagem de água + agentes de limpeza, como detergente e sanitizante, no mínimo 7 vezes, para que ocorra a remoção completa de cor, sabor e aroma do contaminante. (DT 7.9 - Item 7.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.3. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que a conversão das linhas entre produção Halal e suína não deve ser realizada de forma contínua. (DT 7.9 - Item 7.4.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.4.    Caso a empresa fabrique produtos Halal que contém carne, ossos ou outros elementos de origem animal (proveniente de animais abatidos), ou identificados como alto risco para o Halal, existe um supervisor, muçulmano e competente, da FAMBRAS HALAL trabalhando no local durante a produção Halal (DT 7.9 – Item 7.5 e 7.5.1). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 2.5. A empresa prevê em seus documentos, como procedimento de higienização ou o Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ/ HAS), que todos os equipamentos, linhas de produção, ferramentas, utensílios e materiais  utilizados para a preparação de produtos Halal devem ser limpos e livres de impurezas, e não devem ser fabricados ou conter qualquer tipo de material não Halal. (DT 7.9 - Item 7.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.    REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA ARMAZENAGEM E TRANSPORTE DE ALIMENTOS PARA ANIMAIS E RAÇÃO ANIMAL HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.1. A empresa prevê em seus documentos uma sistemática prevista para armazenamento que seja adequada ao uso pretendido do material, atendendo as condições de ventilação, umidade e temperatura. E no caso de alimentos para animais e rações com medicamentos, os controles necessários estão previstos (DT 7.9 - Itens 8.1 e 8.2). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.2.  A empresa está ciente e documenta no Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) que todos os produtos certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL devem estar identificados (sendo suficiente ter o selo visível na embalagem secundária do produto Halal), e conforme aplicabilidade separados de outros produtos não Halal e produtos não conformes, durante o armazenamento e transporte, para prevenir que estes sejam misturados com produtos não Halal. (DT 7.9 – Itens 8.3, 8.3.4, 8.4, 8.5 e 8.7 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.3. A empresa documenta no Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) a avaliação dos perigos e o procedimento adequado, com as medidas que devem ser praticadas, por exemplo em caso de rompimento/ vazamento de embalagens de produtos não Halal no armazém de produto final, para assegurar a não contaminação cruzada do produto Halal. (DT 7.9 – Item 8.3.3 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.1 e 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.4. A empresa documenta os programas de rotação de estoque, por exemplo, sistema PVPS, FIFO (DT 7.9 – Item 8.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.5. A empresa está ciente e documenta no Sistema de garantia Halal (HAS) que é proibido transportar junto produtos Halal e produtos de origem suína e/ ou seus derivados, para evitar contaminação cruzada com produtos Haram. (DT 7.9 – Item 8.8 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 3.6. O procedimento de transporte da empresa prevê os controles adequados para segurança Halal, evitando a contaminação cruzada com produtos não Halal, e o atendimento das condições de higiene. No caso de transporte a granel, ainda é considerado o risco das cargas anteriores (DT 7.9 – Itens 8.9 e 8.10) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.   REQUISITOS GERAIS PARA QUALIDADE E SEGURANÇA DOS ALIMENTOS PARA ANIMAIS E RAÇÃO ANIMAL HALAL |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.   A sistemática de Boas Práticas de Fabricação contém no mínimo (DT 7.9 – Item 10.5): |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.1.1.   Manual de Boas Práticas de Fabricação e/ ou Manual da Qualidade |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | a)   Higienização das instalações, equipamentos, móveis e utensílios |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | b)   Controle de água |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | c) Higiene e saúde dos manipuladores |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | d) Manejo dos resíduos |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | e) Manutenção preventiva e calibração de equipamentos |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | f)   Controle integrado de vetores e pragas |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | g)   Seleção das matérias-primas, ingredientes e embalagens |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | h) Medida prevenção contaminação cruzada. |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | i) Metodologia de tratamento de desvios, reclamações e não-conformidades. |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.2. A empresa possui um programa de rastreabilidade do produto Halal. (DT 7.9 – Item 10.5 e DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.7 e 13.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.3. Se aplicável, a empresa segue as Boas Práticas Agrícolas (BPA), e possui um programa de Análise de Perigos e Pontos de Controle Críticos (APPCC). (DT 7.9 – Item 10.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.4. A empresa possui um programa de recolhimento, o qual contempla a comunicação com a certificadora Halal, e preve a necessidade de realizá-lo mediante contaminação que desabone o status Halal do produto. (DT 7.9 – Item 10.7) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.5. Caso a empresa fabrique alimentos para animais com medicamento veterinário, é evidenciado uma sistemática para controle produção e comercialização destes produtos, além de ser previsto a necessidade de manter os arquivos disponíveis para auditoria por um período mínimo de dois anos (DT 7.9 - Item 10.9). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 4.6. Existem registros de que os alimentos para animais e ração animal atendem aos níveis estabelecidos para pesticidas, poluentes orgânicos persistentes, agentes patogênicos e toxinas, como micotoxinas, e substâncias indesejáveis que possam dar origem a riscos para a saúde dos consumidores (DT 7.9 - Item 10.10). |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.    SISTEMA DE GARANTIA HALAL/ HALAL ASSURANCE SYSTEM (HAS) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.1.  A empresa possui um Sistema de Garantia Halal documentado de acordo com a condição interna da empresa, sendo este desenvolvido em um formato compatível com o sistema de gestão da qualidade, e de forma a garantir o status Halal dos produtos certificados (DT 7.9 - Item 10.1 e DT 7.3 – Item 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 e 6.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.2.  A empresa estabelece uma política Halal (seja ela interligada ou não com outra política da empresa) que indique o seu compromisso com a produção de produtos Halal, estando esta devidamente assinada pela alta direção da empresa. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.1, 7.1 e 7.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.3.  Existe uma equipe responsável pela Gestão Halal, sendo esta apresentada no Sistema de Garantia Halal (SGQ – HAS), onde estão descritas as devidas responsabilidades e autonomias. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.2, 8.1, 8.4) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.4.  A equipe é composta pelas autoridades necessárias (incluindo o supervisor Halal, se aplicável), que estão diretamente envolvidas no processo de produção Halal, e possui autonomia para assegurar a condição Halal dos produtos e responder pelos apontamentos encontrados durante as auditorias (DT 7.3 – Item 8.2 e 8.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.5. A empresa possui um planejamento para realização de treinamentos Halal programados para ocorrer no mínimo uma vez ao ano, e os mesmos são obrigatorios para todas as pessoas envolvidas no processo Halal, incluindo novos funcionários. (DT 7.3 - Item 6.5.3, 9.1, 9.2 e 9.3) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.6. O conteúdo dos treinamentos Halal contemplam o conceito Halal, Haram e Mashbooh, mercado que a empresa pretende atingir com a certificação, e controles internos estabelecidos para garantir a certificação. Ainda, para os  funcionários envolvidos no monitoramento dos PCCH, os treinamentoss incluem a importância da sua atividade diante da segurança do Halal. (DT 7.3 - Item 9.1.1 e 9.2.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.7. O documento de registro do Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) descreve a composição e o fluxograma de cada um dos produtos certificados pela FAMBRAS HALAL. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.4, 10.1 e 10.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.8. Os Perigos e Pontos Críticos de Controle Halal são identificados, validados e registrados no HAS da empresa, através de uma matriz de risco que proporciona a análise da probabilidade de ocorrência do perigo versus a severidade da ocorrência do perigo, levando em consideração a contaminação cruzada com produtos Haram em cada etapa do fluxograma. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3 e 11.6) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.9. O Sistema de Garantia Halal (HAS) elaborado pela empresa prevê a necessidade de separação completa entre as matérias primas e produtos Halal e não Halal, bem como sua identificação, em toda e qualquer etapa do fluxo de fabricação (recebimento, preparação, embalagem, rotulagem, armazenamento e transporte), a fim de evitar qualquer contaminação cruzada (DT 7.9 - Item 7.1, 7.2.3 e 7.2.7 e DT 7.3 – Item 11.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.10. As medidas preventivas para a ocorrência do risco e as medidas corretivas em caso de desvios estão claramente definidas no HAS da empresa. Bem como as tratativas a serem adotadas para gestão dos Pontos Críticos de Controle Halal estão bem documentadas, incluindo os registros dos monitoramentos, os responsáveis e as ações no caso de desvios.  (DT 7.3 – Item 11.4 e 11.5) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.11. A empresa descreve no documento de registro do Sistema de Garantia Halal uma metodologia para auditoria interna HAS. (DT 7.3 – Item 6.5.6 e 12.1) |  |  |  |  |  |  | 
 | 5.12. A auditoria interna do HAS está prevista (para o primeiro ano de certificação) ou foi realizada, pelo menos uma vez ao ano, ou mais vezes, se necessário. (DT 7.3 – Item 12.2) |  |  |  |  |  |  |

=== PAGINA 1/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND 
LEISURE) SERVICES CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.11 
Rev.: 05 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:1 de 39 
 
 
1. 
OBJECTIVE 
This document establishes the general requirements for FAMBRAS HALAL 
certification of food and tourism services, such as hotels, restaurants, transportation, and 
leisure, that fits in categories E and H (GSO 2055-2) and E and H3 (SMIIC 02). 
 
 
2. 
APPLICATION FIELD 
This document is applicable to all food and tourism services certified by FAMBRAS 
HALAL on categories E and H (GSO 2055-2) and E and H3 (SMIIC 02). 
 
 
3. 
REFERENCE DOCUMENTS 
• 
OIC/SMIIC 9: 2019: Halal Tourism Services – General Requirements; 
• 
OIC/SMIIC 1: 2019: General Requirements for Halal Food 
• 
GSO 2055-1 – HALAL products - Part one General Requirements for HALAL 
Food. Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) – Gulf; 
• 
MS 1500: HALAL food - production, preparation, handling and storage - general 
guidelines. JAKIM Maylasian Standard – Malasya; 
• 
MUIS-HC-S001: General Guidelines for the Handling and Processing of Halal 
Food. MUIS - Singapura. 
• 
KMA BPJPH Number 748: 2021 - Products that must be certified Halal – 
Indonesia; 
• 
ISO 18513 - Tourism services — Hotels and other types of tourism 
accommodation — Terminology 
• 
IT 4.1 – FAMBRAS HALAL Seal Use Instruction; 
• 
RESOLUTION N° 216, OF SEPTEMBER 15, 2004 – Provides for Technical 
Regulation of Good Practices for Food Services; 
• 
RDC Nº 275, OF OCTOBER 21, 2002 – Provides for Technical Regulation of 
Standard Operation Procedures applied to Producers/ Industrializers Food 
Establishments and the Checklist of Good Manufacturing Practices in 
Producers/ Industrializers Food Establishments.

=== PAGINA 3/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND 
LEISURE) SERVICES CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.11 
Rev.: 05 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:3 de 39 
 
 
4. 
GENERAL PROVISIONS 
All food and tourism services that aim to obtain the Halal certification by FAMBRAS 
HALAL for categories E and H (GSO 2055-2) and E and H3 (SMIIC 02) shall meet the 
requirements established in this document. 
 
 
5. 
DEFINITIONS 
 
5.1. 
Islamic jurisprudence (Shariah) 
The word Shariah (Islamic jurisprudence) refers to the totality of the orders of Allah 
(God) that regulate all aspects of the life of a Muslim (MS 1900:2005), meaning, everything 
that is described in the holy Quran, that aside the God's revelations to Prophet Muhammad 
(SAWS). In addition to the Qur'an, the Sunnah, i.e. all practices and sayings of the Prophet 
Muhammad (SAW) are also part of the Shariah. 
 
5.2. 
Halal 
The word 'Halal' means lawful, the same as allowed. Halal designated products are 
those which consumption is allowed by God, according to Shariah.  
Halal is then the basis of all that is lawful, in food, in social life, in acts practiced 
(conduct), in justice, in clothes, in finances, etc., it is a system based on the principles and 
values that benefit the humanity.  
The Holy Quran teaches us in 5th Surah, 4th verse: "Lawful for you are all good foods". 
 
5.3. 
Halal Slaughter (Tazkeya) 
Halal slaughter is an Islamic ritual that shall be done quickly, so it does not allow the 
animal suffering, in which the trachea, esophagus and both arteries and jugular vein shall 
be cut to hasten the bleeding and the animal´s death.  
The slaughter ritual shall be practiced ethically and with respect for the animals to be 
slaughtered, following the Islamic laws of the Holy Quran. One of them consists of the 
Tasmiyah, which consist in invoking the phrase Bismillah, Allahu Akbar (In the name of God, 
God is the greatest), immediately before the slaughter. Bismillah is one of the ways to seek 
God's help before any action to be performed by a Muslim. There is no text in Islamic 


=== PAGINA 4/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND 
LEISURE) SERVICES CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.11 
Rev.: 05 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:4 de 39 
 
 
jurisprudence that confirms exactly the meaning of the Tasmiyah, but Islamic scholars claim 
that it would be a way of thanking God for the food offered by Him, since the sacrifice of 
animals is performed to ensure the sustenance of human beings and not for fun or sadism. 
 
5.4. 
Haram  
The word Haram means illicit, impure, illegal. Refers to what is unacceptable, 
detrimental to the health of the human body and its morals, as determined in the Qur'an and 
Hadith (confirmation of the acts, affirmations, opinions, and the way of Prophet Muhammad 
lived (Peace and blessings of Allah be upon him). 
 
5.5. Mashbouh 
There is a large number of industrialized products that use additives and ingredients 
that can bring dangers to spiritual and physical health, as they belong to the Mashbouh 
(questionable/ doubtful) category, which are: 
Elements that can be from numerous sources of extraction, such as: 
a) Glycerin which may be of vegetable or animal origin; 
b) L-Cysteine a non-essential amino acid can be obtained from human hair, pork, 
duck feather, oil and from vegetable. 
Processed products of Halal origin, for example:  
a) Clarified fruit juice with pork;  
b) Products such as yogurt, biscuit, cereal bar, that uses gelatin and mono and 
diglycerides; 
c) Flavours with alcohol; 
d) Cheeses, monosodium glutamate (flavor enhancer) and other products 
fermented with enzymes; 
e) Among others.  
 
5.6. 
Makruh 
Inappropriate. Not recommended (encouraged) according to Shariah. 
 
5.7. 
Najasah (Impurities)  


=== PAGINA 5/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND 
LEISURE) SERVICES CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.11 
Rev.: 05 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:5 de 39 
 
 
Najasah means everything that is considered impure, such as feces, urine, prostatic 
fluid, a discharge from the urethra, sperm, pus, vomit, and excrement of any animal whose 
meat is not allowed for consumption.  
5.8. 
Halal Mizen (weight/ measurement) 
Islam is concerned with protecting the rights of consumers by seeking the correct 
measurement and/or weight on the product. This way, any practice or process applied in an 
ineffective way, is a fraudulent act. The Holy Quran mentions: "Woe to the fraudsters, those 
who, when someone measures something to them, demand the exact/plenary measure. But 
when they measure or weigh for others, they defraud them." (Sura 83:1-3).  
With this in mind, when we also speak of commercialization, we must transpose the 
same concept of justice that is so strongly defended in Islam, as it is in the sacred Koran: 
"Oh my people, dispose of the measure and the weight with equity; do not defraud 
humans in their goods, and do not practice debauchery on earth as corruptors. (Sura 
11:85). 
 
5.9. 
Halal Certificate 
It is a faithful guarantee document issued by a Halal certifying institution, recognized 
by Islamic countries, to attest that the company, process and products, follow the legal 
requirements and criteria determined by Shariah. 
 
5.10. Tayyib 
The literal translation is good/ clean/ healthy. This term is commonly used by 
Muslims to refer to everything that is acceptable, pure and good, such as clothes, 
behaviors, but also it refers to food, of superior quality or purity, such as organic food or 
simply healthy. Therefore, the food can be Halal (made with permitted ingredients), but 
not be Tayyib. 
The concept of Tayyib, regarding food is linked to the manufacturing/ processing 
process, that way the food shall be safe. Therefore, it is not enough that the food is free of 
Haram elements in its formulations, but it also shall be produced according to Good 
Manufacturing Practices, at adequate sanitary facilities, free of any toxic element or even 
Najis/ Khabith (impure). 
 


=== PAGINA 6/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND 
LEISURE) SERVICES CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.11 
Rev.: 05 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:6 de 39 
 
 
5.11. Accommodation facilities  
Any building, including hostels, hotels, inns, boarding-houses, rest houses, 
homestays, and lodging houses, held out by the owner or manager, fully or partially, to offer 
lodging or accommodation for tourists, to rent or by any other reward means, whether or not 
food or drink is offered. 
 
5.12. Quran/ Al-Quran Al Kareem 
Muslims’ holy book.  
 
5.13. Fast 
One of the five pillars of Islam in which Muslims abstain from all carnal and worldly 
pleasures, such as food, drink (including water), sexual activities and refrained from 
smoking, during fasting hours [before the time of fajr prayer (dawn) until the time of Maghreb 
prayer (sunset)]. 
 
5.14. Common areas/ public spaces  
Seats, break rooms, seminar, meeting, work, food, drinking areas, mosque (masjid) 
and prayer room (mussala) etc. reserved for general use of the hotel customers and visitors. 
 
5.15. Halal restaurant 
Facility that only offers Halal food and that does not have any non-Halal services and 
activities. 
 
5.16. Halal tourism services 
Products or services, in the travel and tourism industry, guided by Islamic rules that 
cater to or provide facilities suitable for Muslims guests. 
 
5.17. Halal kitchen 
Facility’s kitchen where all raw material used to prepare food are allowed (Halal) and 
that the food prepare and service does not offer risk of contamination for Halal. 
 
5.18. Iftar 
Muslims’ meal in the time of Maghreb prayer (sunset) to break the period of fasting. 


=== PAGINA 7/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND 
LEISURE) SERVICES CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.11 
Rev.: 05 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:7 de 39 
 
 
 
5.19. Private areas 
Spaces (bedrooms, toilets, shower cabins, swimming pools, massage saloons, 
changing rooms, etc.) where person’s privacy is in question. 
 
5.20. Prayer room or mosque (musallah / masjid) 
A place, space, or room for Muslims that is reserved for prayer (Salah). 
 
5.21. Sajjada (Prayer carpet)  
Mat generally used by Muslims to perform their prayer on it (Salah), it is also known 
as prayer mat or prayer rug. 
 
5.22. Prayer (Salah) 
The prayer is the second of the five pillars of Islam and shall, mandatorily, be 
performed five times at a day. It is the most important practice in Islam after declaring faith 
in Allah and His Messenger. 
 
5.23. Quibla 
Kaaba’s direction in Makkah al-Mukarramah, where Muslims shall direct themselves 
during prayer. 
 
5.24. Ramadan 
Corresponds to the 9th month in the Islamic calendar (lunar calendar), when Muslims 
perform the mandatory fasting practice before the time of fajr (dawn) until the time of 
Magrebe (sunset), besides intensifying the practices of worshiping Allah. 
 
5.25. Sohoor / sahoor 
It is the meal taken by Muslims before dawn, that precedes the start of fasting. 
 
5.26. Wudhu / Ablution 
It is a purification ritual carried out before prayers. It corresponds to the washing of 
several parts of the body, including face, arms, and feet. 


=== PAGINA 8/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND 
LEISURE) SERVICES CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.11 
Rev.: 05 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:8 de 39 
 
 
 
5.27. Identification of the categories GSO 
The certified products are classified according to the categories presented in table 1 
– Gulf standard, according to GSO 2055-2. 
The Gulf countries referenced in this document are those that are part of the GCC 
(GCC Accreditation Center) and follow the standards established by the GSO. They are: 
Saudi Arabia, Oman, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Yemen and the United Arab Emirates.  
FAMBRAS Halal may operate in all categories indicated in the table, however, for the 
Gulf countries the recognition is applicable only for the categories marked in blue. For other 
categories, upon demand and necessity, FAMBRAS HALAL may request recognition.

=== PAGINA 11/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO

5.28. Identification of the categories SMIIC 
Certified products are classified according to the categories presented in table 2 - 
SMIIC 02:2019 standard. 
SMIIC stands for Standards and Metrology Institute for Islamic Countries, being an 
intergovernmental organization, that aims to establish common standards to be 
implemented throughout the region of OIC - Organization of the Islamic Cooperation, which 
aims to ensure consumer protection and interoperability of products, and also to strengthen 
the market position of OIC member states in the global market. 
The countries that are part of OIC are: Afghanistan, Albania, Azerbaijan, Bangladesh, 
United Arab Emirates, Brunei, Darussalam, Algeria, Indonesia, Morocco, Palestine, Gabon, 
Iraq, Iran, Cameroon, Qatar, Kyrgyzstan, Kuwait, Libya, Lebanon, Maldives, Malaysia, 
Egypt, Nigeria, Oman, Uzbekistan, Pakistan, Senegal, Somalia, Sudan, Syria, Saudi Arabia, 
Tajikistan, Tunisia, Turkey, Turkmenistan, Uganda, Jordan, Yemen, Bosnia and 
Herzegovina.

FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO

6. 
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR HALAL FOOD AND TOURISM (HOTELS, 
RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND LEISURE) SERVICES 
 
6.1. 
The establishment shall demonstrate compliance with the legal issues applicable 
to the country and region; 
 
6.2. 
The establishment shall assure that the requirements related to the product and 
services are in compliance with the Islamic rules. 
 
6.3. 
The visual resources (announces, banners, outdoors etc.) of the establishment 
shall not violate Islamic ethics. 
 
6.4. 
There shall be no transmission of content that violates the Islamic ethics, on 
televisions of the establishments certified as Halal. 
 
6.5. 
The Halal certificate shall be clearly and visibly available at the approved 
establishment. 
 
6.6. 
The Halal certified establishment shall have licit actions, for that it shall have 
actions and projects aiming at the environment and even the use of properly 
legalized labor. 
 
6.7. 
If the establishment sells Halal products based on the weight of the product, it 
cannot be less than its actual weight (see 5.8). 
6.7.1. 
The company shall carry out sample tests to measure the Halal products 
weight, comparing the weight described on the package with the actual 
weight;   
6.7.2. 
The company shall keep records of the calibration of equipment used to 
weigh the Halal product; 
6.7.3. 
In case of non-conforming products, where the real weight is inferior to the 
weight of the package, there shall be a segregation, where the product will be 
condemned for commercialization as a Halal product. The company shall

FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO

record the correction and corrective action in cases of non-conformities;  
6.7.4. 
There shall be daily records of these conferences. 
 
 
7. 
HALAL REQUIREMENTS 
 
7.1. 
The procedures derived from Islamic rules shall be adopted in all Halal service 
areas 
 
7.2. 
The rules for the sections, where Halal food and beverages are prepared and 
served in the premises, shall be in accordance with the item 8 of this scheme. 
 
7.3. 
The Chemical substances and materials used for hygiene and sanitation shall not 
offer risk for the Halal certification. Any equipment and personnel that will contact 
food, shall meet hygienic requirements (good practices). 
 
7.4. 
Cosmetics and personal care products, such as soaps and shampoos, found in 
hand washers, bathrooms, toilets, and shower cabins, shall not offer risk for the 
Halal certification. 
 
7.5. 
Alcoholic beverages shall not be served in the premises and alcoholic beverages 
brought from outside for consumption in the premises of the Halal establishment 
shall not be allowed. 
 
7.6. 
The organization shall properly observe the placement, storage and handling of 
the documents containing verses of the Quran. 
7.6.1. The organization shall assure that the discard, when necessary, of the records 
containing verses of the Quran, is conducted according to the requirements of 
the Islamic rules. 
 
7.7. 
Public washroom 
All Halal establishments that have public washroom, shall pay attention to the 


=== PAGINA 16/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND 
LEISURE) SERVICES CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.11 
Rev.: 05 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:16 de 39 
 
 
following obligations: 
7.7.1. The washroom shall be equipped with bidet, hygienic shower, or water hose; 
7.7.2. The washroom floor shall be kept clean. 
7.7.3. The products provided, such as soaps, shall not offer risk to the Halal certification. 
 
 
8. 
HALAL FOOD 
 
8.1. 
General requirements 
8.1.1. All Halal food shall be free of any non-Halal element, described in table 3 and 
of any impurity (Najasah), see item 5.7. 
In the table of attachment 1 are listed some Halal critical points that shall be 
considered, for each product type.  
8.1.2. All Halal food shall not contain toxic substances and dangerous pollutants that 
could be considered as harmful to health. 
8.1.3. The procedures derived from Islamic rules shall be followed in all steps of the 
Halal food production, including receipt, preparation, packaging, labeling, 
transportation, distribution, storage, exhibition, and Halal food service. 
8.1.4. Meat and its derivatives shall be Halal, therefore, they shall be purchased with 
the Halal certificate specific for its batch. 
8.1.5. Any products derivative from pork are prohibited to be in the Halal food, such as 
bacon, ham, sausages, pork lard and etc. 
8.1.6. Alcoholic beverages are absolutely prohibited, and shall not be used in the 
production of any Halal food, such as the use of wine in meat preparation, risotto, 
etc. Besides that, chocolate truffles and other desserts may also have alcohol or 
liquors in its composition, therefore they need attention. 
8.1.7. Blood is also a prohibited element, that way, one shall be careful with dishes that 
use blood in its preparation, such as chicken in brown sauce, or chorizo.  
8.1.8. Gelatin, unless it has a Halal certificate, shall not be used because it may come 
from pork, or animals that were not Halal slaughtered. 
8.1.9. Everything that is not listed in table 3, is allowed for Muslims’ consumption, such 
as: 
a) All fruits; 


=== PAGINA 17/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND 
LEISURE) SERVICES CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.11 
Rev.: 05 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:17 de 39 
 
 
b) All vegetables,  
c) Eggs; 
d) Honey; 
e) All animals that live in the sea, such as fish, crabs, and shrimp, except for 
poisonous fishes. Amphibian animals, such as frogs, shall not be consumed; 
f) 
Any seed/legume, such as wheat, rice, bean, potato, cassava, peas, 
chickpeas, soy, are allowed, as well as their preparation (breads, cakes, 
soups, etc.); 
g) Milk and its derivatives are allowed (with great care, because many 
technological elements applied in these products may be illicit, like enzymes, 
yeasts, coagulants, whey);  
8.1.10. Industrialized products, such as cookies, candies, cheeses, yogurts, sausages, 
chocolates, soft drinks, processed juices may offer risk for Halal certification, 
therefore they should be purchased with Halal certification, and in its absence, 
shall go through a previous validation with the Halal certifier.

Table 3. Table of non-Halal products  
Origin 
Non-Halal 
Animals 
Animals not Halal slaughtered – according to Islamic rules; Animals slaughtered by 
polytheists, irreligious, seculars, atheists, Magis, apostates and other unbelievers, 
except for Christians and Jews; Naturally dead animals; Fatally beaten animals 
(Mawqouza); Dead animals as a result of falling (Motaradiah); Dead animals as a result 
of head butt (Nateehah); Animals eaten by animals of prey; Animals on whose slaughter 
the name of God (Allah) was not mentioned; Animals slaughtered by idolatry. 
Pigs; Domestic donkeys; Mules; Elephants; Monkeys; Dogs; Foxes; Cats and the like. 
Predators such as prey animals and the like, such as lions, cheetahs, bears.  
Birds of prey with sharp claws used for attacking and the like, such as eagles, falcons, 
hawks, sparrows, peregrines, kites, and owls. 
Rodents, reptiles, pests and the like, such as rats, centipedes, serpents, snakes, 
monitors, lizards, geckos, chameleons, hedgehogs, martins, bats, squirrels, polecats, 
moles and Coleoptera (except for dabb lizards and jerboas). 
Scorpions and all types of insects, worms and animals prohibited to be killed by Rules, 
such as ants, bees, woodpeckers, and hoopoes, except for locusts (the cochineal due 
to its similarity with locust) and unavoidable bee parts such as honey. 
Odious creatures and the like, such as shells, larva, and other similar animals. 
Animals fed with unclean items unless they are confined and fed with permissible 
feeding according to Islamic Rules for at least three days. 
Aquatics 
Toxicant animals or harmful species, unless toxicant or harmful substances are removed 
during preparation. 
Plants 
Plants and their products/ toxicant parts, harmful, poisonous, or narcotic derivatives, 
unless toxicant or harmful substances are removed during preparation. 
Beverages 
Alcoholic beverages (containing ethanol), intoxicating beverages or those containing 
alcohols, narcotic, poisonous, toxicant, or harmful substances. 
GMO: 
All elements produced by genetic modification from a prohibited specie or more than 
one prohibited species (non-Halal). 
Blood and 
blood 
derivatives 
from humans or 
animals 
Blood and all its derivatives are prohibited, as well as all body liquids from human or 
animal origin, such as vomiting and urine. 
Food aditives 
All toxicant, harmful additives, and its derivatives.  
Products and derivatives from non-Halal materials. 
Enzymes 
All enzymes derived from non-Halal sources. 
Microorganisms Microorganisms such as germs, fungi, toxicant, and harmful yeast produced on non-
Halal environments or using non-Halal ingredients. 
Source: GSO 2055-1 adapted.  
 


=== PAGINA 19/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND 
LEISURE) SERVICES CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.11 
Rev.: 05 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:19 de 39 
 
 
8.2. 
Requirements for the sections where Halal food and beverages are prepared 
and served  
8.2.1. The establishment shall only deal with Halal products that meet the Halal food 
provisions specified in this scheme, that is, it shall be free of non-Halal 
elements mentioned in item 8.1. 
8.2.2. The establishment shall meet the requirements of Good Manufacturing Practices, 
according to the current national legislation for food service establishments. 
8.2.3. The tools, equipments and utensils used during service handling and 
commercialization of Halal food products shall be completely segregated from 
those used for non-Halal food (for example, bovine Halal meat and bovine non-
Halal meat) 
8.2.4. All devices, tools, production lines and associated materials used for Halal food 
preparation shall be clean and free of impurities, dedicated for handling Halal 
products and shall not be made of or contain non-Halal material, such as bone, 
nail, or teeth (stainless steel is preferable). 
8.2.5. If a facility or service site normally handles with non-Halal products, but intends 
to switch for a Halal production, it shall go through a cleaning process, 
according to Islamic rules, before starting the Halal production. 
8.2.6. When transforming any devices, tools, or utensils, such as knives that have 
been used or in contact with non-Halal food, they shall be cleaned according 
to general cleaning rules to remove completely traces from non-Halal products. 
The alternate use between Halal and non-Halal products shall not be 
performed. 
8.2.7. When cleaning any devices, tools or utensils that are in contact with Halal food, 
there shall not be the use of any detergent liquids, oils or greases that contain 
non-Halal components or materials (prohibited by Shariah). 
8.2.8. All Halal food stored, displayed, or transported shall be segregated, 
categorized, and labeled as Halal to prevent them from being mixed and 
contaminated with non-Halal products. 
8.2.9. It is not allowed to serve/ commercialize alcoholic beverages at the 
establishment. 
 


=== PAGINA 20/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND

9. 
REQUIREMENTS EXCLUSIVE FOR HOTELS 
Hotels, inns, resorts, or any other place with accommodation for lodging, shall meet 
the requirements of this item 9. 
These rules also apply to services (spa, [well-being areas], markets, hairdressing 
etc.) provided by subcontractors. Therefore, the hotel management shall ensure that 
subcontractors meet the requirements of this scheme. 
 
9.1. 
Rooms 
The establishment shall ensure that the room is suitable for Muslims guests, which 
includes, but not limited, to the following requirements: 
9.1.1. The room shall be kept clean and well maintained; 
9.1.2. The rooms shall have a clearly signalized Quibla direction; 
9.1.3. The rooms shall have adequate space so that the Muslim can perform the prayer 
(salah); 
9.1.4. The washroom shall be equipped with bidet, hygienic shower, or water hose; 
9.1.5. The washroom floor shall be kept clean; 
9.1.6. Alcoholic and toxicants beverages shall not be stored in the room’s refrigerator; 
9.1.7. Art pieces (frames or sculpture) shall not have human or animal form in the room; 
9.1.8. Selection of TV channel provided in the rooms shall not contain adult channels; 
9.1.9. Sound insulation shall be provided in rooms at a sufficient level to provide privacy; 
9.1.10. The availability of prayer mats (5.21) for use in the room or upon request by the 
guest; 
9.1.11. A clean female prayer garment available upon request; 
9.1.12. A copy of Quran (5.12) shall be made available upon request by the guest; 
9.1.13. Information for the guests shall be available in the room, bulletin board or upon 
request by the guest on following:  
a) Prayer times; 
b) Location of Quibla Direction sign in the rooms; 
c) Location of mosques nearby; 
9.1.14. The organization is recommended to provide friendly services to disabled guest 
as well. 
9.1.15. There is no reference to images or objects of other religious practices, such as a 


=== PAGINA 21/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND 
LEISURE) SERVICES CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.11 
Rev.: 05 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:21 de 39 
 
 
cross on the wall, bible, etc. 
 
9.2. 
Toilets 
Toilets situated in common areas shall fulfil the following requirements: 
9.2.1. Be kept clean and well maintained; 
9.2.2. Separate toilets for men and women, with at least one toilet suitable for 
disabled persons; 
9.2.3. Toilets’ door shall not be hollow, or transparent; 
9.2.4. In case of the urinals, precautions shall be taken in order to protect the privacy 
of the users, 
9.2.5. Toilets, if possible, shall be located so that the user’s front or back is not aligned 
in the Qibla direction. 
9.2.6. Toilets shall be equipped with bidet, hygienic shower, or water hose. 
 
9.3. 
General 
9.3.1. It shall not be allowed non-Halal products (pork products, alcohol, etc) in any 
of the hotel facilities’ service departments, including sales department. Also, in 
case of clients/ guests or other person bring them from outside, they shall not 
be allowed in hotel’s common areas.  
It shall be ensured, by any means, that there is no risk for the served food to 
be contaminated by non-Halal products during the preparation, processing, 
transportation, and storage. 
9.3.2. People privacy shall be respected, for that, it shall not be taken photos of 
guests without their prior consent. 
9.3.3. Family filter shall be activated in the hotels giving internet access. 
9.3.4. No video or audio recording shall be done in the private areas (rooms, toilets, 
shower cabins, pools, massage saloons, changing rooms, etc.). 
9.3.5. In private areas for men and women, personnel of same gender shall be in 
service, entrance of personnel of opposite gender shall be prevented and 
gender signs shall be placed in the entrance of the private areas. 
9.3.6. It shall be ensured that there is only one key secret for each door, so that one 
key can only open one door. 


=== PAGINA 22/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND

Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:22 de 39 
 
 
9.3.7. If the hotel accepts pets, special areas and/or shelters shall be assigned for 
these pets and measures to prevent them wander freely in the facilities shall be 
taken. 
9.3.8. In cases of maintenance, cleaning, repair and modification in the private areas, 
necessary precautions shall be taken to prevent any possible guest-related 
discontent. 
9.3.9. Information signs in the outdoor and indoor places shall be prepared in Arabic 
and English, together with the language of the country in which the facilities 
reside.  
Note: In addition to these languages, facility administration may prepare signs 
in any other language if they intend to do so. 
9.3.10. Other facilities providing Halal tourism may operate as independent institutions 
or may be part of other institutions. In accordance with their status, they apply 
the provisions of this scheme that defines the requirements for the services 
that they provide. 
9.3.11. During the holy month of Ramadan, the hotel shall offer a differentiated service, 
considering the times and meals for suhoor (5.25) and iftar (5.18). The Halal 
certifier may be called to confirm the program/ schedules. 
 
9.4. 
Public prayer room 
There shall be a public prayer room for Muslims at the hotel grounds. Thus, the 
following requirements shall be practiced: 
9.4.1. Prayer room shall be suitable and properly signalized for its intended purpose; 
9.4.2. Well-lit, with adequate ventilation, kept clean and well maintained; 
9.4.3. Has a Qibla direction clearly defined; 
9.4.4. Has sufficient number of prayer mats (5.21); 
9.4.5. Has current information on prayer time or upon request; 
9.4.6. Provides clean female prayer garment or upon request; 
9.4.7. Has segregated wudhu/ ablution (5.26) facilities for men and women which are 
adjacent to the prayer room; 
9.4.8. Shall have segregated prayer areas for men and women; 
9.4.9. There shall be no music broadcasting at places close to prayer room and while 


=== PAGINA 23/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND 
LEISURE) SERVICES CERTIFICATION 
DT 7.11 
Rev.: 05 
Data: 06/02/2024 
Pág.:23 de 39 
 
 
the adhan is recited; 
9.4.10. If the Friday prayer is not carried out in the hotel prayer room, then sufficient 
means shall be provided for transportation to nearest mosques, if necessary; 
9.4.11. At least one copy of Quran shall be made available in the prayer room. 
 
9.5. 
Recreational facilities 
The requirements for recreational facilities shall be as follows: 
9.5.1. Recreational facilities shall be kept clean and well maintained; 
9.5.2. There shall be separated facilities or with scheduled time dedicated for women; 
9.5.3. Creams, oils, and any other cosmetic products shall not offer risk to Halal; 
9.5.4. The washroom at the facilities shall be equipped with bidet or hygienic shower; 
9.5.5. If there is more than one outdoor or indoor swimming pool, they shall be assigned 
separately as men and women swimming pools and shall be independent of each 
other; 
9.5.6. If there is more than one outdoor or indoor swimming pool, arrangements shall 
be made in order to maintain men/women utilization in turns, to guarantee privacy 
of men and women. Pool and sunbathing areas reserved for women shall have 
an enclosed perimeter and shall be arranged in such a way that these areas 
cannot be visible from outside (upper floors, terraces, adjacent building, etc.); 
9.5.7. For the pools separately assigned as male and female, measures to prevent 
inappropriate entrance of any person of the opposite gender shall be taken; 
9.5.8. Animators in the activities conducted in the private areas assigned to women 
shall be women, whereas animators in the activities conducted in the private 
areas assigned to men shall be men; 
9.5.9. Health services and sports activities (if any) shall be provided to men and women 
separately, in different times or in different halls; 
9.5.10. For the services of hairdressing, massage, wellness, and spa (if any), 
men/women compartments shall be separated, or these services shall be 
provided to each gender in different times and shall be delivered by the personnel 
of the same gender. Activities shall be provided by fair values, in accordance with 
the Islamic ethics; 
9.5.11. Cultural animations, music and entertainment activities, movie and projector 


=== PAGINA 24/39 ===
 
FAMBRAS HALAL CERTIFICAÇÃO  
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FAMBRAS HALAL FOR FOOD AND 
TOURISM (HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, TRANSPORTATION AND
